properties.blurb: At Fat Bottom Brewing, the beer is the star of the show! Their beers are crafted with a focus on a wide variety of styles from around the world, all proudly brewed right here in Nashville, Tennessee. From their rich and flavorful Bertha Nitro Stout to their fruity Sandy Blonde Ale, every sip is a journey around the globe. But that's not all - their avocado smash burger is a must-try! With perfectly grilled beef, creamy avocado, lettuce, tomato and onion, it's a flavor explosion in every bite. So whether you're a beer connoisseur or just looking for a delicious meal, Fat Bottom Brewing is the perfect destination. Come on down and join us for a night of great beer and even better food!
properties.name: Fat Bottom Brewing Co

properties.blurb: The menu here was stacked with delights like the pastrami burger, BBQ ribs, and a Monte Cristo, all capable of satiating even the biggest stomachs. Even on a cold day we warmed right up with a Cinnamon Toast Coffee with rumchata. Friendly confines with pool tables, jukebox, outdoor deck and of course the game of the moment up on the flatscreen, Fatboys doesn’t discriminate…even you skinny guys out there will be well taken care of.
properties.name: Fat Boys Bar & Grill

properties.blurb: Looking for a spot with a wide range of menu options and a great atmosphere? Look no further than Fat Charlies in Urbandale, Iowa! Whether you're in the mood for a juicy New York Strip or a classic plate of spaghetti and meatballs, they've got you covered. With TVs positioned throughout the restaurant, you won't miss a second of the action, and their comfortable cushioned stools will keep you comfortable throughout your visit. Come see for yourself why Fat Charlies is a local favorite!
properties.name: Fat Charlie's

properties.blurb: Grab Maverick, Goose, Iceman and Slider and head to Fat Daddy’s for their famed volleyball courts. Well, maybe not Goose…okay, too soon. Twenty three tap lines ensure you’ll have a bevy of options to quench your thirst. Maybe a smooth Outboard Cream Ale or a cloudy Spotted Cow Farmhouse, sure to take the sting outta getting jousted at the net by that 6’7’’ Latvian dude you swear has to be a pro.
properties.name: Fat Daddy's

properties.address: 8335 Crystal View Rd, Eden Prairie, MN 55344, USA
properties.blurb: At Fat Pants Brewing, they don’t just encourage you to wear your “fat pants”—they practically insist on it. And honestly, you'll regret it if you don’t. Between their extensive beer list, hearty food menu, and laid-back atmosphere, you’ll be wishing you’d taken their advice after one too many bites or sips. Sustainability, fun, and good vibes flow as freely as the drinks. So go ahead, settle in, and make yourself at home—just be prepared to wish you’d worn those fat pants after all!
properties.name: Fat Pants Brewing Co

properties.address: 3555 N Dixie Hwy, Oakland Park, FL 33334
properties.blurb: The vibe is always hoppy at Fat Tap. Their constant rotation of taps and assorted bottles and cans (just clap your hands) means you’ll always find something to love. Enjoy those brews while playing games on the patio, Or step inside and marvel at the local art. Hungry? Dig in to a great selection of flatbreads, sandwiches, and bites. See you there!
properties.name: Fat Tap Beer Bar

properties.address: 830 E Oakland Park Blvd STE 101, Oakland Park, FL 33334
properties.blurb: You don’t have to be a beer snob to appreciate the diversity of brews on tap here—although you can be a little snobby to your Bud-Light- drinking friends at home. Choose one of the local ales and browse the food menu. The Cuban Fatty sandwich is our must-have. Treat yourself for being the friend with impeccable taste. Well done, you. Well done, indeed.
properties.name: Fat Tap Beer Bar and Eatery

properties.blurb: It’s not fate that Fate Brewing has some of the best beer around and they don’t leave things like that to chance. Standouts like Fateful IPA, Buena Vista Blonde Ale, and Fatality have become fan favorites, while the award-winning Candy Bar Milk Stout makes its highly anticipated return each October. Grab a pint, soak in the community vibes, and celebrate life’s wins, big or small, because at Fate Brewing, good beer and good times are never left to chance.
properties.name: Fate Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Faubourg is NOLA’s oldest operating brewery. This O.G. has been at it since 1907! While we’re not exactly sure how they got around the whole prohibition era, we’re awfully glad they made it and continue cranking out classic beers to this day. Their Lager is fantastic, as is the IPA. Whatever you dig you’ll dig it at this destination brewery. And what goes better with beer than live music? Look for frequent events and make a night of it. Faubourg also has plans to roll out a museum dedicated to the history of brewing in The Big Easy. Cheers to that!
properties.name: Faubourg Brewing Co

properties.address: 7500 University Blvd Ste I, Clive, IA 50325
properties.blurb: Fazio’s University Tap isn’t just your average watering hole. It’s the Best Dive in Clive, and they wear that crown proudly. This place has everything: strong drinks, pool tables, live music, and karaoke nights that might just turn into your American Idol audition. The vibe is chill, the regulars are friendly, and the pizza? Let’s just say the sauce is made from scratch and we’ve definitely considered bribing someone for the recipe. Swing by for a drink, stay for the vibes, and don’t leave without a slice.
properties.name: Fazio's University Tap

properties.blurb: While good fences make good neighbors a FENCE STILE makes good neighbors friends, and that's always the goal here. Nestled in picturesque vineyards, they craft wines from grapes lovingly grown right on the premises. Indulge in a delightful array of treats from their Nibbles menu, including mouthwatering paninis, savory soups, artisanal bread, tempting cheese trays, delectable meat and cheese assortments, flavorful olives, nuts, and even delightful chocolates and rum balls. Whether you're savoring their exquisite wines or sipping on their handcrafted spirits, Fence Stile Vineyards, Wine & Distillery offers an inviting setting to unwind and foster lasting connections with both new and old friends.
properties.name: Fence Stile Vineyards, Wine & Distillery

properties.address: 212 Van Dorn St, Polk City, IA 50226
properties.blurb: It’s hard not to root for a brewery started in a garage by two friends who met in church. And that’s exactly the backstory behind Fenders Brewing, nestled in the growing town of Polk City. Their motto is “enjoy your ride,” whether that’s your IPA or the bike you took to get there. Bonus: it’s right next to Papa’s Pizzeria.
properties.name: Fenders Brewing

properties.address: 212 Van Dorn St, Polk City, IA 50226
properties.name: Fenders Brewing

properties.address: 212 Van Dorn St, Polk City, IA 50226
properties.name: Fenders Brewing

properties.address: 10-28 46th Ave, Long Island City, NY 11101, USA
properties.blurb: ** BOGO good on beer of equal or lesser value **
properties.name: Fifth Hammer Brewing Company

properties.blurb: Step into the out-of-this-world realm of Fighting Hand Brewing Co, where Sci-Fi and Fantasy brews blend with heartwarming family vibes. They've journeyed through galaxies of effort to bring this cosmic dream to life, and now, they're thrilled to extend a cosmic invitation to all! In this celestial sanctuary, you'll discover brews that transport your taste buds to distant planets of flavor. Every sip is an adventure in itself.
properties.name: Fighting Hand Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Treat yourself to a craft pint while perusing a bourbon list worthy of The Bluegrass State. If you don’t take your booze neat, get it in their fantastic Whiskey Sour. A few games of pinball or NBA Jam later and you’ll start to wonder why you don’t spend happy hour here every day. Maybe you should. Would that be weird?
properties.name: Fink's

properties.blurb: Finkel & Garf is a name that sounds like they produce the best European record players on the market, but instead, they happen to be a brewery lacking any such pretension. Family owned and operated, they’ve been holding court in Boulder since 2014 and have developed quite the fan base. In fact, much of their brews show up across Colorado bars. But to get right to the source, you’ll have to head to their awesome taproom. We lucked out with a couple of limited releases, the sweet and tart Cherry and Black Currant Wheat and the Bourbon Barrel Aged Imperial Stout.
properties.name: Finkel & Garf Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Perhaps it's been a minute since you've hoisted an Irish Amber and cheersesd the good deeds one can do just by drinking one? This classic, approachable ale is now old enough to drink (as legend has it, the recipe was purchased for a dollar from Kieran Folliard) and still slams like the day it was born. So grab the malty legend that launched this place before exploring the rest of an ever-evolving tap list. Finnegan's is a true Mpls OG and we always feel better about outselves after a session in this sweet taproom. Cheers!
properties.name: FINNEGANS Brew Co

properties.address: 2700 Lyndale Ave S, Minneapolis, MN 55408, USA
properties.blurb: Get fired up for a match made in culinary heaven at Fire & Nice Alehouse! They've perfected the art of pairing wood-fired pizza with a cold pint of the finest brews that Minnesota breweries have to offer. With a living, rotating beer list, you'll always find something new and exciting to sip on. Their menu is a masterpiece of fresh, seasonal ingredients that will make your taste buds dance with joy. Plus, they're always hosting awesome events and promotions that'll keep you coming back for more. So gather your crew, ignite your appetite, and experience the fiery deliciousness at Fire & Nice!
properties.name: Fire & Nice Alehouse

properties.blurb: Fire + Fury Brewing Company is a must-visit for beer enthusiasts in the area. With an impressive selection of brews ranging from sours to stouts and IPAs to pilsners, there's something for every taste. Their beautiful patio is the perfect spot to enjoy a cold one, and their expansive menu features crowd-pleasers like Nachos Tacos & Stuff, wings with 7 different sauces to try, and sandwiches. Come for the beer, stay for the food and atmosphere at Fire + Fury Brewing.
properties.name: Fire + Fury Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Fire On The Mountain loves serving beer and spirits from local Colorado breweries and distilleries. And you’ll love drinking them. Sample flavors like Denver Beer Co. Graham Cracker Porter, Sam Adams Just the Haze, and Drekker Benevolent Intentions. This restaurant is also committed to their community, recycling their used cooking oil, composting food scraps, and supporting local schools with food donations.
properties.name: Fire On The Mountain (HIghlands)

properties.blurb: A restaurant that pays a living wage and benefits to their staff, is committed to sustainability, and has a sauce of the month called BOOberry Ghostpepper? Yeah, we’re in. Not only is Fire On The Mountain committed to composting all food scraps, recycling used oil locally, and supporting local schools with food donations, they also just serve really fantastic food and beer.
properties.name: Fire On The Mountain (Wash Park)

properties.address: 518 N Water St. Milwaukee, WI to 53202
properties.blurb: There’s something about a long, wooden bar that makes us feel like we’re in an old west saloon. Except this is a saloon with 50+ beers, a fun crowd, free pool and a huge screen to watch the games on. Slide another Sarsaparilla our way, barkeep. We just realized there’s live music here tonight.
properties.name: Fire on Water

properties.address: 3500 Lemay Ferry Rd, St. Louis, MO 63125, USA
properties.blurb: Firehouse Bar and Grill is the perfect place to honor firefighters while satisfying your taste buds. Start with their Stuffed Mushrooms—Italian sausage and cream cheese-filled, breaded, and fried to crispy perfection, served with Cajun horseradish sauce. Feeling bold? The 5-Alarm Bard Burner Burger is so fiery, you might need to dial 911! Cool it down with a cold beer from their extensive tap selection. Every visit feels like a tribute to the brave firefighters, so eating here often? Yeah, that’s just you doing your duty!
properties.name: Firehouse Bar & Grill

properties.blurb: The name isn’t just a gimmick: Firetrucker Brewery was built in an old fire station. That means it has expansive space, with a covered patio, a bar area, and a “couch zone.” It offers both beers on tap and canned creations. It’s also a great place to stake out if you’re into the art scene: Firetruck Brewery works with Art for Ankeny, holding art shows and community events.
properties.name: Firetrucker Brewery

properties.blurb: A bar inside a bookstore. What a novel concept. First Draft Book Bar—located within the walls of Changing Hands Bookstore—has beer and wine on tap, plus sangria, mead, and a handful of bar bites. If you love reading and drinking, you’ll love reading while drinking even better at First Draft Book Bar. Cheers to you bookworms/boozehounds!
properties.name: First Draft Book Bar

properties.blurb: A bar inside a bookstore. What a novel concept. First Draft Book Bar—located within the walls of Changing Hands Bookstore—has beer and wine on tap, plus sangria, mead, and a handful of bar bites. If you love reading and drinking, you’ll love reading while drinking even better at First Draft Book Bar. Cheers to you bookworms/boozehounds!
properties.name: First Draft Book Bar

properties.blurb: Here a harmony of farm-to-table delights and handcrafted cider awaits you. Nestled in Fairfield, Iowa, their hard cider journey has been one of dedication and passion. Sip on the crisp, award-winning ciders, lovingly pressed, fermented, aged, and packaged right on-site. Each sip carries the essence of their commitment to quality and flavor. But that's not all! Prepare your taste buds for a mouthwatering treat—the Best Burger in Iowa! We won't argue with that title, because it truly is a masterpiece of taste.
properties.name: Fishback & Stephenson Cider House

properties.blurb: "Fisher’s Bar lives where Crooked Creek Saloon used to be, but the similarities end there. The former dive bar has been completely remodeled and reinvented, and now serves up made-from-scratch food, an expansive draft list, and delicious cocktails like the Caramel White Russian. They also have live music to make the brew go down extra sweet. "
properties.name: Fisher's Bar

properties.blurb: As one of the key players in the vanguard of new businesses that have transformed Murray Hill into the hippest neighborhood in town, this trendy (in the best way) joint is a must-stop on any respectable Jacksonville beer tour. Sip on a Bait and Switch Coffee Blonde and camp out on the front porch while watching eclectic crowds of passersby browse the funky shops of Edgewood Avenue. Swing by on a Sunday morning for outdoor yoga on the beautiful “backyard” and treat yourself to an après Vinyasa Lazy River Pilsner. Order in some Taco Bodega to pair with your King of the Hill Hazy IPA and enjoy the immaculate interior design of the laid-back taproom. Navy vet turned brew guru Broc Flores prides himself on his barrel-aged sours (for good reason). Make sure to grab a few cans to go.
properties.name: Fishweir Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Whether you’re a trivia person, a shuffleboard person, a food truck person, or simply a happy hour person, Five Cities Brewing has your after-work needs covered. Pop in for the extensive wine list or unique beer options like Bummer City Bomb Pop Sour and Schwifty Hippie Hazy IPA. Stay for the regular happy hour deals and rotating food trucks.
properties.name: Five Cities Brewing

properties.blurb: It’s not surprising to find community at a brewery but there’s something extra special to be found here. Although they host a plethora of events it is the monthly potluck that really pulls on our heart muscles - it’s just so sweet and lovely. Anywho, being a microbrewery allows them to truly focus on the intricacies of their brews and you’ll be hard pressed to find one that isn’t excellent.
properties.name: Five.0.Five Brewing Co

properties.address: 1315 SW Oralabor Rd, Ankeny, IA 50023, USA
properties.name: Flame Cantina

properties.address: N US Hwy 69 Suite 931, Huxley, IA 50124
properties.blurb: Flight Bar + Grille is where to go for both great food and drinks. The Foundation Burger is not to be missed - double smash patties, cheddar cheese, bacon, caramelized onions, and a house-made mustard aioli make this burger an instant classic. Wash it down with one of the local beers on tap, carefully selected to represent the best of the Des Moines beer scene. Come for the burger, stay for the beer - and the friendly atmosphere that keeps us coming back for more.
properties.name: Flight Bar + Grille

properties.blurb: When are we going? What time should we be there? Now? Okay great, we’ll see you there because there’s no way we’re missing out on one of their burgers. Ever. A bison burger covered in perfectly melted boursin cheese? Hard yes. Ohhh but also, the One Red Door because crispy shallots and brie cheese will always suit our fancy. Of course then there’s the BBQ beef fries or the Brisket Mac…You know what, we’ll just come pick you up now. Be there in twenty.
properties.name: Flipside Burger

properties.address: 85 E Towne Way, Madison, WI 53704, USA
properties.blurb: Lights, camera, beer! Get ready for the ultimate cinematic experience at Flix Brewhouse, where movies and brews come together in perfect harmony. As America's first run cinema Brewery, they've mastered the art of pairing blockbuster films with their own handcrafted beers. With a lineup of 12 brews to choose from, plus an extensive selection of draft beers, wine, and non-alcoholic beverages, there's something to satisfy every palate. And let's not forget about the delicious hand-made food that complements your movie-watching adventure. Flix Brewhouse will take you on a thrilling journey of flavors and entertainment!
properties.name: Flix Brewhouse

properties.address: 3800 Merle Hay Rd #1300, Des Moines, IA 50310
properties.blurb: The next time you and a buddy are arguing about whether to see a movie or hit up a brewery, remember us: we’re about to save your evening (and potentially your friendship. We don’t know how heated it got). Flix Brewhouse, true to its name, offers both beer and flicks, so you can take in the latest blockbuster and draft beers simultaneously.
properties.name: Flix Brewhouse

properties.blurb: Handcrafted cocktails, 16 beers on tap and a penchant for experimental styles of beer that are perfectly-paired with brewpub food offerings? Count us in! Florida Avenue Brewing Co. is exactly the sort of place we dream of taking our friends. So what are you waiting for, intrepid PubPasser? Get in there and check it out!
properties.name: Florida Avenue Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Two words: Destination brewery. With 16 beers on draft, handcrafted cocktails, a full-service restaurant and walk-in beer cave? You’re gonna want to get to Florida Avenue Brewing Co. right quick. Wait, there’s also a covered-patio with a spacious beer garden and state-of-the-art production facility you can tour? Yes please. We’ll see you there!
properties.name: Florida Avenue Brewing Co (Wesley Chapel)

properties.blurb: If you're on the hunt for a brewery that could be the backdrop to a Lana del Rey melody, your search ends here. Flower Crown Kombucha is like a remix of your kombucha expectations, delivering refreshing takes on this crisp beverage. When you step into their world, don't miss out on the delights of Pineappley Ever After, Mexican Hippie, Key Lime Mojito, and Pink Passion Fruit kombuchas. It's not just a brewery; it's a symphony of flavors waiting to be savored.
properties.name: Flower Crown Kombucha

properties.blurb: Not sure we’ve ever hyped a BYOB (Bring Your Own Basset Hound) joint before, but there’s a first time for everything. This locally owned brewery and taproom offers up a variety of brews and we know it can be hard for you, our adventurous PubPassers, to pick just one. And yes, everything in the lineup at Flying Basset looks like it’ll quench your thirst. Go with the beer flight (make sure you include the Flat Top Mulley and Lazy Hazy). Still can’t pick a fave? Grab a four-pack of something different to go.
properties.name: Flying Basset Brewing

properties.blurb: Flying Boat’s tasting room is awash in fascinating history and great beer. Learn about the chapter in St. Pete’s past that gave the brewery its monicker as you work your way around their tap list. The Gose was perfect for a hot afternoon and the Porter was dark and malty yet very sessionable — a common theme here. Hop aboard!
properties.name: Flying Boat Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Flying Boat’s tasting room is awash in fascinating history and great beer. Learn about the chapter in St. Pete’s past that gave the brewery its monicker as you work your way around their tap list. The Gose was perfect for a hot afternoon and the Porter was dark and malty yet very sessionable — a common theme here. Hop aboard!
properties.name: Flying Boat Brewing Company

properties.blurb: Flying Fish Taphouse is North Jacksonville’s new go-to spot, serving up beach vibes, locally brewed beers, and seafood that’s as fresh as it gets. With a menu that’ll make your taste buds dance, this microbrewery brings a splash of the beach to the northside. Whether you’re here for a cold one or a seafood feast, the good food, great brews, and chill vibes make it a must-visit.
properties.name: Flying Fish Taphouse

properties.blurb: Expect the unexpected at Flying Horse Taproom. Like pizza cut into squares. Wines from around the world. And flying horses. Okay, maybe not that last bit. This spot offers over 30 local Kansas City craft brews and spirits, and expands its horizons with artisan flatbreads topped with flavors like Thai Chicken Basil and Cuban Mojo.
properties.name: Flying Horse Taproom

properties.blurb: Welcome to FlyteCo Brewery, a place where you can drink inside the fuselage of an airplane. You've probably never thought about drinking in a fuselage, but now you are. And that's a good thing. With 10-15 different beers on tap, including gluten-free options, FltyeCo has a beer for every palate and every preference. Trust us—we've tried them all. Not in one sitting mind you.
properties.name: FlyteCo Brewing

properties.blurb: This vibrant downtown Des Moines eatery has become a bona fide institution, dishing up hella creative fusion delights like its Food Network-famous Crab Rangoon Pizza (topped with crispy wontons and sweet chili sauce), and General Tso’s Chicken Pizza (featuring the titular rich, sweet and spicy sauce with breaded chicken, roasted red pepper and green onion). More regional pie offerings include the Iowan, with BBQ sauce, pulled pork and Canadian bacon. Don’t skip the Chinese Cheesesticks—deep fried mozzarella encased in crispy egg roll wrappers. Wash it all down with your choice of many local craft beer taps, or select from nearly a score of specialty tiki drinks, served in delightfully appropriate Polynesian receptacles.
properties.name: Fong's (Downtown)

properties.blurb: As its name suggests, Fong’s Pizza does really good pizza. It is also are home to the original Crab Rangoon Pizza, which is every bit as delightful a combo as it sounds. Fong’s also has a rotating beer list (we’re including them on PubPass for a reason!) and we can’t let you go in good conscience without mentioning the Kamikaze flights.
properties.name: Fong's Pizza

properties.blurb: History runs deep at Forgotten Star Brewing Co, housed in a WWII military production facility recognized for “Excellence in Production.” Today, that same dedication fuels award-winning beers like Northstar Cream Ale and Big Stick IPA. With a resident food truck keeping the bites flowing, it’s a place where history, craft, and great flavors come together.
properties.name: Forgotten Star Brewing Co

properties.address: 6616 Rose Farm Rd, Ocean Springs, MS 39564
properties.blurb: The only pretentiousness you’ll find at Fort Bayou Brewing Co. is the idyllic gazebo overlooking a koi pond. The Coast’s newest locally owned microbrewery and brewpub focuses on brewing simple, quality beer by expanding on brewing trends learned from other regions. And the Cypress Taphouse serves memorable Cajun-inspired dishes alongside pouring some of the best brews in town. Stop by for happy hour, live music, or a quiet moment of beer and contemplation. At the koi pond, natch.
properties.name: Fort Bayou Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Get ready for the tastiest deal in town, folks! Founders Irish Pub knows how to make your Thursdays sizzle with their unbeatable $8 Classic Cheeseburger and Bucket deal. Sink your teeth into a juicy burger masterpiece while sipping on a bucket filled with your choice of refreshing domestic beer or seltzer. But the goodness doesn't stop there! From Monday to Friday, happy hour kicks off from 3-6, serving up smiles and discounted drinks. And when the sun is shining, head out to their sprawling open patio, where you can soak up the summer vibes and connect with the Chicchaqua Valley Trail. Founders Irish Pub is where good times and great flavors come together.
properties.name: Founders Irish Pub

properties.address: 111 S 11th St STE 100, West Des Moines, IA 50265
properties.blurb: Foundry Distilling’s goal is to be the most innovative distillery in the world, and they’re well on their way. Located in the historic Foundry Building, originally built to repair rail cars, this private-barrel Iowa whiskey distillery is a truly one-of-a-kind drinking (and distilling) experience. Stop in for a perfect glass of whiskey — or to indulge in a customizable private barrel all your own.
properties.name: Foundry Distilling Co

properties.blurb: Jay and Melissa, the owners of Fountain City Winery, met at a winery – so it makes perfect sense that now they should run one together. Every wine on the menu is described with 3 thoughtfully-chosen adjectives. Trumpeter is smooth, harmonic, and inviting. Novel Sojourn is rich, rhapsodic, and worldly. But our three words apply to every sip on the list: Really excellent wine.
properties.name: Fountain City Winery

properties.blurb: Embark on a mead-drinking adventure like no other at Four Brothers Mead, where the spirits of Norse Vikings, Saxons, and Celts converge in a heavenly elixir. These brewing warriors, all veterans who have served our country, infuse their meads with a touch of divine inspiration. With each sip, you'll taste the valor and passion that flows through their veins, creating meads fit for the gods. Join the ranks of the chosen few and experience the glorious libations crafted by these fearless brothers.
properties.name: Four Brothers Mead

properties.address: 103 S 11th St, West Des Moines, IA 50265
properties.blurb: ‘Make great craft beer and have fun while doing it.’ As a founding principle goes, you can’t do much better… and if the beer at Fox is any indicator? They’re nailing it. A couple flagships got us rolling (we loved the Hefe!) and then we explored the space a bit with a seasonal Churro Baltic Porter we won’t soon forget. At Fox we drank great craft beer and had fun while doing it. Can that just be our principle for life?
properties.name: Fox Brewing

properties.blurb: Appleton’s oldest saloon has a rotating selection of the finest craft beer. Even better, said beer has quirky names like Tailgate the Zoo, Flamingo Dreams Nitro, and Will You Berry Me? (Careful who you offer that last one to). Fox River House also has live music on weekends, an expansive patio, and serves gooey, hot pizzas from Home Run Pizza.
properties.name: Fox River House

properties.blurb: Foxtown Brewing is where craft beer, history, and good times collide. Set inside a beautifully restored 1800s brewery in Mequon, Wisconsin, it pours a rotating lineup of house-brewed beers alongside elevated comfort food in a lively, laid-back atmosphere. Whether you’re grabbing a pint on the beer deck, catching live music next door, or exploring the historic lager caves beneath the building, Foxtown delivers a brewery experience that’s equal parts heritage and hangout
properties.name: Foxtown Brewing

properties.address: 1015 SE Stark St, Portland, OR 97214, USA
properties.name: Fracture Brewing Taproom

properties.address: 431 SW Harvey Milk St, Portland, OR 97204, USA
properties.name: Fracture Midtown Taproom

properties.address: 5372 St Charles St, Cottleville, MO 63304, USA
properties.blurb: Who would've thought we'd ever become the "Green Thumb" types? Our attempts at gardening have been, well, let's say less than stellar. But here's the twist – Frankie Martin's Garden is our kind of garden, and it's unlike anything you've ever experienced. This isn't just a dining spot; it's a culinary journey like no other. Picture this: award-winning food trucks serving up mouthwatering delights, a collection of carefully curated wines that'll leave you feeling like a sommelier, a whiskey library boasting over 200 golden gems, and a showcase of the finest breweries across the great state of Missouri.
properties.name: Frankie Martin's Garden

properties.blurb: The tagline on their website says: Drink Beer. Pet Dogs. Soooo, it’s pretty clear that these are excellent people with brilliant minds and are to be trusted. With rotating seasonal taps there’s always something new to try and they offer in house brewed kombucha and root beer! Grab a pint of something good, put in an order for some tasty tacos from Shreddy’s, find a dog to pet, and bask in the kitschy, cute vibe.
properties.name: Fraser River Beer Co

properties.address: 1730 E Warner Rd Suite 11, Tempe, AZ 85284
properties.blurb: Freely is the kind of place that has you coming back again... and again (and maybe even a few more agains). With 30 taps pouring the best local Arizona brews alongside standout favorites from across the country, you’ve got endless reasons to keep trying something new. Pair those pours with anything from their full menu and you've just upgraded your night out. Oh, and did we mention? Boozie Brunch. That’s right—two more words to seal the deal on your next weekend plans!
properties.name: Freely Taproom & Kitchen

properties.address: 110 Hilltop Village Center Dr, Eureka, MO 63025, USA
properties.blurb: Their name says it all – it's about setting your own rules and dancing to your own beer-loving beat. So, why not join in on the fun and celebrate the art of brewing without boundaries? Trust us, the beers here are nothing short of delicious. It's a flavor adventure waiting to be explored. So, come thirsty, come curious, and come ready to savor brews that defy convention. Cheers to the endless possibilities at FreeStyle Brew Co!
properties.name: FreeStyle Brew Co

properties.address: 225 Louisiana St Suite 107, Buffalo, NY 14204, USA
properties.name: Frequentem Brewing Co. Buffalo

properties.blurb: A terrific chef-driven menu with weekly specials like green chile cheese fries and a hearty beef stew, Freshcraft is exactly what the name implies. And they do as considerate a job curating their draft list too. About 25 taps available like the Neural Glipe IPA from Cerebral and the hilariously named Shirtless Putin Catchin’ Rays Lager from Call to Arms. If that’s not enough, 100+ bottles and cans should seal the deal.
properties.name: Freshcraft

properties.address: 400 Crossroads Business Park Ct, Wentzville, MO 63385, USA
properties.blurb: “Friends making beer, beer making friends” is a motto we can get behind! True to their name, Friendship Brewing Co is a spot where you’ll instantly feel at home. Always brewing up something new, their highly drinkable beers pair perfectly with the welcoming taproom and cozy patio making it easy to relax, share a laugh, and discover your next favorite pour with friends, old or new.
properties.name: Friendship Brewing Co (Flint Hill)

properties.blurb: It’s totally fine to have more than one best friend. Especially when they both pour beers worth talking about. The Flint Hill location carries the same spirit as the original, with a laid-back vibe, plenty of fresh brews, and an atmosphere that makes it easy to stick around for another round. When the beer’s this good, your circle of friends can only get bigger.
properties.name: Friendship Brewing Co (Pitman)

properties.blurb: Have you ever found yourself longing for the circus? No? Well, that doesn’t mean Fringe isn’t for you. The cozy, yet eclectic, taproom features circus-chic décor almost as memorable as their beer. You may be tempted to start with the Bearded Lady or Monkey Business, but may we suggest the rich-bodied Madam X Milk Stout? We don’t need a fortune teller to know you’ll love it.
properties.name: Fringe Beerworks

properties.address: 1611 W Whispering Wind Dr #7, Phoenix, AZ 85085
properties.blurb: We know we’re always telling you “You gotta try this place! You’ll thank us later!” Well, we’re here to tell you that you gotta try this place! Front Pourch has the friendliest beertenders. They have the coolest, most laidback space. They invite food trucks with menus that do not disappoint. We won’t talk you out of the Toasty Blonde Ale, which packs a real punch, but we will talk you into the Toasty Junior if you want something crushable. Just try it. You’ll thank us later.
properties.name: Front Pourch Brewing

properties.address: 400 W South Boulder Rd #1650, Lafayette, CO 80026
properties.blurb: Front Rage has pulled in some hardware at GABF and, as we wound our way through the High Grove Kviek Farmhouse to the Blackhawk Black IPA  it became evident as to why. Family owned, you’ll likely meet one of ‘em on your visit, and they’re happy to devise the perfect tasting flight to your likes. A smartly-styled brick building with ample outdoor (and dog-friendly) seating, Front Range is Layfette’s oldest such brewery, but their daring and creative output feels like they’re on the leading edge of what’s possible with beer.
properties.name: Front Range Brewing Co

properties.blurb: This Quad Cities institution does everything right, from a solid lineup of flagship beers to some downright tasty pub fare on the menu. Let’s be real, can you go wrong with poutine? Just watch yourself - ordering a second pound of Peach Whiskey wings might seem like a good idea in the moment, but you could come to regret that decision. We recommend ordering a Cherry Bomb Blonde and taking a minute before diving back into those wings.
properties.name: Front Street Brewing

properties.blurb: Coffee and Cocktails? This is the perfect place to find your equilibrium-balancing elixirs and we dare you to say that three times fast after indulging in their delightful Jarritos Paloma. But wait, there's more! Fuel Cafe offers all-day breakfast that will surely rev your engines. From classic favorites like hotcakes and eggs to the irresistible Bread Pudding French Toast, their menu is a testament to culinary wizardry. And let's not forget about the Beer-Mosa, the perfect way to celebrate the magic happening in their kitchen. Whether you're looking to fuel up or wind down, Fuel Cafe has got you covered.
properties.name: Fuel Cafe 5th St

properties.address: 40 Arena Way Suite 2, Council Bluffs, IA 51501
properties.blurb: Full Fledged Brewing is Council Bluff’s first craft brewery and has really taken off since it opened in 2019. Housed in an open, industrial space, they have plenty of beers to choose from—we recommend Floyd the Barbarian or Chili Buzz if you’re feeling spicy. Hungry? BYO food or check the calendar to see if a food truck will on site.
properties.name: Full Fledged Brewing Co

properties.blurb: A full range of beers awaits you at Full Mile. Start things off with a Cromulent Cream Ale and peruse a lineup that includes Belgian Farmhouse style beers right alongside more malt-forward brews. Then there’s the scratch kitchen cranking out wood-fired pizzas with locally-sourced ingredients. Not feeling pizza tonight? We won’t try to understand you. We’ll just direct you toward the Falafel Sandwich, Plain Ass Cheeseburger (real name), or Pulled Pork Nachos.
properties.name: Full Mile Beer Co

properties.address: 132 Market St STE 100, Sun Prairie, WI 53590
properties.blurb: A full range of beers awaits you at Full Mile. Start things off with a Cromulent Cream Ale and peruse a lineup that includes Belgian Farmhouse style beers right alongside more malt-forward brews. Then there’s the scratch kitchen cranking out wood-fired pizzas with locally-sourced ingredients. Not feeling pizza tonight? We won’t try to understand you. We’ll just direct you toward the Blue Gill Sandwich, Plain Ass Cheeseburger (real name), or Pulled Pork Nachos.
properties.name: Full Mile Beer Co & Kitchen

properties.blurb: There’s a lot to love at Fulton — from classics like Sweet Child of Vine and Lonely Blonde to their tasty take on hard seltzer (the blood orange is our fave). Then there’s the taproom itself... perfectly located for pre or postgame brews on the patio with your crew. The place also has a lineup of Fulton beers you can’t get anywhere else. Need a breather from booze? We love their tasty take on Hop Water.
properties.name: Fulton

properties.blurb: This place is so chill. We recommend starting with a Vibin’ or Hop Gun before expanding your horizons with one of their more adventurous beers. And man, they’re adventurous. In the past, we’ve seen beers made with oysters, sweet potatoes, s’mores, bacon and PB&J. Not all together, but you get the idea. Take a tour, grab a seat and settle in with some meditative munchies. Funky Buddha is a temple of taste where all can get religious about great beer. Ahh, yes, we feel more zen already.
properties.name: Funky Buddha Brewery

properties.blurb: Darts, pool tables, plenty of patio seating, a jukebox might get you in the door, but the friendly staff will keep ya there. And a menu featuring wings, sandwiches, salads and Mexican grub makes sure there’s something for everyone. FuNuGyz has a weird name but everything else delivers the classic sports bar experience.
properties.name: FuNuGyz

properties.address: 833 Howard Ave, New Orleans, LA 70113, USA
properties.name: G's Kitchen and Bar

properties.blurb: The stunning modern prairie style architecture and surrounding pastoral views will leave your mouth agape — all the easier to pour the fine offerings of Wisconsin’s southernmost brewery down your parched gullet. Choose from a slew of quality offerings, including the Anonymous Caller — a heckuva hazy that took home top honors at the 2022 Wisconsin IPA Fest. Elevated food offerings include the signature G5 Sandwich (a warm pretzel bun stuffed with ham, gruyere and cranberry aioli), or just stick to the essential Sconnie brew accompaniment — cheese curds. Why mess with perfection?
properties.name: G5 Brewing Co

properties.blurb: The Gallery Pub is more than just your typical neighborhood pub - it's a haven for art lovers and pizza enthusiasts alike! With a rotating selection of local St. Louis artists' works adorning the walls, the Gallery Pub is like a mini art gallery where you can enjoy a delicious slice of pizza with your beer. And with new shows every 2-3 months, there's always something new to see. But that's not all - their mouth watering pizzas will keep you coming back.! From classic pepperoni to unique creations like theirBanksy with ricotta, chicken, bacon, mushrooms, and a pesto drizzle, every bite is a flavor explosion. So whether you're in the mood for some culture or just a tasty slice of pie, the Gallery Pub has got you covered. Come on down and join us for a night of art, pizza, and good company!
properties.name: Gallery Pub on Thurman

properties.address: 141 4th St E Suite LL2, St Paul, MN 55101, USA
properties.blurb: Webster’s Dictionary may define “Gambit” as a calculated risk, but stepping into Gambit Brewing is a no-brainer. With a rotating selection of beer brewed on-site and hard seltzer cocktails crafted just for you, the payoff is instant. You’ll quickly realize that the only risk you’re taking here is running out of room to try everything. Whether you’re a craft beer aficionado or just looking for something new, Gambit’s got the menu to make your visit more than worth it.
properties.name: Gambit Brewing Co

properties.address: 4721 S Orange Ave, Orlando, FL 32806, USA
properties.blurb: As a brewery and food hall, these guys have mastered the art of combining high-quality food and drink with a dash of lighthearted fun. Their mission? To create a family and pet-friendly oasis where you can indulge in all things local. Step into their outdoor beer garden and game area, where you can sip on refreshing beers, sip on smooth bourbon, or savor a glass of wine. And when it comes to grub, their grill and wood-fired oven serve up delectable treats like mouthwatering pizza pies and so much more. So gather your crew, bring your furry friends, and get ready for a fantastic time at Gatlin Hall Brewing!
properties.name: Gatlin Hall Brewing

properties.address: 404 SW Forest Ave, Redmond, OR 97756, USA
properties.name: General Duffy's Waterhole

properties.blurb: The folks at Genesee have spent generations perfecting the art of brewing and it shows. This Rochester fave puts together a lineup of brews that can satisfy even the pickiest of beer drinkers (we all have that one friend, and if you think you don’t, then you might be that friend). Grab a pint or get creative with a flight - just make sure to include the flagship lager and a cream ale. Go for the beer, stay for the rooftop view of the High Falls.
properties.name: Genesee Brew House

properties.blurb: This place knows how to kick off the weekend right – catch all the Premier Football action every Saturday and Sunday morning. And if you're an Orlando City Lions fan, they've got you covered too! Their "Red Bar" is the ultimate relaxation spot where you can unwind with a glass of wine or a cocktail. If you're up for some livelier vibes, head over to the "Blue Bar" for a pint, live music, and some friendly darts competition. And speaking of darts, you won't miss a moment of the PDC Darts action – they've got it all on the telly! Don't forget the outdoor seating, where you can enjoy your drink of choice while soaking in the Florida sun.
properties.name: George & Dragon English Tavern

properties.address: 13899 River Rd Ste B, Luling, LA 70070, USA
properties.blurb: German Coast Beer Co is all about keeping things fresh, literally. With a solid lineup of staples like an IPA, American lager, and English stout, plus a rotating cast of seasonal brews, there’s always something new to try. They’re on a mission to make every season feel like a reason to raise a glass. Classic pints, bold seasonal experiments, and plenty of hops. This is the kind of place that keeps your beer calendar full and your taste buds happy.
properties.name: German Coast Beer Co

properties.blurb: Is this a bottle shop or a mini beer hall? Trick question. It’s both. And it’s the best. Grab something from their tap list and then wander over to peruse the coolers. There are so many ridiculously cool beers here. Drink one here. Take more home. Rinse. Repeat. You’ll be back often. Oh —and they’ve got you covered with NA beverages, spirits, ciders, wines, and even mead. Niiiiiiiiiice.
properties.name: Gezellig

properties.address: 103 S Public Rd, Lafayette, CO 80026, USA
properties.blurb: ** BOGO 10oz Stem Ciders or 16oz Howdy Beer**
properties.name: Ghost Box Pizza by Stem Ciders

properties.address: 931 E. Main St. Suite 9 Madison, WI 53703
properties.blurb: Big beers for everyone! That’s the mantra at Giant Jones and we’re fully on board. We’re also fully on board with the fact that this powerhouse is independent, certified organic and woman-owned. Most everything being brewed here tips the scales north of 7% and brings big flavor to match. Their DIPA is complex yet drinkable — truly one of the best we’ve had anywhere — and seasonals like their Pale Weizenbock and Grand Stout will steal your heart as well.
properties.name: Giant Jones Brewing Company

properties.blurb: Did we make a wrong turn on our way to New Prague and end up in Bavaria? From the looks of it, ja. Ja, we did. With a spacious taproom and a German-inspired biergarten, Giesenbrau Bier Co. is a little taste of Deutschland right here in Minnesota. Their Frickin Scruggly DIPA is strong and delicious—it’ll make you want to break out your finest pair of hosen and hoist your steins high. And, while you’re at it, have a second one. We both know that German goiter isn’t going to grow itself. Prost!
properties.name: Giesenbrau Bier Co

properties.address: 3500 S College Ave #194, Fort Collins, CO 80525
properties.blurb: What happens when a family goes on a pub crawl across Europe? They come home and start a brewery. (Yeah, we want to be part of that family too.) After extensive…research abroad, the crew at Gilded Goat found the perfect blend of pub tradition and local flavor. One visit to their taproom and a pint of Guten Bock might just make Gilded Goat “greatest of all time” in your book too.
properties.name: Gilded Goat Brewing Co

properties.address: 132 W Mountain Ave, Fort Collins, CO 80524, USA
properties.blurb: Gilded Goat is a study in blending. Art and science and fresh ingredients. European flavor and Rocky mountain beauty. Brewery and taproom. Food trucks and Bingo. The family behind the brewing company was inspired by European brewing traditions, applying microbiology to their own technique to produce consistent, high-quality craft beer. And every blend is 100% worth drinking.
properties.name: Gilded Goat Brewing Company - Downtown

properties.blurb: Located in Ascension Parish, this warm, fuzzy brewery specializes in sours, NE IPA’s, and pastry stouts. They serve up nothing but enthusiasm. It’s where you go when you want to take your taste buds out for a night on the town. We recommend the beer, and if you’re interested in today’s special, it’s also the beer.
properties.name: Gilla Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Gilly’s has over twenty rotating taps from local and regional breweries and you can even class it up with a glass of wine if you want. And believe us, you want. This hidden gem in Rockville makes sandwiches so good they rival Ross Gellar’s Moistmaker. Where’s the genius who invented Timmy G’s Ham Cruncher? We wanna shake their hand and buy ‘em a beer.
properties.name: Gilly's Craft Beer & Wine

properties.blurb: A relaxed hangout with lots of TVs serving what they call Stylish American Comfort Food, Gilroy’s is so reliable as a go-to when we’re in Des Moines, we have it saved as a preset on our Waze. Brunch, lunch, dinner, they have it all and it’s all good. Family friendly as well, as we noticed kids chowing down on massive burgers. A respectable beer selection and a perfect patio showcasing live music, Gilroy’s aces the basics.
properties.name: Gilroy's

properties.address: 2308 W Wisconsin Ave, Milwaukee, WI 53233, USA
properties.name: Gin Rickey

properties.address: 9500 University Ave #1118, West Des Moines, IA 50266
properties.blurb: Ever wanted to traverse the planet one pint at a time? Now you can. Global Brew is tapped in (get it?) to what’s hip in beer whether it’s across town, across the state or across the pond. So there’s lots of favors of beer. You’re set there. But wait… there’s also lots of ways to enjoy tots. You heard us. Get an order for the table and get ready to dunk those bad boys in more sauces than you can shake a beer list at. Just don’t shake Global Brew’s beer list too recklessly. It’s huge and you might knock something over…
properties.name: Global Brew

properties.blurb: Gluek's was the very first bar to sign up for the very first PubPass. Five years later, this downtown gem is as fun as ever. Come before or after a game. Enjoy live music multiple nights a week. Or just belly up to nosh on a pretzel and swill an ice cold Gluek Original or three.
properties.name: Gluek's

properties.blurb: How does one assume the name Gnarly? You start with a brewer that uses techniques and recipes from around the world. Then you add a 140” screen for games. Top it off with live music and events, and then, and only then, can you claim that monicker. Start off with a Catahoula Common — the crisp, crushable Lager named for the state dog of Louisiana (did you know we had a state dog?). Then, whether or not you’re a fan of dark beer? We recommend the Korova Milk Porter. It’s so smooth.
properties.name: Gnarly Barley

properties.address: 3052 El Cajon Blvd Suite 101, San Diego, CA 92104, USA
properties.name: GOAL. Brewing

properties.blurb: Though he cut his teeth working for some of the bigger bourbon distilleries in Kentucky, Gold Dirt Distillery founder Judd Kaufman focuses mainly on gin these days. A good choice, as the botanicals native to the Rocky Mountain wilderness make for one hell of an herbaceous spirit. The Lavender Gin is our fave, and the number one seller. Visit the rustic Rollinsville tasting room (way up at 8,670 feet) and try it in a Levitation cocktail with lemon juice, hibiscus syrup and cherry bitters—perfection. Chose from roughly 20 other knockout drink concoctions made with various craft gins, vodka, rum, absinthe—even a honey spirit which features mead distilled like whisky. Catch live music on the weekends, or simply sit outside with a refreshing beverage and contemplate the majestic snow-capped mountain panoramas.
properties.name: Gold Dirt Distillery

properties.address: 3000 Dr M.L.K. Jr St N, St. Petersburg, FL 33704, USA
properties.blurb: Good times and great coldies await at Golden Isles Brewing Co. With laid-back Florida vibes and a cozy, welcoming taproom, you'll feel the warmth of golden sunshine no matter the weather. Whether you're soaking up the breeze on the spacious patio or kicking back indoors, you can't go wrong with one of their exceptional brews. It’s the perfect spot to unwind and enjoy that easygoing, coastal energy.
properties.name: Golden Isles Brewing Co

properties.blurb: More good news: there’s always something new to try. Good News Brewing keeps the taps rotating with seasonal beers that highlight fresh flavors year-round. From sun-soaked summer brews to cozy winter options, the lineup changes often enough to keep every visit a new experience. Great beer, great pizza, and a reason to keep coming back.
properties.name: Good News Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Fantastic news awaits at Good News Brewing in Augusta! Prepare to immerse yourself in a world of flavorful delights as this brewery brings you a repertoire of beloved brews, both iconic staples and exciting seasonal varieties. But the excitement doesn't stop there. Prepare your taste buds for a journey through the "trail eats" section, a haven of culinary pleasures that includes the mouthwatering wood-fired feta cheese dip and a tantalizing duo of nachos. And as if that weren't enough to tempt your palate, brace yourself for another wave of good news: Good News Brewing also presents an array of delectable sourdough wood-fired pizzas that promise to elevate your dining experience to new heights.
properties.name: Good News Brewing Co (Augusta)

properties.blurb: Pizza and beer. Two words that belong together forever. At Good News Brewing, they’re inseparable. You don’t roll into this joint without pairing a wood-fired sourdough pie with a perfectly poured ESB or West Coast IPA. You could… but only if you enjoy denying yourself humanity’s greatest gastronomic duo.
properties.name: Good News Brewing Co (Defiance)

properties.blurb: Hold onto your glass, because at Good News Brewing, more good news is always on tap! Lucky you, right? It's a place where every visit brings fresh opportunities to savor exceptional craft beer and indulge in their famous scratch crust sourdough pizza. Good News Brewing knows how to make your day shine brighter, one sip and bite at a time. Whether you're a craft beer aficionado or a pizza lover, there's something here that'll make your heart sing.
properties.name: Good News Brewing Co (Frenchtown)

properties.address: 70 SW Century Dr suite 100-464, Bend, OR 97702, USA
properties.name: GoodLife Brewing Company

properties.blurb: This Denver tavern was named for a Colorado bar where German immigrants used to commune. And that bar was named for the Goosetown district, which said immigrants inspired by creating ponds and lakes, encouraging migrating geese to stick around. And while Goosetown Tavern today does not (of course) serve goose, it does honor its history with a whole host of beers and delectable sandwiches. It also hosts regular open mic nights and live shows.
properties.name: Goosetown Tavern

properties.blurb: We love coming to GB’s with our friend Gordon. His last name isn’t Biersch but he loves Biers. So we call him Gordon Biers. It gets pretty confusing at times, but nothing about this place is confusing. Fantastic beers. Equally righteous grub. If you don’t start with some of their Garlic Fries you’re doing it wrong. From there? It’s your world. But don’t sleep on Wagyu Cheeseburger. Thank us later!
properties.name: Gordon Biersch

properties.address: 321 E Gregory Blvd, Kansas City, MO 64114
properties.blurb: Everyone knows the legend of Governor Stumpy, but if you don’t - let us enlighten you. Edward A Stone was somewhat of a local hero. He gained the nickname after a run in with a local band. His Grandchildren opened a restaurant in his memory with the tradition of hospitality and light-hearted fun that he evoked in so many. They always make sure to have a rotating beer list with local breweries featured. Their huge menu is full of pizzas, pastas, burgers and more.
properties.name: Governor Stumpy's Grill House

properties.address: 7085 W Alaska Dr, Lakewood, CO 80226, USA
properties.blurb: At GQue BBQ you can get Colorado’s only Championship BBQ restaurant paired with Craft Beers served from local Colorado Breweries. You can’t go wrong with some smoked USDA Prime Beef Brisket or the smoked chicken wings, they all pair nicely. If you come by on a Monday-Friday between 2p-5p, they will be serving up $1 Beers when you need a second round. Weekly food specials offered every week so there is always something new happening at GQue.
properties.name: GQue BBQ (Lakewood)

properties.address: 8433 Park Meadows Center Dr Suite 143B, Lone Tree, CO 80124, USA
properties.blurb: At GQue BBQ you can get Colorado’s only Championship BBQ restaurant paired with Craft Beers served from local Colorado Breweries. You can’t go wrong with some smoked USDA Prime Beef Brisket or the smoked chicken wings, they all pair nicely. If you come by on a Monday-Friday between 2p-5p, they will be serving up $1 Beers when you need a second round. Weekly food specials offered every week so there is always something new happening at GQue.
properties.name: GQue BBQ (Lone Tree)

properties.address: 5160 W 120th Ave UNIT K, Westminster, CO 80020, USA
properties.blurb: At GQue BBQ you can get Colorado’s only Championship BBQ restaurant paired with Craft Beers served from local Colorado Breweries. You can’t go wrong with some smoked USDA Prime Beef Brisket or the smoked chicken wings, they all pair nicely. If you come by on a Monday-Friday between 2p-5p, they will be serving up $1 Beers when you need a second round. Weekly food specials offered every week so there is always something new happening at GQue.
properties.name: GQue BBQ (Westminster)

properties.address: 4591 Lakeside Dr ste 108, Jacksonville, FL 32210, USA
properties.blurb: Grace Note Brewing hits all the right notes, blending a love for music with a passion for craft beer. With roots in Jacksonville’s music scene since 1994 and brewing expertise since 2010, the owners have crafted a lineup that’s as diverse as their playlists. From classical precision to punk rock energy, every beer echoes their musical influences. Whether you’re into smooth harmonies or bold riffs, you’ll find something to love at Grace Note Brewing.
properties.name: Grace Note Brewing

properties.address: 2379 La Mirada Dr, Vista, CA 92081, USA
properties.name: Grafted Cellars Winery

properties.blurb: Grain + Still is proof that good things happen when passion meets pints. What started as two bartenders slinging drinks at MIA Brewery turned into a BBQ pop-up that won over the crowd, then the in-house kitchen, and eventually the keys to the whole place. Now rebranded and reimagined, Grain + Still is serving up crave-worthy food alongside their own lineup of crisp, creative beers. It’s a love letter to the community that raised them, only with more barbecue sauce and a whole lot more beer.
properties.name: Grain + Still at MIA

properties.address: 14141 Airline Hwy  Bldg 4, Ste A-B  Baton Rouge, LA 70817
properties.blurb: Recklessly Crafted and skillfully consumed. With all styles of beers from Stouts to the Haziest of IPA’s Grain Creations are really creating something tasty. Pair that Crystal Lake Lager with one of the many rotating food options from tacos to crawfish they have it all. Come spend an afternoon with some corn hole and giant Jenga. Or stick around into the evening for some trivia, there is always something to do here.
properties.name: Grain Creations Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Settle in with a refreshing beer like the Escape Groom or get adventurous and start brewing at home with one of Grains and Taps’ home brewing kits for beer or wine. You’ll be crafting your own brews in no time—but trust us, you'll still want to swing by Grains and Taps to grab a pint from the experts. After all, they’ve got the know-how and an ever-rotating tap list, so there’s always something new to discover!
properties.name: Grains & Taps

properties.address: 310 SW Blue Pkwy, Lee's Summit, MO 64063
properties.blurb: At the Pine Tree Plaza location, settle in for a refreshing beer like the Strawberry Told You So, a wheat beer, or the Mango Habanero Milkshake IPA. Or take matters into your own hands and buy a home brewing kit for beer or wine. This location also has a food menu with tasty, crave-able items like a bowl of chili, a sausage basket, and a chicken salad croissant.
properties.name: Grains & Taps (Pine Tree Plaza)

properties.blurb: Prepare to be awestruck by the brewing mastery at Grand Canyon Brewing. Their lineup of great beers is like a magnificent natural wonder, making it tough to choose a favorite. While staying hydrated with water is typically recommended during hikes in nature, Grand Canyon Brewing offers a refreshing alternative with their ice-cold beers. And fear not, fellow patio enthusiasts, there are plenty of options to satisfy your "I'm outdoorsy" spirit. One standout brew you won't want to miss is their Prickly Pear IPA, a tantalizing blend that will transport your taste buds on a flavorful adventure. So whether you're exploring the trails or simply enjoying the great outdoors, Grand Canyon Brewing has the perfect brew to elevate your experience.
properties.name: Grand Canyon Brewing (Page)

properties.address: 1800 S Milton Rd Suite 11, Flagstaff, AZ 86001
properties.blurb: Is there anything the folks at Grand Canyon Brewing and Distillery can’t do? Lots of people might think you should just pick one thing and do it well. We’re here to tell you that Grand Canyon has picked a lot of things and they do them all well. Seriously. This includes the Bavarian pretzel on the menu. And the plentiful outdoor seating. And the prickly pear vodka. And the award-winning gin. And the diverse lineup of craft beers. Quit it already, you overachievers. (Just kidding. Please don’t quit.)
properties.name: Grand Canyon Brewing + Distillery

properties.address: 312 E Beale St, Kingman, AZ 86401, USA
properties.name: Grand Canyon Brewing + Distillery (Kingman)

properties.blurb: We are just seriously impressed by the brewing setup at Grand Canyon Brewing. So many great beers in the lineup that it’s hard to pick a fave. For those of you that live life adventurously, the Trail Hike IPA is definitely for you. It’s light and refreshing and while most experts would recommend water as the preferred method of hydration while hiking in nature, an ice cold beer is a lovely backup option. Don’t worry, there are plenty of options for you “I’m outdoorsy in that I like drinking on patios” folks, too.
properties.name: Grand Canyon Brewing + Distillery (Williams)

properties.blurb: There are moments in life where you don’t want to be bothered by staff. Not here. With European-style serving tanks for fresher beer and longer shelf life, Grand Central takes their craft seriously, right down to the passionate staff they hire. Bonus: each month, they donate 15% of their sales from three beers toward three different local charities. Welcome to one of your new faves.
properties.name: Grand Central Brewhouse

properties.blurb: You’ll spot Grand Central by its bold blue exterior in the heart of Adams Morgan. However, it’s what’s inside that keeps the crowds coming. By day, it’s a laid-back sports bar with elevated pub fare and local beers on tap. By night, it transforms into one of DC’s most energetic party spots, complete with DJs, dancing, and all the high-energy vibes. Whether you're catching the game or the beat, Grand Central delivers a good time on every level.
properties.name: Grand Central Restaurant & Bar

properties.blurb: Gravity Brewing offers an ever-evolving menu, a welcoming and inviting taproom (ping pong, anyone?) and the outdoor biergarten is the perfect place to sip one of their latest offerings. You’ll find a wide array of crushable Lagers, rich Stouts and Porters, and hearty IPAs. Now grab a refill and challenge your pals to a rematch. You only lost because you didn’t want to spill your beer.
properties.name: Gravity Brewing

properties.address: 777 N Orange Ave Suite D, Orlando, FL 32801, USA
properties.blurb: Gravity Taproom isn’t just your average beer spot, it’s a whole vibe. Sure, we could tell you to dive into their ever-evolving beer list (and you absolutely should), but they’ve got way more than just brews. From canned cocktails and ciders to wine and soda, there’s a little something for everyone, even if you're not in the mood for beer. Hungry? Their pizzas and shareables pair perfectly with anything on the menu, whether it’s from a local Florida brewery or one of the best craft spots around. Grab a seat and stay a while. The options are endless and always changing!
properties.name: Gravity Taproom

properties.blurb: This is the OG Great Dane. The Dane that started it all. The Dane that was brewing craft beer in the heart of Madison before you had your first West Coast Hazy Northeast Double Superlative Imperial Qualifier Ale. So grab a Hopsconsin or a Pils and some Old Glory cheese curds as you check out a menu as eclectic as the building you’re sitting in is historic. Patio season? There might not be a better spot to dine al fresco in town. Hey, are you gonna finish those Andouille Corn Dogs?
properties.name: Great Dane Pub

properties.blurb: The Great Dane isn’t just a brewpub, it’s a Madison institution. From classic lagers to bold, hoppy IPAs, they’ve got a beer lineup that’s as reliable as it is adventurous. Whether you’re kicking things off with a crisp, no-nonsense pilsner or diving into something tropical and juicy, their house-brewed beers strike that perfect balance of drinkability and flavor. And that’s just round one.
properties.name: Great Dane Pub & Brewing

properties.blurb: Because once you’ve had a taste of what the Great Dane is pouring, it’s hard not to come back for more. Each location offers a fresh reason to linger longer. It's the same relaxed energy, same excellent beer, always something a little different on tap. Consider this your official invitation to start making the rounds.
properties.name: Great Dane Pub & Brewing (Fitchburg)
Great Dane Pub & Brewing Co (East Side)

properties.blurb: Cozy but lively, Great Divide tends to bring back many a customer who have fallen for their beers. We embraced the fruit with a Strawberry Rhubarb sour and a Peach Milkshake IPA. Food trucks nearby quell the hunger pangs and growlers-to-go make sure anything we loved was gonna come home with us. Definitely worth doing a flight or two to find that special something.
properties.name: Great Divide Brewing Co

properties.blurb: If you want your beer to be substandard, cliché, and unremarkable, steer clear of Great Divide Brewing Co. It’s one of the most decorated breweries in the country, and has been brewing great beer since the early ‘90s. Great Divide has also made serving the environment a top priority, with sustainable breakrooms, eco-passes for employees, a community recycling program, Certified Green taprooms, and more.
properties.name: Great Divide Brewing Co (Barrel bar)

properties.blurb: Great Escape embodies one of our favorite mottos - we’re not here for a long time, we’re here for a good time. If your idea of a good time is choosing from one of thirty beers on tap while playing arcade games, then this is the place for you. Wanna pre-game before you decimate your nephew in a round of laser tag? This is the place for you. Need to impress your girl? Roll up your sleeves and join in on the bags tournament. Only true Iowans can play with a beer in one hand and a bag in the other without spilling a drop
properties.name: Great Escape

properties.blurb: Great Heart Brewing Co is all about thoughtful flavor with a whole lot of heart. Their small-batch European-style ales and lagers are brewed in St. Louis with ingredients sourced from Bavaria, Bohemia, and the Pacific Northwest. Each pint crafted with care from the first sip to the last. Add in their farm-to-table Alpine-fusion cuisine, and you’ve got plates and pours that complement each other beautifully. By the time you head out, you won’t just be full, you’ll be Great-full.
properties.name: Great Heart Brewing Co

properties.blurb: We settled in at GLD and ordered a draft Old Fashioned (we recommend you start here as well) just to get the lay of the land. The drink and the vibe was fantastic. From there, we wandered a menu with enough different spirits and cocktails to warrant several return trips. This place will remind you that while Cream City is famous for beer? They know a thing or two about perfecting booze as well. Cheers! Your PubPass is good for one cocktail on tap!
properties.name: Great Lakes Distillery

properties.address: 25 Drexel Dr, Bay Shore, NY 11706, USA
properties.name: Great South Bay Brewery

properties.address: 2294 N Batavia St STE C, Orange, CA 92865, USA
properties.name: Green Cheek Beer Co.

properties.address: 4010 Pennsylvania Ave Suite D, Kansas City, MO 64111
properties.blurb: Do you want beer from the oldest brewery in Kansas City? What about some real Rascals Smashburgers? Let us answer that for you by saying yes, yes you do. Trust us, the beer is delicious and the burgers are unmatched. The beers are always rotating as they are made in small batches which has us coming back time and time again. They even have a Poppin’ Plant Burger if that’s your speed. Add some fries or tots and you won’t be sorry.
properties.name: Green Room Burgers and Beer

properties.blurb: With 4,500 square feet of outdoor space, picnic tables that can hold up to 350 people and a huge selection of both draft and bottled beers we can only wish our home garden was as cool as Green Valley Ranch Beer Garden. Not only all of that but they also have a grill with casual fare with locally-made gourmet brats and sausages, freshly baked pretzels and house ground burgers. Bring your whole family for a backyard party with friends you just haven’t met, yet.
properties.name: Green Valley Ranch Beer Garden

properties.blurb: There are two types of people in the world: people who have their go-to beer and never stray from it, and people who are always mixing it up. Greg’s Kitchen & Taphouse is the perfect place for the latter, with 75 taps dedicated to the best local, regional, and national craft beers and ciders. They also offer classic American pub food designed with their brews in mind.
properties.name: Greg’s Kitchen & Taphouse

properties.blurb: While the Grimm Brothers might spin dark and twisty tales, the folks at Grimm Brothers Brewhouse are all about good times and great pours. They’ve taken the charm of the old legends and worked it into an impressive lineup of beers and classic German-inspired dishes. Favorites like Snow Drop, a German Kottbusser, keep the story going, while rotating guest taps make sure there’s always a new twist to try.
properties.name: Grimm Brothers Brewhouse

properties.blurb: This Kansas City location features an eclectic rotating beer list and Death Wings that come in wimpy, molten, near death, and death nectar. For legal issues, we have to inform you that death nectar will burn off a few taste buds and is not for the faint of wing. Grinders also offers pizza (naturally) and the appropriately named Big Ass Fries and Big Ass Tots.
properties.name: Grinders Pizza

properties.blurb: This Stonewall location features an eclectic rotating beer list and Death Wings that come in wimpy, molten, near death, and death nectar. For legal reasons, we have to inform you that death nectar will burn off a few taste buds and is not for the faint of wing. Grinders also offers pizza (naturally) and Famous Chili Bomb Pie, which the menu warns may need a defibrillator nearby.
properties.name: Grinders Stonewall

properties.address: 1650 N Hercules Ave Unit I, Clearwater, FL 33765
properties.blurb: No one commits to a bit better than Grindhaus Brew Lab. From their current fall selections including David S. Pumpkins (pumpkin pie spiced American amber ale) and Good Morning Maple Cream! (rasp-straw-blueberry Berliner Weisse Sour with cinnamon, vanilla, and milk sugar) to their Harry Potter-themed beers, this brew lab has a potion for every muggle. And for the people who don’t know what that word means.
properties.name: Grindhaus Brew Lab

properties.address: 9150 Commerce Center Cir #300, Highlands Ranch, CO 80129
properties.blurb: What do a Peanut Butter White Russian, Sucker Punch beer, deviled eggs, and a Sake bomb have in common? You can order every one of them at Grist Brewing Company. With an extensive food, cocktail, and — naturally — beer menu, Grist has something for every palate. For the beer super fan, we recommend joining the 2022 Grist Classic Mug Club for a little extra beer in every pour.
properties.name: Grist Brewing Company

properties.blurb: If you’re from the west side of town, you already know Grizzly’s has a phenomenal tap list and burgers worth the drive. If not, you might start making regular trips…
properties.name: Grizzly's

properties.blurb: In our experience, a bearded bartender equals a solid beer. It’s just science. The beer (and beard) enthusiasts behind Grossen Bart have one goal—make delicious beers—and do they ever. The year-round award-winning lineup is as stunning as the mountain view on the patio. The Strip-Teaser Pale Ale and Stubble Kolsch are some of our faves and pair well with just about any snack from the food trucks that frequent the brewery. Grab your buds, bearded and otherwise, and hit this gem in Longmont.
properties.name: Grossen Bart Brewing Co

properties.address: 1834 Saint Clair Ave, Saint Paul, MN 55105
properties.blurb: The Tap is the neighborhood bar all neighborhood bars are judged against. They were tinkering with burgers before it was chic. Half the clientele “studied” here when they were in college. This is a true tavern where newcomers and grizzled veterans alike can belly up as equals. Get some curds or fries while you settle in and scope the tap handles on the walls… they’ll remind you just how long the Tap has been pouring one of the better lists around.
properties.name: Groveland Tap

properties.blurb: The mantra here is taste it, tap it, and take it. It’s like a beer drinking version of Bop-It. Featuring at least 16 draft options, belly up to the bar and sample any of these tasty beer beauties before you commit to a growler. Proudly stating that their selections often can’t be found in bottle form, Growlers To Go-Go is a definitive must-stop when your poor palette is exhausted from just the same old same old. Wake up your tastebuds, friend.
properties.name: Growlers To Go-Go

properties.blurb: The concept at Growlers to Go-Go is beautifully simple: They put amazing beers on draft. You come drink them. Then you leave with more beer to take home or to a gathering, where you impress your friends with your craft beer knowledge that is actually Growlers to Go-Go’s knowledge but nobody has to know. Your PubPass is good for a pint from select lines, but you’ll want to try them all.
properties.name: Growlers to Go-Go

properties.address: 101 W 22nd St, Kansas City, MO 64108, USA
properties.blurb: Imagine pulling up to a big family dinner with your European relatives—the kind where every dish has been perfected over generations. At Grünauer, even if you don’t have that European side, you do now. They welcome you with Austrian and Central European classics made from cherished family recipes, with top-tier ingredients and techniques straight from Vienna. It’s tradition meets quality in every bite, whether you’re digging into schnitzel or savoring strudel. And trust us, you’ll leave feeling like part of the family.
properties.name: Grünauer

properties.blurb: Sometimes, what beer you drink isn’t as important as where you drink it. At least, that’s the motto of Guanella Pass Brewery, nestled at the foot of the Guanella Pass Scenic Byway in Historic Georgetown, Colorado. GPB is the first brewery in Georgetown since the Prohibition, and they welcome “those with an explorer’s spirit, who find themselves on elevated paths.” Just beware of their 3 Bark Rule for dogs: at the third bark, owners are asked to leave. But the dog gets to stay forever.
properties.name: Guanella Pass Brewery

properties.blurb: Who would’ve thought some of Colorado’s best tacos would be found at the mall? Guapo Taco, you had us at “a stocked bar with a dozen tequilas.” Yes, the inventive and extensive taco lineup is impressive. Sure, we love a happy hour. But a seasoned PubPasser is gonna have some big decisions to make when it comes to choosing just one beverage to have alongside their corn ribs and brisket tacos. Although, legend has it that it is possible for a seasoned PubPasser to have more than one drink, but just remember, don’t drink and shop.
properties.name: Guapo Taco Taco Bar

properties.blurb: We will never not order a giant bavarian pretzel with a side of beer cheese. At Guilford, it’s so enormous, your friends are gonna have to roll you out the door at the end of the night, but trust us, it will be so worth it. We’d recommend pairing that pretzel with the Guilford Lager, the flagship beer that’s crisp and fresh, or the Golden Ale. The rustic charm gives off a European beer hall feel and we are totally here for it.
properties.name: Guilford Hall Brewery

properties.blurb: Beer for you. Soda for the kiddos. Pizza for everyone! We had a blast at Gulf Stream. Start your session with a Hold My Beer or Transplant. Then get hoppy with a Dr. Dank or Graphic. Prefer your beers historic and awarded? Gulf Steam is a true Steam Beer that won a US Open Silver Medal in 2021. Pair all this with incredible pies from Pizzeria Magaddino and you’re ready to sail.
properties.name: Gulf Stream Brewing

properties.address: 3007 Beach Blvd S, Gulfport, FL 33707, USA
properties.blurb: Gulfport Brewing has a remarkable story that began in 2019, fueled by a vision to bring exceptional beer and food to the dynamic town of Gulfport, FL. With meticulous planning and the addition of a talented head brewer, this dream turned into a reality in the summer of 2020. Locals and visitors alike have been drawn to the scratch kitchen's mouthwatering offerings and the top-notch brews. Situated in the historic, beachside charm of Gulfport, they’ve become a hub of community and flavor. Don't miss out on experiencing the intriguing blend of flavors and vibes that define Gulfport Brewing!
properties.name: Gulfport Brewery + Eatery

properties.address: Second Floor, 68 34th St, Brooklyn, NY 11232, USA
properties.name: Gun Hill Publick House

properties.blurb: Our kinda place respects pinball, and Gun Barrel had a bevy of games to choose from. If that wasn’t enough, they also happen to brew some of Colorado’s finest beers. We were quite taken by the Velvet Sea, a New England IPA that was just hoppy enough. Plenty of room to spread out with friends, both indoors and on their patio, and the nearby Colorado Fried Chicken Food Truck that rolled up made the night all the more special.
properties.name: Gunbarrel Brewing Co

properties.blurb: You don’t come to Gunter Hans for a simple beer. You come for a beer flight. You come for a Lavender Haze cocktail. You come for a tasty shot like the Tree Sap with maple syrup and apple cider and whiskey. You come for a German-style bretzel, made from scratch with gourmet dips like mustard and cinnamon butter. You come for a gourmet dip flight. And, okay, you can come for a simple beer. But let's face it: why would you?
properties.name: Gunter Hans

properties.blurb: Chandler is killing the happy hour game, and HOP Central Brewery and Taproom is at the top of the list. All the cool kids are popping in after work to partake in a wide variety of local and national craft brews. We know peer pressure is bad, but it’s important to tell you that the COOLEST kids are checking out the House IPA. So, grab your pals, be the cool kid, and just get to HOP already and start sampling any of the 36 beers and wine on tap.
properties.name: H.O.P. Central Brewing Co and Taproom

properties.blurb: It’s a good thing they have really excellent beer here, otherwise we would wax on poetically only about their food menu and how absolutely heavenly it is. But they’ve decided to double dip with the taste gods and have created equally as amazing beers. Their dabbles in experimentation have successfully procured them an ever-changing lineup that never fails. They hold no punches when it comes to flavor and they’re going to satisfy your craving no matter what style you have in mind.
properties.name: Hacienda Beer Co

properties.address: 18651 Lake Dr E, Chanhassen, MN 55317, USA
properties.blurb: At Hackamore Brewing, you don’t have to choose between a brewery, live music, or perfecting your golf swing in their highly-advanced golf simulator. You can have it all, plus a brew shop, all in one spot. Start off with a Can Crusher cream ale (it’s like a secret weapon for your golf game, which we'll always take the help), then follow it up with Grandma’s Kitchen Strawberry Rhubarb Pie Kettle Sour while swaying to the live tunes. Whether you’re here to sip, swing, or sway, Hackamore's got your evening covered.
properties.name: Hackamore Brewing Co

properties.address: 325 N Washington St, Green Bay, WI 54301
properties.blurb: Wisconsin might get chilly in the winters, but Hagemeister Park is ready for it with riverside igloos complete with pillows, blankets, candles, board games, and, of course, heating. The proud first home of the Green Bay Packers, this restaurant also offers year-round draws like clam chowder and beer battered cod on Fridays, and a beer menu including Neon Beer Hug and Watermelon Salt Sipper.
properties.name: Hagemeister Park
