properties.blurb: They say, “Purveyors of local libations,” and we say…well dog-gone it, we say they’re absolutely right. The tap list is paws-atively sure to satisfy offering an array of brews from different AZ breweries (plus a couple of out-of-towners), a selection of 16 wines for especially ruff days, and rotating choices from the Superstition Meadery. Make sure to check out their food truck selection, the next movie night, live music, and various dog-themed events. All four-legged furry friends are welcome and encouraged to make for a howling good time. We should warn you now, this place is about to have you going absolutely mutts.
properties.name: Hair of the Dog

properties.blurb: At Half Barrel Beer Project, small batches mean big flavor. Their half-barrel system keeps things fresh and rotating, so there’s always a new brew waiting to surprise you. Think of it like an ongoing experiment in great beer. One that just happens to taste amazing. With handpicked craft beers from Florida and beyond on tap, plus plenty of bites to keep you hanging out, this isn’t just another brewery. It’s the kind of place you come for “just one” and somehow end up closing out the night.
properties.name: Half Barrel Beer Project

properties.blurb: The unofficial motto of Half Batch is “never half ass.” And founder Nate lives up to that motto, with a classic British bitter named after it and the mascot Cheeky Pete. Half Batch is Henderson’s first and only craft brewery, just 20 minutes outside of Nashville, and has been delighting whole-assed drinkers (sorry) for over five years now.
properties.name: Half Batch Brewing

properties.address: 5150 E Arapahoe Rd unit d1-b, Centennial, CO 80122
properties.blurb: Get ready for a hop-tastic adventure at Halfpenny Brewing! These beer enthusiasts specialize in crafting handcrafted brews, with a special love for German and English styles that'll make your taste buds dance with joy. Sip on their zesty Clementine American Wheat or dive into their ever-changing lineup of tantalizing rotating beers. Halfpenny Brewing is your ticket to flavorful fun and a beer-filled escapade that'll leave you coming back for more. So grab a pint, raise it high, and let the good times flow at Halfpenny Brewing! Prost, mates!
properties.name: Halfpenny Brewing

properties.blurb: When we tell you to roll up to a PubPass spot hungry? We always mean it. But we REALLY mean it at HalfSmoke. Get yourself a round of beers and dig into a peerless bar menu. Start with some Dough Gods — heavenly next level garlic knots that pair well with something hoppy. Then get a Yardbird or a Walk of Shame and you're really in business. But the real show stopper here? The sausages. Trust us.
properties.name: HalfSmoke

properties.blurb: Half Time knows how to fuel a good time. We started with Mike’s Mac N Cheese Bites, moved on to Paul’s Famous Burgers, and then dove into Theo’s MVP Pizza, a St. Louis-style thin-crust gem. With a beer in hand before halftime, we realized we might need to be rolled out like a soccer ball! No game on tonight? Music bingo and live tunes keep the energy up. Whatever the plan, Half Time’s menu and vibe make sure you never leave hungry or bored.
properties.name: Halftime Pub & Grill

properties.blurb: We came for a tour, stayed for a tasting, and enjoyed some of the finest cocktails this side of the Lower Miss. The husband and wife team at Happy Raptor draws their inspiration from the Caribbean, creating hearty, full-flavored white rum infused for days or weeks with real botanicals, fruits, and spices. Looking to raise your spirits? Stop in for a monthly cocktail class on the patio. It’s sure to do the trick.
properties.name: Happy Raptor Distilling

properties.address: 3820 S Palo Verde Rd #102, Tucson, AZ 85714, USA
properties.blurb: Here a touch of medieval enchantment meets contemporary brewing expertise. Drawing inspiration from the historic Harbottle Castle in England, this whimsical brewery delights in crafting beers that pay homage to the castle's legacy while infusing them with modern flavors and creativity. Each sip takes you on a journey through time, where the essence of the castle's history intertwines with the art of brewing. From knights to maidens, Harbottle Brewing Co invites all beer enthusiasts to indulge in their imaginative brews and experience the magic that flows from their taps.
properties.name: Harbottle Brewing Company

properties.blurb: Harding House Brewing lives up to their motto of being "Creatively Crafted. Uniquely Local. Seasonally Brewed." This innovative brewery takes pride in their ability to craft beers that are not only delicious but also full of character. Each brew is thoughtfully created, showcasing the passion and expertise of their talented team. With a strong commitment to supporting local suppliers and using high-quality ingredients, Harding House Brewing brings a distinct local flavor to every pint. But what truly sets them apart is their dedication to brewing seasonally. Embracing the changing seasons, they capture the essence of each time of year in their carefully curated beer selection. From refreshing summer ales to robust winter stouts, every sip tells a story. So, whether you're seeking a hop-forward IPA or a rich and malty brew, Harding House Brewing is sure to have something that will tantalize your taste buds.
properties.name: Harding House Brewing

properties.address: East building, 2711 E Lakin Dr #2, Flagstaff, AZ 86004, USA
properties.name: Harrington's Tap House

properties.blurb: Is This Heaven? is not just the name of a gin cocktail at Hartman’s, it also encapsulates how we feel about the place. This craft cocktail list is insane in all the best ways - full of unique ingredients that shouldn’t work all shaken together, but somehow, miraculously do. Heaven. Also the perfect noun to describe the Quebecois Dip. A visit to Hartman’s Distilling is as close as you’re gonna get to heaven in Buffalo until the Bills bring home a championship.
properties.name: Hartman's Distilling Co

properties.address: 5840 N 60th St Suite NW, Milwaukee, WI 53218, USA
properties.name: Havenwoods Taproom and Beer Garden

properties.blurb: Do you bleed black and gold? Does your heart beat to the rhythm of a hawk’s wings? Do you have a general dislike of the song Cyclone by Baby Bash? If any of those things ring true for you then this here is your new home away from home. Even if you don’t love the Hawkeyes this tavern is an excellent spot to kick back with your friends. Make sure you don’t miss out on a comedy night or the live music!
properties.name: Hawk Heaven Tavern

properties.address: 861 E Hennepin Ave, Suite 100 Minneapolis, MN 55414
properties.blurb: Phenomenal beers in equally impressive surroundings await you here. Start off with their flagship beer, "It Was All a Dream", or their "Vanilla Bean Porter" on Nitro.  End the night with their rotating kettle sour of the season, or one of their refreshing lagers or other rotating Hazy IPAs
properties.name: HeadFlyer Brewing

properties.address: 100 W Pearl St, Harrisonville, MO 64701, USA
properties.blurb: Who says you need to jump on a plane to sip the best wines from California and six other countries? At Headquarters Winery and Mercantile, you can globe-trot through flavors without ever facing an airport TSA line. Build your own pasta bowl, grab a hearty salad, soup, or pizza, and toast to international adventure with no luggage, long security lines, or middle seats required. It’s the world, delivered straight to your table.
properties.name: HEADQUARTERS Winery & Mercantile

properties.blurb: Helio Basin Brewing Company touts itself as a place where the sun is always shining. It also touts an impressive roster of craft beer that have brought home some serious hardware. Take for example their Fayuca Rizing Xtra Pale Ale. Ranked as the third best American Pale Ale by Beer Connoisseur, this Pale Ale pays homage to local band Fayuca. Every drink from this crushable, concert thirst quencher feels like the cool sounds of Reggae jamming on your taste buds.
properties.name: Helio Basin Brewing Company

properties.blurb: You mean to tell us the brewery is inside a 118 year old house, the brewery is called Hello, and all the beers are named after people? Now that is a cute bit we can get behind. Any guesses on what they’ll name their next beer? We think Petunia has a nice ring to it, but maybe something timeless like Eleanor or Arthur. Either way it’s comforting to know their latest creations are always going to be top tier, they are truly flavor geniuses. We’ll race you to the house!
properties.name: Hello Brew Co

properties.address: 2144 E Indian School Rd Phoenix, AZ 85016
properties.blurb: Helton Brewing was founded by Brian Helton, a brewmaster and certified Cicerone, so they know what they’re doing when it comes to beer. They also know what they’re doing when it comes to supporting the neighborhood by contributing to charities, as well through events and promoting artists. If you want to drink for good, do some good and tip back a pint of their heavily dry-hopped Northeast IPA, an absolute must-drink.
properties.name: Helton Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Sitting in the shadow of a hockey arena and inspired by the legacy of one of the greatest puck coaches of all time, Herbie’s on the Park is down-to-earth-yet-refined. Start with the craft beer that brought you in here, but don’t sleep on one of the better Old Fashioneds in town. Hungry? Whether you’re in the mood for a snack or a steak, Herbie’s will have you believing in miracles.
properties.name: Herbie's On The Park

properties.address: 2015 Winnebago St Suite 101, Madison, WI 53704, USA
properties.blurb: Prepare to embark on a whimsical journey at Herbiery Brewery, where old meets new in the most delightful way. This modern brewery is all about using timeless ingredients to create beers that are truly out of this world. Instead of hops, they harness the power of herbs and spices to infuse their brews with unique and enchanting flavors. Step outside the traditional and try their Great Sage Witbeer or Zingibeer Ginger Lager for a taste experience like no other. Nestled in the picturesque landscapes of Wisconsin, Herbiery Brewery has found the perfect home to craft their magical concoctions. So come, embrace the whimsy, and let Herbiery take your taste buds on an unforgettable adventure
properties.name: Herbiery Taproom

properties.blurb: One of the O.G. brewpubs in town, this local gem probably introduced you to many of the styles of beer it still brews. Drinkable and diverse, The Herk’s tap list is always sure to please. We started with a Gose and some pretzels before pairing a High Point Dunkel with their inimitable pulled pork sandwich and some sweet potato fries. Brunch here is stellar as well and, if you’re a Packer fan? This is your new spot for games.
properties.name: Herkimer

properties.address: 5940 W Union Hills Dr e100, Glendale, AZ 85308
properties.blurb: Heroes Pub in Glendale is your ultimate destination for a night of fun and flavor. Sing your heart out at karaoke, challenge your wits at trivia, and indulge in their delicious pizza. With a wide selection of local brews, a lively patio, and even a golf machine, there's never a dull moment at Heroes Pub. Come for the football, stay for the vibrant atmosphere and the mouthwatering menu. Cheers to good times and unforgettable memories at Heroes Pub in Glendale.
properties.name: Heroes Pub

properties.blurb: No one will tell you you’re not in Germany at Hessen Haus. With 50 imported German biers on tap, authentic German fare on the menu, and the old-world atmosphere of a German bier hall, it’s easy to convince yourself you’re at Oktoberfest instead of in Iowa. Stop in to take in the local or regional polka bands, and make sure to pass the boot before you go.
properties.name: Hessen Haus

properties.address: 11804 Tesson Ferry Rd, St. Louis, MO 63128, USA
properties.blurb: Hessler’s Pub & Grill has been one of South County’s favorite hangouts since the early ’90s. An old favorite in all the best ways. With six rotating seasonal brews pouring alongside the classics you already know and love, it’s easy to settle in, grab a pint, and feel right at home. Some things never go out of style, and Hessler’s proves that great beer and good company are at the top of that list.
properties.name: Hessler's Pub & Grill

properties.blurb: Hi Sign Brewing started with a simple idea, brew incredible beer for people who love to drink it. That spark was born way up in Montana on a stretch of road called Hi Sign, and it’s been flowing ever since. Now proudly veteran-owned and brewing in East Austin, they’re all about fresh ingredients, bold flavors, and a welcoming space to kick back with friends, old and new.
properties.name: Hi Sign Brewing

properties.address: 2500 N Pleasant View Rd Suite 102, Middleton, WI 53562
properties.blurb: No spelunking necessary at the Hidden Cave Cidery. Instead you'll find picturesque Wisconsin prairie farmland, a frills-free tasting room, and some of the best damn ciders made nationwide. Founded by award-winning Cider Maker Walker Fanning, a few words with him and the passion for cider is lip-smackingly evident. Using local apples like  McIntosh, Cortland, Haralson, and Granny Smith, the ciders are refreshing, bright and never overly sweet. Unique flavors like chocolate hazenut and hibiscus juniper ensure that even the most stalwart ciderphile will find something new. Kid-friendly, occasional live music, and a setting worthy of a postcard all craft a cider experience worth a return trip.
properties.name: Hidden Cave Cidery

properties.address: 2332 La Mirada Dr #700, Vista, CA 92081, USA
properties.name: Hidden Hive

properties.address: 1631 N Franklin St, Tampa, FL 33602, USA
properties.name: Hidden Springs Ale Works

properties.blurb: Colorado pride abounds at High Brau Taphouse. Behind the sleek bar are over 30 taps, and each one features an independent Colorado brew. And it’s not just beer, folks! They’re also pouring cider, mead, kombucha, and their latest additions: craft soda, cold brew coffee, and spirits from local microdistillers. Come taste the best this state has to offer, in whatever variety you prefer.
properties.name: High Brau Taphouse

properties.blurb: There’s nothing we didn’t love about HIllsboro Brewing Company, a brewery nestled in the heart of the Driftless Wisconsin area. From the beer lineup to the pizza menu to the historic building that is at once cozy and massive—we loved it all. We especially loved the Snappy IPA, which is super drinkable, and the bright and crisp Friday Night Lights Oktoberfest. Clear eyes. Full hearts. Eat cheese curds. That’s a thing, right?
properties.name: Hillsboro Brewing Co

properties.blurb: They want to take you off the beaten path of beers and honestly you should just go with them. Let them guide you through their year round brews like Saving Gracie (Hazy IPA) or Splitty (Amber Ale), or what pairs best with their limited releases and seasonal brews. Try to snag a reservation at their High Gravity Test Kitchen on available weekends and enjoy the captivating and mouthwatering menus they create.
properties.name: Hinterland Brewery

properties.address: 110 S San Francisco St, Flagstaff, AZ 86001, USA
properties.name: Historic Brewing Barrel House (Flagstaff)

properties.address: 4366 E Huntington Dr, Flagstaff, AZ 86004, USA
properties.blurb: Historic Brewing Co is more than a brewery; it's an immersion in a world of taste, camaraderie, and shared appreciation for exceptional craft beer. Whether one is a seasoned aficionado or embarking on a beer odyssey, the doors are open to step in, raise a glass, and become part of the historical narrative. As you step into the welcoming tap rooms, they're greeted with a fusion of flavors that range from the timeless Piehole Porter to the seasonal delight of Undercover Cucumber. Yet, the exploration doesn't stop there. The limited-release brews offer a window into the brewmaster's artistry, each with its devoted following. These unique creations embody a spirit of creativity and boundary-pushing, resulting in liquid treasures that captivate the taste buds.
properties.name: Historic Brewing Company

properties.address: 1636 N Main St, Jacksonville, FL 32206, USA
properties.blurb: At Historically Hoppy, or H2 as the regulars know it, they’ve mastered the art of brewing by blending the best of the old and the new. Whether you're craving a crisp IPA, a smooth lager, or a refreshing Kölsch, their 16 rotating taps have got you covered. The tap list is always evolving, so there's always something fresh and exciting to try. Add in the cozy vibe and lively events, and you’ve got the perfect spot to unwind with a great beer in hand.
properties.name: Historically Hoppy Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Hodge Podge can do a little bit of everything. Honestly, we go just as hard for a handcrafted latte as we do a handcrafted cocktail. Hodge Podge is the perfect meeting spot because everyone has THAT friend who says stuff like “Ugh, I’m too tired to go out.” Well, guess what, Becky. You can’t get out of happy hour this week because they serve espresso. Don’t mind us, we’re ordering gimlets. But you sip on that delicious cup of bean juice and just like that, everyone is happy.
properties.name: Hodge Podge

properties.blurb: We’ve had our fair share of beer over the years. But Hogshead’s Gilpin Black Gold Porter is hands-down one of the smoothest we’ve ever imbibed. These are real-deal British beers in the heart of Denver. Stand on the patio, take a sip, close your eyes and you might just feel transported to a sidewalk outside a pub in London. Proper pints for a proper good time await. Cheers!
properties.name: Hogshead Brewing

properties.blurb: Thirst things first: If authentic English-style beer is your thing, then Hogshead Brewing should be, too. These guys specialize in cask-conditioned ales, and—truth be told—they’re all mighty tasty. Knock back a few Chin Wag Extra Special Bitters, and you'll feel like you've hopped across the pond without having ever left your barstool.
properties.name: Hogshead Brewing

properties.blurb: When gluten-free beer drinkers finally find a tasty brew, it can feel like Christmas morning. Enter Holidaily Brewing: a 100% gluten free brewery with beer good enough to fool anyone. Build your own flight or order a suggested one to sample the range of styles Holidaily has on hand, from hazy IPA to blonde ale. Once you’ve picked a favorite, be sure to order a crowler to-go.
properties.name: Holidaily Brewing (Golden)

properties.address: 5370 Greenwood Plaza Blvd Suite 107, Greenwood Village, CO 80111
properties.blurb: Holidaily’s newest location in DTC has tons of seating and daily happy hour specials, making it the perfect place to unwind after a long workday. They continue to set the standard for gluten free beer, and you know it’s working when so many non-GF folks love them too. Try the ever-popular Favorite Blonde or toe the line with a Cease and Desist Cookie Stout. Order delivery from nearby restaurants to make a night of it.
properties.name: Holidaily Brewing (Greenwood Village)

properties.blurb: Hollywood Brewing is our home away from home. Well, let’s be honest, we’re PubPass… we have many homes away from home. But this is the only one where snow crab legs, local gulf shrimp, potato skins, and a dozen different pizzas can coexist under the same delicious roof (and next to the beach!). Oh and we didn’t even get to the fantastic beers?! Come on in!
properties.name: Hollywood Brewery Bar & Grill

properties.address: 1103 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
properties.blurb: Look at that, LOL. Sorry, we’re not laughing at you. We’re admiring the substantial head on Hollywood's Locals Only Lager (LOL). Light and bready, it’s perfect for a hot Florida day. Beers like this are the reason we come to Hollywood Brewing. But we end up staying longer because their food menu is 10/10. Truffle burgers, sausage platters, traditional Russian borscht soup … interesting flex, we know, but you can’t go wrong with anything they’re serving up. Now, come join us for some LOLs at either the Los Olas or Lauderdale Beach location.
properties.name: Hollywood Brewing Las Olas

properties.blurb: Although they’ve been brewing crooked and demented beers for well over a decade, Holy Mackerel finally has its forever home. And what a home it is. Their green space is the perfect place to kick up your feet and knock back a pint of their Panic Attack Belgian-Style Specialty. Clocking in at 10%, it’ll surely help you take the edge off. Need a nosh? The Loaded Fries will get you started and any of HM's BBQ offerings will take things next level. Is it just us or do you need another beer?
properties.name: Holy Mackerel

properties.address: 5401 Linda Vista Rd #406, San Diego, CA 92110, USA
properties.name: Home Brew Mart

properties.address: 6325 Elysian Fields Ave, New Orleans, LA 70122
properties.name: Homegrown Pizza

properties.blurb: Homegrown Taproom and Kitchen is a true Nashville gem that embodies the spirit of "homegrown" in every aspect. They take pride in sourcing their ingredients from local farmers and growers, ensuring that every bite is fresh and flavorful. With 24 taps at the ready, beer enthusiasts can explore a wide selection of craft brews while enjoying the welcoming atmosphere. When it comes to their menu, Homegrown Taproom and Kitchen has something for everyone. From munchies that will tantalize your taste buds to comforting soups and hearty salads, their culinary offerings are a delightful fusion of flavors. And let's not forget about their mouthwatering sandwiches that are crafted with care and creativity.
properties.name: Homegrown Taproom and Kitchen

properties.address: 4225 W Fortune Dr. Suite 100, Anthem, AZ 85086
properties.blurb: Homeland Brew Co. is a family-owned taproom in Anthem, AZ, serving up great beer, good people, and small-town pride under one roof. From Arizona craft favorites to rotating taps and food trucks, there’s always something new to sip and savor. With a bold Americana vibe and a love for community, this isn’t just a bar, it’s a hometown hangout built on grit, flavor, and heart. Welcome to the Neighbrewhood where home is always just a cold beer away.
properties.name: Homeland Brew Co

properties.blurb: We wish our backyard was like The Oasis at Hook Hall. They have private tables and cabanas you can reserve for your group. We don't have that. They've got Seafood Fritters and a rockin' Cubano. We don't have those, either. Come to think of it, there's really no reason we shouldn't spread out here more often. Can we just move in?
properties.name: Hook Hall

properties.blurb: Legend has it that brewmasters with beards brew the best beers. The bearded best buds behind Hop & Barrel Brewing offer up some refreshing and bold brews. When we say refreshing, we mean a classic shandy, and Hop & Barrel brews THE best in the business. Yes, that might be controversial. No, we don’t care! Take that shandy out to the gorgeous patio and soak up those summer rays before winter hits and you remember where you live.
properties.name: Hop & Barrel Brewing Co

properties.address: 5055 W. Ray Rd Suite 2 Chandler, AZ 85226
properties.blurb: Great house beers? Check. Extensive tap list? Check. A variety of varietal wines and more? Check. From local residents and businessfolk to hop heads and cork dorks, HOP Central’s upscale taproom is the place to be. So pull on your fancy pants, put on your drinking shoes, and ready your taste buds for a pint of their House IPA. You’ll be glad you did.
properties.name: Hop Central Brewery

properties.blurb: It all starts in Belleville. Housed in a historic 1917 garage and fueled by hops grown just down the road, this is where Hop Garden Brewing comes to life. Grab a pint of their unpasteurized, small-batch beer, which is the way the Beer Gods intended, and settle into the cozy taproom or spacious beer garden. The beer lineup rotates with the seasons, giving you an excuse to come back again... and again. Trust us, you’ll want to.
properties.name: Hop Garden Brewing (Belleville)

properties.address: 18 E Main St, Evansville, WI 53536, USA
properties.blurb: Next stop: Evansville. Same great beer, same small-batch approach, just with a little more barbecue smoke in the air. The taproom brings all the easygoing charm you’d expect from Hop Garden, and their BBQ menu takes things to the next level. Bring your appetite and your curiosity. Between the seasonal beers and the smoked eats, you’re in for a proper flavor adventure.
properties.name: Hop Garden Brewing (Evansville)

properties.blurb: Round out your Hop Garden tour with a trip to Paoli, where the Sugar River provides the soundtrack and the farm-to-pint vibes are strong. It’s the kind of place where time slows down, live music drifts through the air, and even your most Type-A friend forgets to check their email. Grab a beer, snag a picnic table, and let the good times do their thing. You’ve earned it.
properties.name: Hop Garden Brewing (Paoli)

properties.blurb: You can use this page at either Hop Haus location, but we recommend you try them both. How else will you try everything? From curds to tacos — pizzas to a buttermilk fried chicken sando — the menus are different but equally packed with great bites to complement a lineup of stellar beers. So get down to Fitchburg. Or Verona. Or both!
properties.name: Hop Haus Brewing (Fitchburg)

properties.blurb: You’re here for the beer, and Hop Haus does not disappoint. First off was the Magic Dragon, a walloping 8.2% abv DIPA that filled our soul with hoppy citrus. Next, we danced with Let’s Fest Oktoberfest, a clean amber that afforded us a daydream to Munich. Hop Haus has year-round standbys and then mixes in rotating seasonals so subsequent visits always provide some surprises. They also have a sundrenched patio in case you’d like to soak in the Verona weather along with your sudsy, delicious brews.
properties.name: Hop Haus Brewing (Verona)

properties.blurb: Get ready to embark on a hop-tastic journey filled with flavor and fun. The Plaid Partner Scotch Ale is a brew that's, like us, as comfortable in the woods as it is dressed for an urban encounter. It's a sweet and smooth concoction with malty overtones that will tempt, seduce, and leave you wanting more. But why settle for just one beer when you can have a whole flight of four 5oz pours? Indulge in the hoppy goodness and explore a rotating selection of brews that will tickle your taste buds and keep you coming back for more.
properties.name: Hop Haus Brewing Company

properties.address: 3216 Judson St, Gig Harbor, WA 98335, USA
properties.name: Hop Pharm

properties.blurb: Home to Tampa’s first and only custom seltzer bar, we’re flipping the script on how seltzer is done. While most spots offer one or two taps, we hand you a crisp 4% ABV base and let you build your own flavor adventure — mix, match, and make it yours. Innovation drives everything we brew, from dessert-inspired Berliner Weisse to barrel-aged stouts layered with unexpected adjuncts. Bold, creative, and always a little surprising — our beers and seltzers are made to keep you coming back for the next pour.
properties.name: Hop State Brewing

properties.blurb: Self. Serve. Craft. Beer. Four words that, when joined, bring a smile to our faces and water to our mouths. Hopportunities is a beer lover’s Valhalla. Make Friends, Meet Friends, and Beer Friends. That’s all they ask of you. All we ask is that you venture out a bit and try a few pours you wouldn't normally order. Because here? Good times are measured in ounces and there's plenty to go around!
properties.name: Hopportunities

properties.address: 9579 S University Blvd lot 3b, Highlands Ranch, CO 80126, USA
properties.name: Hops n Drops

properties.address: 22 E Birch Ave #2, Flagstaff, AZ 86001, USA
properties.blurb: At Hops on Birch, variety is the name of the game. With 31 rotating taps and up to 100 craft beers and beverages available in cans, you're guaranteed to find something new each time. It’s more than just a taproom though, with real dartboards, live music, and events keeping the atmosphere lively. Whether you're a die-hard beer enthusiast or just looking for a fun hangout spot, Hops on Birch has you covered.
properties.name: Hops On Birch

properties.blurb: HopScotch Café calls their beer “happy juice,” and to be honest, we can’t think of a better description. With 10 rotating drafts and 100 cans in the cooler, ordering your drink feels almost as high-stakes as deciding who you’re going to marry. The good news? It’s pretty damn hard to mess up. They also sell 6-packs that drinkers can customize themselves. It’s every DIYer’s dream.
properties.name: HopScotch Cafe

properties.address: 9201 Cockleshell Dr Unit 13, Bonita Springs, FL 34135
properties.blurb: At Hopsized Brewing Co, they've mastered the art of crafting high-quality brews with a touch of magic. They release newly curated beers every Thursday, making each visit an adventure for your taste buds From traditional lagers to Nebulous IPA and APAs, from stouts to Kölsch's and fruited sours, their diverse selection caters to every beer lover's palate. Step into their warm and inviting third space, and you'll find the perfect blend of art and science, where passion and precision come together.
properties.name: Hopsized Brewing Co

properties.address: 4925 W Bell Rd c9, Glendale, AZ 85308, USA
properties.blurb: At Hopsology, beer isn’t just a drink, it’s a study. With 21 taps dedicated to showcasing the best of Arizona’s craft scene (and a few guest stars from beyond), there’s always something new to discover with every pour. The lineup also includes wine, ciders, seltzers, and even non-alcoholic options, so everyone can join in on the fun. Think of it as a crash course in great taste, one glass at a time.
properties.name: Hopsology Taproom and Wine Bar

properties.address: 100 Anastasia Blvd, St. Augustine, FL 32080, USA
properties.blurb: Hornski’s is your one-stop sip shop. Lagers, IPAs, cider, wine, seltzers and even kombucha. They’ve got a pour for every mood. Vinyl keeps the vibe spinning, the arcade keeps the scores rising, and food trucks keep the cravings in check. Come for the taps, stay for the tunes… and maybe beat your high score while you’re at it.
properties.name: Hornski's Brewing Co

properties.blurb: House Divided Brewery doesn’t just have playfully-named beers like Bat Out of Helles Bock, Tide is High Blonde Ale, and Drunken Sailor Irish Stout. It also offers canned seltzers, ciders, and bottles of wine for the “can we please go somewhere other than a brewery” drinkers. And despite its name, this Ely-based brewery is a house united when it comes to live music, cold beers, and their lounge-worthy patio.
properties.name: House Divided Brewery

properties.address: 1885 Elk River Rd, Steamboat Springs, CO 80487
properties.blurb: A love for fresh and natural ingredients meets a passion for crafting delicious pizzas come together at How Ya Doin Pizza n’ Eatz.! This quirky joint takes pride in serving top-notch food and creating a welcoming environment for all customers. With a strong focus on using natural and non-GMO ingredients, their extensive menu offers a delightful range of options. From delectable pizzas and mouthwatering sandwiches to refreshing salads and finger-licking appetizers, there's something to satisfy every taste bud, young or old. Whether you choose to sit at the lively bar or enjoy the laid-back patio setting, they'll find themselves surrounded by good vibes and great flavors. And don't forget to pair that perfect slice with a cold beer, available on tap, in bottles, or cans!
properties.name: How Ya Doin Pizza n' Eatz

properties.blurb: "We’re big fans of funky mountain town breweries, and this Rollinsville gem is one of the good ones. Perfect for a pit stop to or from Eldora Mountain Resort, swing by for jaw-dropping views, laid back vibes, a dog-friendly taproom, string band music on weekends—oh, and the beer ain’t nothing to sneeze at either. If you’re into Belgian-inspired offerings, you’re in luck—the affable couple behind Howlin Wind specialize in this sacred tradition. Start with a bright, fruity and eminently sessionable Dawn’s Early Light Saison before graduating to a splendidly dry-hopped Atop the Perch Farmhouse Ale. The more hop-forward crowd will enjoy an Everything’s Round Juicy Pale Ale, or Big Rock Park Hazy Terpene IPA with their Rocky Mountain High vistas. Kombucha is also on offer for those not into barley pops, and the brewery shares a space with a most excellent coffee shop. "
properties.name: Howlin Wind Brewing

properties.blurb: When we tell you Hubbleton Brewing Co. believes in handcrafted, we aren’t just talking about the beer. They also renovated the building, which they’ve had to expand twice as the brewery gained popularity. The current taproom has an indoor and outdoor beer garden, bar games, live music, and of course, great beer. Try the Crawfish River to see why it’s their #1 seller.
properties.name: Hubbleton Brewing Co

properties.address: 225 E Monroe St, Phoenix, AZ 85004, USA
properties.name: Huss Brewing (Monroe)

properties.blurb: From humble beginnings with a husband, wife, and an F-150 to becoming Arizona’s third-largest brewery, Huss has earned every bit of its reputation. Sure, you can grab a Papa’s Orange Blossom in just about any grocery store in the state, but the Uptown taproom is where the magic really happens. Between their lineup of crowd favorites and a food menu that’s basically begging you to order a giant pretzel with Cactus Valley Beer Cheese Fondue, this is the kind of place where “quick beer” turns into a full-on evening.
properties.name: Huss Brewing Downtown Brew Pub

properties.address: 1520 W Mineral Rd Suite 102, Tempe, AZ 85283
properties.blurb: This cozy taproom offers a wide selection of beers, from the Koffee Kolsch to the refreshing Orange Blossom, even sneaking in a few popular nationwide taps just to keep the locals on their toes. Huss Brewing Co. offers some of THE best beers in all of Arizona, so if you pop in while you’re roadtripping, be sure to snag a six pack or a case of your favorite to smuggle across the border when you head home. Just remember that beer is considered precious cargo in all fifty states.
properties.name: Huss Tempe Taproom

properties.blurb: What started as a small operation with a husband/wife team and a F 150 truck has grown into the 3rd largest brewery in Arizona and after stopping by for a few beers, we know why. You can buy a Papa Orange Blossom in stores all over the state but then you would miss their expansive food menu. The brats at this location will keep us coming back. The Upt Sonoran Dog is bacon-wrapped. Need we say more? No, but we will. Pinto beans, pico, lime crema AND cotija cheese. Yes please.
properties.name: Huss Uptown Taproom and Kitchen

properties.blurb: Jeffery Paul Hyder decided to honor his great grandfather, Andrew Jackson Macgillvery, by opening a brewery. Macgillvery made the “best whisky in the lowest valley of the highlands,” and so Hyder carries on his legacy with a “wee taste of the highlands” at Hyderhead Brewing. Nestled in the heart of historic downtown DeLand, Hyderhead has a selection of Craft Scottish Brews, from the Bonnie B-Lass Smokie Pale Ale to the Maide Leise to the Weight Over the Bar, as well as a Scottish-inspired menu with Braunschweiger Liverwurst, Haggis and more.
properties.name: Hyderhead Brewing

properties.address: 12920 Lowell Blvd Unit G, Broomfield, CO 80020
properties.name: I Hop IPA

properties.blurb: Locally crafted burgers, beer, and a whole lot more await you! This neighborhood gathering place is all about good people, great times, and unforgettable experiences. At I.C. Brewhouse, they take pride in supporting the local community, and that includes serving up a selection of fantastic local brews always available on tap. But that's not all – their carefully curated cocktail menu offers a variety of delightful concoctions to tickle your taste buds. Sports enthusiasts will feel right at home here, as I.C. Brewhouse boasts multiple TVs, perfect for catching your favorite team in action. For those seeking some fresh air and a furry companion, the pet-friendly outdoor patio is the place to be.
properties.name: I.C. Brewhouse (Castle Rock)

properties.blurb: Cocktail menu: check. Pet-friendly: check. Beer: check, check, check. I.C. Brewhouse is everything you could want out of a brewery, with a full cocktail menu, pet-friendly patio, and 16 local beer taps. Honorable mentions: 17 sprawling TVs, a daily happy hour, and a signature brew: the Block Party Cranberry Wheat beer.
properties.name: I.C. Brewhouse (Centennial)

properties.address: 845 Serenoa Rd, Santa Rosa Beach, FL 32459
properties.blurb: Looking for a brewery that's always pushing the boundaries of their craft? Look no further than Idyll Hounds Brewing Co. With a commitment to sustainability, tradition, and a pioneering spirit, this brewery is always exploring new territory and rediscovering old favorites in the world of beer. And the proof is in the pint glass - try their Ghost Crab Pilsna' for a crisp and refreshing take on a classic style, or dive into the hazy goodness of their Boosh New England Style India Pale Ale. At Idyll Hounds, adventure, creativity, and great taste go hand in hand.
properties.name: Idyll Hounds Brewing Co

properties.address: 2200 1st Ave S, St. Petersburg, FL 33712, USA
properties.blurb: If I Brewed the World is on a mission to brew its way into your heart, one irresistible beer at a time. It's all part of the grand master plan, concocted with love and creativity. This small batch brewery is your ticket to a world of fun and eclectic flavor profiles that'll tantalize your taste buds in ways you never thought possible. Think of If I Brewed the World as the mixtape of breweries. Every hour spent brewing, every successful experiment, and every delightful oddity comes together in a harmonious brew list that caters to every mood and palate. The beer isn't just a drink; it's a canvas for artistic expression. Instead of selling you paint, If I Brewed the World wants to be your art gallery, where each sip tells a unique story. So, let your taste buds embark on an unforgettable journey through a world of creative brewing!
properties.name: If I Brewed the World

properties.address: 3338 Land O' Lakes Blvd, Land O' Lakes, FL 34639, USA
properties.blurb: There’s nothing worse than being out of the loop—especially when it comes to In The Loop Brewing, here cold beers and stunning lake views are always on tap. The relaxed yet inviting atmosphere makes it easy to settle in and stay a while. Grab a spot on their incredible patio for the ultimate hangout, sip on some top-tier brews, and enjoy the laid-back vibes. It's the kind of place that keeps you coming back, just to make sure you never miss out.
properties.name: In the Loop Brewing

properties.blurb: A decade of glory in Minneapolis has paved the way for this remarkable 414 outpost. They're celebrating all that put them on the map in MN while venturing into the future with a pilot system that showcases their creative spirit. Not sure where to start? Fear not, for the Hello, Milwaukee brew is the perfect liquid welcome mat for thirsty PubPassers like us! Indeed has arrived in Milwaukee, and we're absolutely here for it. Raise your glasses to the exciting journey ahead you will not be disappointed.
properties.name: Indeed Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Three words for you: Pistacho. Cream. Ale. We brag about this beer everywhere we go. Lucky for you? It's a staple right here in Nordeast. Whether you belly up inside or grab a table near the tracks? You'll feel welcome at this ever-growing-but-always-original taproom. If you have friends in Cream City? Tell them Indeed's landed there now, too. If you don't know what Cream City is? That's okay. Just order another Cream Ale and smile, knowing you've got one of the best midwest breweries in your backyard.
properties.name: Indeed Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Independence Brewing has been fueling Austin’s free spirits and craft beer lovers since 2004. Bold beers with Texas-sized personality are their specialty — and like their grackle mascot, they’re loud, proud, and a little rebellious. From hop-heavy favorites to smooth classics and a few curveballs along the way, this brewery’s all about great beer, good company, and embracing your inner black sheep.
properties.name: Independence Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Beer and barbeque belong together. Nothing feels more natural than stepping into Industry for one of 65 local craft beers on tap (the largest selection in Naples) and tender pulled pork shoulder. All the meat is authentic and smoked daily, and if beef and barbeque weren’t enough, the walls are also covered with local art. Shop artists like Christopher Sweeney and Shelby Finger while you eat. Just make sure you don’t touch: we see that barbeque sauce on your fingers.
properties.name: Industry Beer & Barbeque

properties.blurb: At Infamous Brewing, every pint tells a story. Whether you’re swapping tales with a stranger or just unwinding after a long day. With a lineup of tried-and-true staples alongside rotating brews that keep things fresh, there’s always something worth raising a glass to. Plus, their tasty eats make sure no one’s drinking on an empty stomach. Good beer, good food, and good company. Now that’s the kind of infamy we can get behind.
properties.name: Infamous Brewing Company

properties.blurb: A poker player, country singer, and tech bro walk into a bar. This could be the start of a really excellent joke, or just your average night at Inga’s. This quirky neighborhood pub has Bacardi and Bitcoin trivia, Monday night poker, Saturday night karaoke, and specialty cocktails to make it all go down smooth. And if you need a post-singing/poker/trivia snack, you’re in luck: the kitchen is open late.
properties.name: Inga's Alpine Tavern

properties.blurb: Already one of our favorite taprooms in MSP, Insight went and topped themselves by putting in a massive outdoor bar that's perfect for your next session. What are you drinking at that bar? We love Avant - a dry-hopped Pilsner that drinks like a dream. Or explore Insight's menu of 'House beers' - Light, Pale & Dark. If seasonals are more your jam? Keep an eye on their small batch system for ever-changing suds to complement your stay on a patio worth a return visit or two.
properties.name: Insight Brewing

properties.address: 2160 W Drake Rd A1, Fort Collins, CO 80526, USA
properties.blurb: At Intersect Brewing, crossing paths feels less like chance and more like a guarantee. You’ll be back again and again. Independently and family-owned, this is where craft beer, good food, and pinball all come together in one welcoming space. It’s the kind of intersection you don’t just pass through. You stick around for another round. ** Deal good on in house core beers **
properties.name: Intersect Brewing

properties.blurb: Always trust your gut. Your hunch that Northeast Florida’s largest craft brewery kicks out some of the finest — and most creatively varied — barely pops in the Sunshine State is spot on, Sherlock. Kick it up on a stylish rooftop bar with an El Guapo Mexican-style Lager or Easy on the Eyes Session IPA to compliment that sweet FLA heat. Or treat yoself to a Cocoa Caliente Milk Stout (brewed with local Datil Peppers, Cocoa Nibs, Cinnamon and Vanilla Beans) in a spacious modern taproom. Everything about this sprawling, red-brick facility is first-class. Take a tour of the impressive production facilities, or plan your trip around one of Intuition’s many live concerts!
properties.name: Intuition Ale Works

properties.blurb: We need to talk about James Brown. No, not the godfather of soul (though we could go on for hours about the drum and bass groove on Cold Sweat). We need to talk about James Brown Ale — the malty, pecan-packed and maple syrup-kissed wonderment being poured at Invictus. It’s dessert in a glass. More of a hophead? Hazy Crazy IPAzy will get you where you need to be. Or perhaps you just want to session a Kölsch or three. Whatever you try, you might just decide that papa’s got a brand new brewery…
properties.name: Invictus Brewing

properties.blurb: Iowa Distilling Co. honors the “Iowa” in their name. They buy corn from Iowan farmers. They were one of the first establishments in Iowa to get a native distilling license. And they’ve been voted the best local Iowan distillery by CityView for four years running. Speaking of awards, their Front of House manager has also been awarded Best Local Bartender – so expect some of that local Iowan charm when you stop in.
properties.name: Iowa Distilling Co

properties.blurb: Why visit just ONE brewery when you can visit ALL of Wisconsons best in one place? From a Ned Flanders by Titletown Brewing Co to an IceHenge Belgian-Style Dubbel from Tyraneana Brewing Co and everything in between. The best part? It’s self-serve so you can try as many brews as you want before committing. Come on the right night and you will be serenaded by some of the best local musicians. Come on any night and order the yummy pretzel bites. Thank us later.
properties.name: Iron Hop Taphouse

properties.address: 508 N Main St, Grain Valley, MO 64029
properties.blurb: Iron Kettle Brewing brings a touch of Europe’s old-world charm right to Grain Valley, Missouri with no passport or jet lag required. With a rotating lineup of guest taps and a solid selection of house favorites, there’s always a brew to keep your taste buds entertained. They partner with local farmers and suppliers to keep ingredients fresh and the community thriving. Every day, Iron Kettle serves up craft beer, friendly service, and good vibes in a space that feels like it was made just for hanging out.
properties.name: Iron Kettle Taphouse

properties.address: 514 Perry St c106, Castle Rock, CO 80104
properties.blurb: Iron Mule Brewing seamlessly combines old and new techniques to craft globally inspired, locally brewed beers. With a diverse lineup of  beers, from hop-forward ales to rich stouts, they deliver flavors that excite and satisfy. Experience the perfect blend of tradition and innovation at Iron Mule Brewing. Cheers to exceptional beer!
properties.name: Iron Mule Brewing

properties.blurb: Step up your drinking game at Ironton Distillery and Crafthouse! Our BOGO deal here is for their draft beers or draft cocktails, which are always rotating to include new and exciting flavors. When the weather is beautiful, the best patio in Denver is perfect for sipping on a drink or two with friends. It is complete with cozy fire pits to keep you warm in the evenings. If the weather doesn't cooperate, the tasting room is just as cozy and spacious. No matter when you visit we can guarantee a good time with outstanding drinks.Idyll Hounds Brewing Co
properties.name: Ironton Distillery and Craft House

properties.blurb: Irwin Brewing Co doesn't mess around when it comes to brewing greatness. With their time-tested craft beer techniques and a touch of mountain wizardry, they've conjured up award-winning ales and lagers that'll leave you spellbound. From snowcat dreams to liquid reality, this brewery knows how to turn pristine snowmelt into pure liquid gold. Whether you're carving up the slopes or chilling with friends, a sip of their brews will have you dancing with delight. But it's not just about the beer—it's a celebration of adventure, camaraderie, and the wild spirit of Colorado's last great ski town.
properties.name: Irwin Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Picture this: a charming coastal byway leading straight to the doorstep of a remarkable brewery. Indeed, it's not just a whimsical notion; it's the reality offered by Islamorada Brewery & Distillery, beckoning all adventure-seekers. Enter Islamorada Brewery & Distillery, the embodiment of an exquisite blend of flavors. It's not just a brewery—it's also a distillery, offering an array of libations to satisfy every palate. With a draft list spanning a spectrum of beers and cocktails, from the timeless classics to novel small-batch creations, there's no shortage of options to tantalize taste buds.
properties.name: Islamorada Brewery & Distillery

properties.address: 3200 St Lucie Blvd, Fort Pierce, FL 34946
properties.blurb: For the perfect Island State of Mind™ experience, ride the next big wave up to these shores. With every sip you take, you’ll find your worries slipping away. This location houses their production facility, so take a tour of the process, and hey, do us a favor - while you’re on the tour, try and figure out how they get their Channel Marker to taste like you’re always on vacation.
properties.name: Islamorada Brewery & Distillery (Fort Pierce)

properties.address: 2294 Mayport Rd, Atlantic Beach, FL 32233, USA
properties.blurb: Island Girl Taproom is where island time meets top-shelf pours. Craft beer, premium spirits, and a walk-in humidor give you plenty of ways to unwind. Whether you’re savoring a smoky cigar, sampling a rotating craft beer, or nursing a perfectly mixed cocktail, the laid-back vibe makes it easy to forget the clock. Think of it as your mini-vacation without the packing or the plane ride.
properties.name: Island Girl Mayport Taproom

properties.blurb: Let’s get one thing straight: you might come to an island to get away from it all, but “all” doesn’t include pizza. Voted the #1 pizza on Sanibel Island for twenty years running, Island Pizza Company clearly knows what they’re doing. Try a classic Hawaiian pizza, or feast on their weekly smoked BBQ special. Pair with one of the craft beers on tap for your own little slice of paradise.
properties.name: Island Pizza

properties.blurb: We love the simple things in life: fresh-cut grass, sunsets, and great beer made with the best hops, barley, and malts. The results? Top-notch craft brews that somehow manage to be both approachable and adventurous. Light and zesty Belgian Wits, playful sours, or layered, bold ales. You’ll find a lineup that’s anything but simple. Get ready for a seriously satisfying sip at Ivanhoe Park Brewing.
properties.name: Ivanhoe Park Brewing Co

properties.address: 23 N Orange Blossom Trl, Orlando, FL 32805, USA
properties.blurb: Didn’t have a chance to try that one beer calling your name last time? It’s your lucky day. The Lager House takes the best of Ivanhoe Park Brewing and pours it into a space made for lingering. Crisp, refreshing lagers and a rotating lineup of craft favorites make it easy to find your next obsession. Swing by, grab a pint, and finally give that elusive brew the attention it deserves.
properties.name: Ivanhoe Park Lager House

properties.blurb: Spaghetti and craft beer don’t typically go together. But at J Moe’s, it just works. This craft brewery operated for six years before introducing Italian cuisine, and the rest was… well, delicious. J Moe’s Italian Bistro & Brewery combines generational family recipes with a steadfast love of craft beer for a surprising — and delightful — combination.
properties.name: J Moe's Italian Bistro & Brewery

properties.blurb: Step up to the plate at J. Leinenkugel's Barrel Yard, where beer and baseball unite for an epic experience! Situated overlooking left field of American Family Field, open year-round, this brewery is a grand slam. Get ready to savor pilot brews crafted on site by the master brewers at Leinenkugel's while cheering on your favorite team. But it's not just about the hops and homers—treat your taste buds to mouthwatering Wisconsin comfort food with a twist of Northwoods flavor. Whether you're a beer enthusiast or a die-hard baseball fan, J. Leinenkugel's Barrel Yard is a winning destination that hits it out of the park every time! ** VALID ON NON-GAME DAYS ONLY*
properties.name: J. Leinenkugel's Barrel Yard

properties.blurb: Hold onto your hops, because Jackalope Brewing Co is here to unleash a wild world of delicious beer! These brewing rebels know how to brew with a sustainable twist, putting the "eco" in brewski. Sip on their legendary lineup of year-round brews, like the mighty Sarka Pilsner, an unruly concoction that'll make your taste buds dance with joy. But the fun doesn't stop there! Jackalope Brewing Co keeps things fresh with a rotating lineup of seasonal brews that will keep you coming back for more. Get ready to hop on the flavor train and embark on a beer-tastic adventure with Jackalope Brewing Co.
properties.name: Jackalope Brewing Co

properties.blurb: “Jackass” may have started as a joke between family and friends, but now it’s the official name of a friendly brewpub overlooking the gorgeous South Platte River valley. And with a sprawling outdoor beer garden, 24 taps, and an assortment of whiskey, wine, and other spirits, there’s not a lot of jackassery to go around. Except, of course, on the t-shirts.
properties.name: Jackass Hill Brewery

properties.blurb: When a free beer takes you to a James Beard-nominated spot you know you're doing something right. Jackie has it all. From snacks like Fried Grits and Steak Tartare to unique morning twists like Brunch Fried Rice and one of the best damn Croque Madames we've come across anywhere. Whether a date night or a lazy Sunday morning, you'll love it here.
properties.name: Jackie

properties.blurb: PSA to all Brewer’s fans: if you’re looking for a place to pregame, this is it. Jackson’s offers shuttle service for all home games! And if you’re not a Brewer’s fan, you can still come for the build-your-own-burger, Reuben rolls, rotating beer taps, seltzer, and wine. But let’s face it, if you’re a Brewer’s fan, this is the pre-game pub for you.
properties.name: Jackson's Blue Ribbon Pub

properties.blurb: Welcome to the home of adventurous beer crafted in the heart of downtown Denver. Just steps away from Coors Field, Jagged Mountain is a perfect pregame spot. Nothing beats knocking back a Squishy Hazy DDH IPA and reminiscing the days of yore when The Rox won 21 out of 22 games and punched a ticket to the 2007 World Series. Now, excuse us while we order a second beer in an attempt to erase the 2007 World Series from memory.
properties.name: Jagged Mountain Craft Brewery

properties.blurb: JAK’s Brewing’s motto is “aggressive beer for aggressive beer drinkers.” That’s also a nod to the Air Force Aggressor program that helped inspire this brewery. Its logo is the red star, the symbol of the fallen Soviet Union that today is the unifying emblem of the Air Force Aggressor program. Aside from its Air Force ties, JAK’s Brewing also has a rotating food truck schedule from local options like Bite Me Gourmet Sausage and All Things Pita.
properties.name: JAKs Brewing (Colorado Springs)

properties.blurb: JAKs is a bold brewery whose beers aren’t for the faint of heart. Can you handle the hop? Try the JAKalope Double IPA if you’re ready to find out. If you’re a chocolate lover, take our advice and bet on the BlackJAK Porter. With food trucks, frequent events, and a dog-friendly taproom, even the shy ones will find something to love here.
properties.name: JAKs Brewing (Falcon)

properties.blurb: You go to Jams for dinner and open the menu. Then you do a double take, because there’s no way you just read the words “blue crab egg rolls,” “short rib grilled cheese,” and “lobster tacos.” Good news, friend: you aren’t dreaming. And guess what else? Jams has a huge patio, live music, and a full menu of beer. Yeah, we got emotional too. Here’s a tissue and a menu. Go live out your dream.
properties.name: Jams Old Market

properties.address: 2400 George Flagg Pkwy, Des Moines, IA 50321
properties.blurb: A flight of wine and a plate of cheese: ah, the simple things in life. Jasper Winery has been making their own wine for over 20 years. With plenty of seating indoors and out, it’s a great spot to bring friends or host an event. In summer, they put on a free outdoor concert series every Thursday: bring a chair or blanket and let them take care of the rest.
properties.name: Jasper Winery

properties.blurb: The fine folks behind Jax Craft Beer set out to build a blessed oasis, a sacred temple, a one-stop shop and hang spot with the goal of supplying the Mandarin neighborhood with every manner of bespoke suds offering imaginable. Mission accomplished. The vibe is corner store meets coffee shop, which is not a diss. We love the casual, communal atmosphere — all the better to talk barley pop with the supremely knowledgeable staff. The staggering selection of cans and bottles stacked high along the walls will have your eyes bugging out. Pull up a stool at the bar and do your best to choose from 32 rotating taps, most of which hail from nearby southeast US craft breweries. Tasty bites are also on offer, including Florida’s beloved 5-Star Pizza (IYKYK).
properties.name: Jax Craft Beer

properties.address: 131 1st Ave N Suite 1, Jacksonville Beach, FL 32250, USA
properties.blurb: Jekyll Brewing brings a splash of Jekyll Island, Georgia, straight to Jacksonville with its lineup of flavorful brews. Dive into the crisp and refreshing Big Creek Kölsch Style Ale, or pucker up with the Sour Thangs, a sour ale bursting with cranberry and orange zest. If you’re craving even more citrus, the Southern Juice IPA will hit the spot with its grapefruit kick and bright citrus notes. And with their rotating seasonal and limited releases, every visit promises a new and exciting adventure in flavor.
properties.name: Jekyll Brewing

properties.blurb: “JJ’s Place” sounds like the house of a good friend you’d hit up for an after-school, after-dinner, or after-work hang, and it’s a pretty fitting name. People head to JJ’s for live sports, for a bite to eat, for karaoke night, or for a poker game with their buddies. You can get 5 shots of Fireball for $20 at JJ’s, and $4 Root Beer Barrels. We know what you’re thinking. Typical JJ.
properties.name: JJ's Place

properties.blurb: The atmosphere at McGurk’s transports you straight to Dublin, with Irish musicians performing every night of the week, plus a generous selection of Ireland’s finest beers and whiskey. If drinking outdoors is your thing, the luck of the Irish has indeed found you. McGurk’s 15,000 square feet of space—complete with a waterfall and three outdoor bars—won Best Patio in St. Louis in 2021. Sláinte!
properties.name: John D. McGurk's

properties.address: 16905 Jowler Creek Rd, Platte City, MO 64079
properties.blurb: The wines here are as delightful as they are locally produced! Their award-winning creations are crafted with love and sold right here at the winery, available by the glass or bottle. If you get hungry they've got you covered with a mouthwatering assortment of gourmet cheeses, fresh-baked bread, locally-produced meats, and more. It's like a symphony of flavors, perfectly tuned to complement your wine tasting experience.The fun doesn't stop at the wines and food—Jowler Creek's tasting room is an experience in itself. Step into a world of charm and character, where the ambiance will whisk you away to a wine wonderland. And if you prefer the great outdoors, their beautifully-landscaped wine garden awaits you, nestled next to their sustainable vineyard.
properties.name: Jowler Creek Winery

properties.blurb: Joyride Brewing Company has been a go-to of ours for years now. Their beers are so good you’d almost think they’d come straight from the teat of a sacred golden calf god of beer. But we digress. Swing by on a Sunday to take in a Broncos game, enjoy a couple cold ones, and get ready to gobble up some killer food-truck food.
properties.name: Joyride Brewing

properties.blurb: Joyride Brewing Company has been a go-to of ours for years now. Their beers are so good you’d almost think they’d come straight from the teat of a sacred golden calf god of beer. But we digress. Swing by on a Sunday to take in a Broncos game, enjoy a couple cold ones, and get ready to gobble up some killer food-truck food.
properties.name: Joyride Brewing

properties.address: 3021 St Claude Ave, New Orleans, LA 70117
properties.blurb: Our session on the front porch at Junction started innocently enough. We were simply planning to house a few Lagers and some Tijuana Fries. Then our buddy Seth got a wild idea. “Guys. Let’s run it back with the fries, but this time? Let’s make ‘em Loaded Fries.” Minds were blown. More beers were ordered. Then burgers — try the KCS or AWRR — then some wings and before we knew it? The friendly staff was explaining to our group that, while we were welcome to continue our ‘Flavor Odyssey’ at home, they were indeed closed for the evening. We’ll be back tomorrow.
properties.name: Junction

properties.blurb: Gather around fellow mortals, for we have great gifts to bestow unto you from the majestic beer gods. Offering two core brews year-round - a delightful light lager or a citrus forward IPA - their rotating specialty beers are truly delectable. Add a plate of their New York Strip or a bowl of Beer Mac & Cheese for dinner and you’ll have a feast of ambrosia before you.
properties.name: Kalona Brewing Co

properties.address: 6922 W Orchard St, West Allis, WI 53214
properties.blurb: This lively spot is all about making memories with live music, open jams, and karaoke nights that'll have you singing your heart out! Fridays are extra special with their legendary all-you-can-eat fish and chicken fry. It's a feast you won't want to miss! But the fun doesn't end there—Kane's is always serving up something exciting. From wing nights that'll make your taste buds dance to Mexican Monday and Taco Tuesday for a fiesta of flavors, they've got it all covered. But Kane's isn't just about the events—it's the chill vibe and great food that keep people coming back for more. Whether you're grabbing a bite with friends or enjoying a night out, Kane's Bar and Grill knows how to show you a good time.
properties.name: Kane's Bar and Grill

properties.blurb: We were sold on this place the second we heard about “Woofstock” — an annual dog-friendly music festival that benefits the Jacksonville Humane Society. (Seriously, how cool is that?) Northeast Florida’s first private indoor/outdoor dog park boasts 7,000 square feet of indoor, climate-controlled play area, plus an additional 7,000 square feet of outdoor space for your furry friend(s) to frolic about. There’s also a grooming service, doggy daycare, boarding options — even an on-site veterinarian! For human types needing a little hair of the dog, there’s a hip bar area with 15 local and regional craft beers on tap (also kombucha and nitro cold brew coffee), as well as “ruff-ly” 50 excellent options for canned beer and seltzers — “woof dah!”
properties.name: Kanine Social

properties.address: 310 W 79th St, Kansas City, MO 64114, USA
properties.blurb: At KC Bier Co, they’ve put the "I" back in "Bier," serving up German-style beers that are as authentic as they are refreshing. With malts sourced from a 160-year-old Bavarian maltster and imported German hops, these brews pack all the rich flavors of traditional German craftsmanship with a Kansas City twist. Lederhosen might not be required, but with bier this good, we wouldn’t be surprised if they catch on. YOUR PUBPASS IS VALID ON .5L BIERS ONLY
properties.name: Kansas City Bier Co

properties.blurb: We could tell you to get to Karben 4’s taproom because their Crab Cakes and Steak Nachos are both fantastic with a cold Dragon Flute APA. We could also tell you that the K4 Beer Burger and a cold Lady Luck changed our lives. We could tell you those things. But really? You just need a new Fantasy Factory shirt. Your old one is pretty worn out. Oh - and can you grab Steve a new K4 hat? He’ll get ya back.
properties.name: Karben4

properties.blurb: The rad dudes behind Karben4 always know what time it is: Happy Silly Time. At least, that’s the name of one of their Lime Gose beers. (The full name, technically, is Happy Silly Time: It’s Noon O’Clock Somewhere). Like most Wisconsinites know, peak patio season is all too short. So you’re gonna want to stretch that season out and chill on this patio with your Threat Level Midnight (S’mores Stout) or Hawk Jones IPA well past summer.
properties.name: Karben4 Brewing

properties.blurb: The appeal of Keel Farms is two-fold: their winery and their agrarian ale & cider. Tour both any weekend for a trolley ride and behind-the-scenes experience. And if that trolley ride makes you wish there were a modern or traditional beer or glass of wine in your hand, all the better.
properties.name: Keel Farms

properties.address: 2501 Bienville Blvd, Ocean Springs, MS 39564, USA
properties.name: Keg & Barrel (Ocean Springs)

properties.blurb: The self-appointed “Valhalla of Nostalgia,” Jacksonville’s first ever arcade bar lives up to its lofty moniker by transporting you into a masterfully-curated, neon-filled shrine to 80s and 90s pop culture. Classic video games abound, from Rampage to NBA Jam to Mortal Kombat. Foosball, skee-ball, a choice selection of ever-rotating pinball machines — it’s all here. They even sell packs of Garbage Pail Kids trading cards and play retro cartoons on Saturday mornings! Did we mention beer yet? Choose from 15 rotating taps and a staggering selection of bottles and cans that span the entire country’s finest purveyors of craft suds. Check their website and socials for special events galore.
properties.name: Keg & Coin

properties.blurb: We’d expect nothing less than a warm, kind, friendly atmosphere from an Iowa brewery. Lots of places in Iowa are just a little bit out of the way, but if you’re willing to drive just a bit, you won’t regret it. Be careful, though, you might think you're swinging by just for the staple Midwest Haze but then you find yourself accidentally joining a cornhole league with your buddies. Don’t say we didn’t warn you.
properties.name: Keg Creek Brewing

properties.blurb: A sports bar standard bearer for over 35 years, Kelly’s will not only show the game on one of their many tvs, they even offer a free shuttle back and forth to the Brewers games for FREE. You can also settle in with a Cheese Curd Burger and microbrew for music bingo and their recurring beer pong tournaments. Friendly vibes, a menu that would take a year to completely work through, and the warm touches of an Irish pub all point to thoughtful curation by the owners
properties.name: Kelly's Bleachers

properties.blurb: Kieran's is a perfect spot for a few patio pints before or after a ballgame. Or, come here in the dead of winter and snuggle up to a Big Ginger in a cozy nook.
properties.name: Kieran's Irish Pub

properties.address: 355 Iris Avenue, Jefferson, LA 70121 Suite A
properties.blurb: Ever held an apple in your hand and marveled at the ability of people much smarter than you to turn it into hard cider? Get ready to start feeling the same way about pears, my friend... Kingfish makes hard cider and Perry... a fun way to pear-derived alcohol. We know, it's new and you've always been slow to adopt trends (we weren't going to mention those shoes). That's ok. Grab a Prohibition (dry Perry) and you'll start to understand.
properties.name: Kingfish Cider

properties.blurb: Kinkaider’s newest taproom in Omaha has the vibe of a neighborhood bar crossed with a cozy cocktail lounge. That’s pretty appropriate though, because in addition to their many beers, they also serve craft cocktails and cider courtesy of their side hustle, Sideshow Spirits. Regular bingo and trivia nights make this a must-visit spot.
properties.name: Kinkaider Brewing

properties.blurb: As a locally owned and operated micro-brewery deeply rooted in Rochester, they've mastered the art of crafting lip-smacking beers and tantalizing hard seltzers. The Taproom is a haven of good vibes, where folks gather to savor the flavors and share laughter. Kinney Creek Brewery proudly presents a lineup of craft brews and hard seltzers that'll make taste buds dance the tango of delight! And oh, don't miss their claim to fame – the "Med City Seltzer" – boasting an impressive 25 flavors on tap that'll take your seltzer game to soaring heights! With each visit to Kinney Creek Brewery, you're in for a tail-wagging experience. Their fun-loving atmosphere and creative libations are a recipe for endless smiles. So gather your crew, unleash your adventurous spirit, and let the good times flow at Kinney Creek Brewery!
properties.name: Kinney Creek Brewery

properties.blurb: We’ve seen a lot of breweries, so take it from us: Kinship Brewing is one of the best-designed spaces around. The gorgeous outdoor space includes a half-acre dog park and pairs great with their Amber Lager and your four-legged friend. Or soak up the sun through the taproom’s giant windows while you drink an Old Gregg. Weekly events and an in-house kitchen make it obvious why this spot’s a favorite.
properties.name: Kinship Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Step into beer heaven at Kippy's Place, where the taps flow with liquid gold and the vibes are always electric. This vibrant joint is a mecca for beer lovers, offering an unrivaled selection of brews that will make your taste buds sing. Kippy's Place has the perfect pint to quench your thirst and ignite your senses. And let's not forget about the grub! Their menu is a gastronomic adventure, with irresistible pub fare that will make your mouth water. So gather your crew, raise your glasses, and get ready for a legendary experience at Kippy's Place! Cheers to good times and even better beer!
properties.name: Kippy's Place

properties.address: 3321 E Bell Rd Suite B-5, Phoenix, AZ 85032
properties.blurb: Kitsune is a young brewery in Phoenix, but it definitely holds its own with the elders in the community. The tap lineup is full of inventive and whimsical flavor combos that had us questioning everything we thought we knew about beer. Come for fan fave The Forager and stay for the laid back atmosphere. Or leave, that’s cool too, just take a four-pack of a refreshing IPA to-go and enjoy the spoils of Kitsune Brewing at home. Did you know Kitsune pairs well with pool season?
properties.name: Kitsune Brewing Co

properties.blurb: The folks at Knotted Root Brewing look at brewing beer the same way they look at life, a work in progress, constant evolution and crossing a bridge into the unknown. They push the boundaries with their brewing process. Can you say hoppy Gummy Bears? Where can we sign up? While they focus on those amazing hoppy beers they also love to experiment with those rustic, mixed-culture farmhouse ales so there is something for everyone. With their ever changing line up of brews we never know what we are in for, but we know it’s going to be amazing.
properties.name: Knotted Root Brewing

properties.address: 842 Market St, San Diego, CA 92101, USA
properties.blurb: Deal valid on Knotty Barrel beers
properties.name: Knotty Barrel

properties.address: 13782 E I25 Frontage Rd Unit B1, Longmont, CO 80504
properties.blurb: Grab your buddies and get in formation* at Knuckle Puck Brewing. These folks know their beer like Gordon Bombay knows a triple deke. The tap list is stacked with something for everyone, from hoppy IPAs to smooth stouts, plus a few fun surprises along the way. Ducks fly together to happy hour at this cozy taproom. *Flying V highly encouraged, but all formations are welcome.
properties.name: Knuckle Puck Brewing

properties.blurb: Look no farther for your go-to tavern, this place is the only correct answer. With their killer steak dinner deal on Monday, the double stack of trivia AND poker night on Thursday, and karaoke on Sunday you’d just be a silly goose to go anywhere else. The menu offers classic tavern eats and drinks and the ambience offers classic settle in on your favorite barstool and get ready to chill vibes. Sundays are our favorites, so come enjoy us butcher our most favorite Celine Dìon songs.
properties.name: Knucklehead Tavern

properties.blurb: Nothing splashy here, just folks who take the freshest, highest quality ingredients and turn them into delicious craft beer and serve artisan pizzas from a wood-fired oven built with their own two hands. Okay, so maybe it’s a little splashy. Pair one of those pizzas with an Alexander Pilsner or the Classic Lager. Are we bummed we weren’t in town for the mac and cheese pizza of the week? Yes, yes we were. A little heads up next time, friends.
properties.name: Knuth Brewing Co

properties.blurb: A relative new kid on the block, Kodiac is a high ceiling and sparkling-clean beast in Centennial, more than worth the trip down Highway 25 from Denver. Their Hammond's Cane Red, Cosmic Reality IPA, and creatively delicious Mango Leotard Sour all punched our palette ticket in the best way. Couple that with beer battered cod and fries or the 12” brick oven pizzas,and brother, you got yourself a fulfilling evening in gorgeous Centennial.
properties.name: Kodiac Brewery

properties.address: 3027 E Harmony Rd Suite #2, Fort Collins, CO 80528, USA
properties.name: Konstruct Brewing

properties.address: 9030 Kenamar Dr #309, San Diego, CA 92121, USA
properties.name: Kove Brewing

properties.blurb: Act like a local: pronounce it coyote. You’ll fit right in. The indoor sand volleyball court is a surefire standout and know that your killer aces will serve as the backdrop for the live bands Kyote’s brings in frequently. Friendly barkeeps, flatscreens for the game, and even a couple of pinball machines wrapped around a sandwich and wing-centric menu make it a local favorite of Packer Country.
properties.name: Kyote's Bar & Grill

properties.blurb: So much to love about this spot — starting with a mission that puts people, planet and community first. As a registered For Benefit Corporation (ask them about it!) La Doña is harnessing the power of good beer for good. So what about the beers? They’re fantastic. We threw back a round of their flagship Lager before spicing things up with a Slow Burn and a Michelada.Stop by when there's soccer happening outside. It's the perfect way to spend an hour or two marveling at the athletic acumen of others while pondering a third beer.
properties.name: La Doña Cervecería

properties.blurb: We’re gonna skip right past the beer list (it’s good) and cocktails (they’re great) and talk about the empanadas you’ll be eating. As in all of the empanadas. From the Argentine Beef to the Meatball & Mozz to the Sriracha Mac & Cheese, there’s a hand pie for everybody at La Masa. Pair something hoppy with a Chicken Tinga or something dark with a Banana Nutella or just choose your own adventure.
properties.name: La Masa

properties.blurb: Are you the kind of person that looks at a menu days in advance so you have time to strategize a plan of attack? No? That’s just us? Seriously, everything on the menu at Labelle is mouthwatering, from the shrimp and grits to the pretzel with beer cheese. Might we suggest starting up a text thread with your pals to see what they are interested in ordering and go from there. Be sure to include friends who know how to share, you’re going to want a bite of everything.
properties.name: Labelle Brewing Co

properties.blurb: This small brick-and-mortar joint in the heart of the city was quick to impress us folks at PubPass. From kick-ass smash burgers to killer sandwiches and barley pops aplenty, this new kid on the block fits right in with the Des Moines food and beer scene. Grab a burger, guzzle a beer, and thank us later for giving you the inside info about Lachele’s Fine Foods.
properties.name: Lachele's Fine Foods

properties.address: 14141 Airline Hwy bldg 3 suite a, Baton Rouge, LA 70817, USA
properties.blurb: Laissez Versez Distillery is bringing Bourbon back to its roots, with a distinctly Louisiana twist. Committed to using local ingredients and pushing the boundaries of traditional aging, they’ve crafted a Bourbon rich in both history and flavor. Expect strong notes of oak and vanilla, a smooth hint of caramel, and a finish that lingers just right. Proudly rooted in the Bayou State, Laissez Versez is for those who appreciate quality, tradition, and a good time. So, when it’s time to raise a glass, let the good times pour with Laissez Versez Bourbon!
properties.name: Laissez Versez Distillery

properties.blurb: Escape any unseasonable temperature at the sunny-all-year-long Laka Lono Rum Club. This tiki bar and lounge turns Omaha into a tropical paradise. Expect drinks with gummy shark garnishes and orchid petals and an ambience so beachy you’ll be checking your bag for sunscreen. Try the Zombie, an enormous shareable drink that is quite literally… lit.
properties.name: Laka Lono Rum Club

properties.blurb: In the charming abode of Lake 11 Brewing, one discovers a harmonious blend of chill lake vibes and unbridled merriment, all woven together into a delightful brew-tastic experience! This brewery draws inspiration from the surrounding nature and the abundant recreational opportunities in the region. With utmost dedication, Lake 11 Brewing has masterfully crafted a collection of beers that guarantees a celebration of life's simple pleasures. The inviting ambiance encourages visitors to let go, savor each moment, and indulge in the flavors of their delightful concoctions.
properties.name: Lake 11 Brewing

properties.blurb: Imagine warm sunshine, a rope swing inviting you, and a crisp, refreshing brew in hand. That's the kind of experience you'll find at Lake Louie. Their beers are easy-drinking and crafted for those who know how to have a blast. It doesn’t matter if you choose an IPA, a pilsner, or a pale ale, you can bet it's going to be top-notch. They only use the finest ingredients, and the result? Well, it's a whole lot of flavor, an easy-drinking experience, and pure enjoyment in every sip.
properties.name: Lake Louie Brewing

properties.blurb: Lake Monster's surroundings are as unique as their beers. Pull up a barstool or pop a squat on the massive patio and enjoy a milk stout or three.
properties.name: Lake Monster Brewing

properties.blurb: We came to the northern reaches of Minnesota to ice fish for the elusive walleye. We spent days on end drowning minnows in an attempt to lure this torpedo-shaped fish up our holes and into our bellies. But all we caught were tullibee and a steady buzz. Finally, nearly defeated, we found our way to Lake of the Woods Brewing. Here we were greeted by warm smiles and cold beers—and limited out on stories about the one that got away.
properties.name: Lake of the Woods Brewing

properties.address: 1950 W Rose Garden Ln #130, Phoenix, AZ 85027
properties.blurb: To the folks at Lake Pleasant Brewery beer is about more than, well, beer. To them “it’s about how it draws people together from different walks of life and its ability to turn strangers into friends and friends into family.” And let us tell you - they are doing just that. Walking into their taproom you instantly feel welcomed with incredible beer and fun games all around. With a healthy mix of styles there is a beer here for everyone. We found ourselves feeling a little German and stuck with the Bavarian Hefeweizen and it did not disappoint. With food trucks almost every night of the week you will find us here, unwinding from a long work day often.
properties.name: Lake Pleasant Brewery

properties.blurb: At Lakefront, we recommend the Bumble Bear, a rich tasting Honey Brown Ale with ample fresh-baked biscuits, clover honey and notes of chocolatey, roasted malt. We also recommend the 45-minute brewery tour with optional adds of local cheese, meat, fruit, chocolate, and coffee.
