properties.blurb: Imagine this: you cruise in, greeted by the whir of laughter, clinking glasses, and the irresistible scent of freshly brewed creations. It's like finding that perfect gear that propels you forward with excitement. At Pedal Haus Brewery, they've mastered the art of crafting beers that are as adventurous as a bike ride down an unexplored trail. And just like a well-tuned bicycle, their menu is finely balanced. Savor burgers that handle like a dream, appetizers that kickstart conversations, and a whole vegan section of the menu. It's a culinary journey that pairs perfectly with their liquid masterpieces. So, if you're ready to hop on a flavor-packed adventure, pedal your way to Pedal Haus Brewery. It's more than a brewery – it's a two-wheeled haven where every sip and every laugh keeps you rolling back for more.
properties.name: Pedal Haus Brewery (Tempe)

properties.address: 2010 Sylvia Ave NE, Cedar Rapids, IA 52402
properties.blurb: Biking and beer, if done safely, can make for the best afternoon. Next time you have your wheels on the Cedar Valley Nature Bike Trail you’d be wrong to not stop at Pedalers Fork. With everything from all you can eat Wings on Saturday nights to Bingo on Tuesdays there is always something going on. Just don’t take too long trying to decide which of their 108 tap beers you want to try this visit.
properties.name: Pedalers Fork

properties.address: 6340 49th St N, Pinellas Park, FL 33781, USA
properties.blurb: Rev Up Your Good Times at Pee-Pa's Garage Craft Brewery! If you're on the hunt for a perfect blend of fun, camaraderie, family vibes, automotive, art, and exceptional beer, look no further than Pee-Pa's Garage Craft Brewery. This vibrant hub has its own collection of signature house beers ready to grace your taste buds. Additionally, their taps feature a rotating selection of guest brews from neighboring breweries, ensuring there's always something delightful on tap to suit your palate. Whether you're an auto aficionado, an art enthusiast, or simply seeking a fantastic time with friends and family, Pee-Pa's Garage Craft Brewery is your ultimate destination.
properties.name: Pee-Pa's Garage Craft Brewery

properties.blurb: Perennial flowers are ones that return every year. Perennial’s beer drinkers return way more frequently than that. That’s because at Perennial Artisan Ales they’ve been brewing up excellent beer for a decade now, and their tasting room is just the type of low-key place you want to spend an afternoon. Here you’ll find us daydreaming over their Belgin-style witbuer with coriander, pink pepercorns and orange peels. Best of all, unlike flowers, beer is always in season.
properties.name: Perennial Artisan Ales

properties.blurb: We stan any brewery that urges us to “get weird.” We’re all a bit weird, yeah? So why not embrace it and dive headfirst into one of Persimmon Hollow’s unique brews. They offer a fabulous lineup, from IPAs to porters to sours. There’s not a dud in the lineup, which is really saying something. The appetizer menu is full of bangers, too (yeah, we’re looking at you, Blue Cheese Chips). Mind your manners and don’t forget to share.
properties.name: Persimmon Hollow Brewing

properties.address: 4647 S Clyde Morris Blvd, Port Orange, FL 32129
properties.blurb: At Persimmon Hollow Brewing Co, the culinary journey is as diverse as a palette of craft brews. Picture this: a menu bursting with flavor like a fireworks display of tastes and aromas. From smash burgers that play a symphony on your taste buds to pizzas that redefine cheesy goodness, their food lineup is a gastronomic masterpiece. And now, let's talk about the liquid gold – the beer. Brace yourself for a tantalizing adventure through their carefully crafted brews. Ever heard of pairing a Beach Hippie IPA with a juicy burger? It's like a harmonious dance of hops and happiness. And when you're diving into a slice of pizza, the Daytona Dirty Blonde is your trusty sidekick, making each bite an explosion of flavors.
properties.name: Persimmon Hollow Brewing Co (Port Orange)

properties.address: 7508 W Greenfield Ave, West Allis, WI 53214
properties.blurb: Our middle school math teacher told us to 'get some perspective,' and we laughed, thinking, "Who needs math?" Well, it turns out, we do, in the form of directions to West Allis! We finally did get some Perspective, and it's way better than algebra, Mr. Carlson! At Perspective Brewing, the owners, former math teachers, now teach us about beer which is a lesson we actually enjoy. With a huge menu of brews, there's always a reason to come back and 'study' more. This is the stuff we want to be learning about.
properties.name: Perspective Brewing Co

properties.address: 2 E Pikes Peak Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80903, USA
properties.blurb: Phantom Canyon Brewing Co is where the art of brewing meets the mastery of culinary delights. Prepare yourself for a tantalizing experience as thoughtfully crafted brews and award-winning food await you. Here, they believe that great beer deserves great food, and every dish is carefully selected and created with passion and style. From delectable appetizers to mouthwatering salads and enticing entrees, their menu is a testament to the dedication and artistry that goes into every aspect of their culinary offerings. As for their beers, they are brewed with the same level of dedication and passion, resulting in a bold and diverse selection that sets them apart from other breweries. Get ready to embark on a flavor journey unlike anything you've experienced before at Phantom Canyon Brewing Co.
properties.name: Phantom Canyon Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Here the mantra is simple: the best products, the best value, and an explosion of flavor that will have you coming back for seconds. Hold onto your taste buds, because these guys are serious about quality. They're not just making burgers, they're crafting legends. Picture this: grass-fed beef transformed into the stuff of dreams, stacked between buns and topped with all the trimmings. And the soda? Pure cane sugar perfection that'll have your inner kid jumping for joy. But wait, there's more. Enter Music City's mightiest kitchen, where wursts (sausages) are not just cooked, they're crafted. These folks grind, stuff, and smoke their way to sausage heaven. The result? A wurst experience that'll have you singing their praises. Oh, and did we mention the craft beer? It flows here like a liquid treasure, ready to elevate your culinary experience to new heights.  **DEAL GOOD ON CORE BEERS **
properties.name: Pharmacy Burger and Beer Garden

properties.blurb: This local joint is where you want your night to end. Get your barhopping out of the way early and land at Philadelphia Sandwich Co for an ice cold brewski, a friendly game of pool, and a carb overload to cap off your night. Nothing makes us happier than rolling into an established local bar and sitting among friends while eating a giant meat sandwich. The rad dudes behind PSC know their hoagies AND their craft beer.
properties.name: Philadelphia Sandwich Co

properties.blurb: Can you even call yourself a brewery and taproom if you don’t serve a giant soft pretzel with beer cheese? Okay, well, the interwebs tells us that yes, you can, but luckily it’s a moot point here at PHX Beer Company. It’s game on when your pals say “winner gets the last bite” over a friendly game of cornhole. That last bite will pair well with an ice cold Arizona Gold. Losers can drown their tears in the Bird City Lager. So basically, everyone is a winner here.
properties.name: PHX Beer Co

properties.blurb: Over here in Scottsdale, the food might just steal the spotlight. Think wings, salads, hearty sandwiches, and those crispy black iron pizzas that hit the spot every time. Not that we’re keeping score (ahem), but after one bite, you’ll feel like a winner. And of course, the beer menu is locked and loaded with brews crafted with care—each sip is a victory of its own. So, whether you’re here for the food or the drinks, let’s call it another W for the books!
properties.name: PHX Beer Co (Scottsdale)

properties.address: 8300 N Hayden Rd, Scottsdale, AZ 85258, USA
properties.name: PHX Beer Co. Scottsdale Brewery & Restaurant

properties.address: 918 New York Ave, St Cloud, FL 34769, USA
properties.blurb: Looking for a place that’s as hot as it sounds? Phyre Saloon is bringing the heat (in the best way possible). Cold drinks, warm vibes, and a whole lot of fun are always on tap here. Whether you're down for a pint with your crew or just seeing where the night takes you, this is your go-to spot. Who needs fireworks when you've got Phyre? Come for the drinks, stay for the fire—because it’s always lit at Phyre Saloon!
properties.name: Phyre Saloon and Steakhouse

properties.address: 1130 S Paca St, Baltimore, MD 21230, USA
properties.blurb: Pickett Brewing Co. is your ticket to a world of diverse flavors and endless enjoyment. They're masters at crafting an array of styles, focusing on both American and European ales and lagers. But their creativity doesn't stop there. With an impressive range of beers, along with Mead and Kombucha, Pickett Brewing Co. ensures that every palate finds its perfect match. And the fun doesn't stop at the brews – get ready for trivia showdowns and live music that'll keep your spirits high. When hunger strikes, you're in for a treat, as some of the finest food trucks regularly swing by, ensuring you stay fueled for a day of fun and flavor. Pickett Brewing Co. is your one-stop destination for good times, great tastes, and unforgettable memories!
properties.name: Pickett Brewing Company

properties.address: 900 Rosa L Parks Blvd, Nashville, TN 37208
properties.blurb: ​​Nashville's craft breweries all gather here in one delightful spot. This is the place for those who crave local goodness and want to savor the best of Nashville's brewing scene all at once. Picnic Tap Farmers Market is your ticket to a craft beer extravaganza. Whether you're up for a couple of pints, a flight to sample the spectrum, or a growler to take the joy home, this lively hub has you covered. The taps here are like a merry-go-round of taste, always spinning with Nashville's finest: seasonal marvels, limited-time delights, and those well-loved classics that keep you coming back for more. Now, let's talk dogs – hot dogs, that is! These aren't your run-of-the-mill franks; we're talking top-notch, all-beef creations from the renowned Porter Road Butcher right here in Nashville. Hand-crafted with love, these dogs come in all shapes and sizes, ready to treat your taste buds to a carnivorous delight. And guess what? The buns are no ordinary buns; they're baked fresh by the masters at Provence Breads. Your hunger doesn't stand a chance!
properties.name: Picnic Tap Farmers Market

properties.blurb: Pig and Fiddle just gets it. Pair a couple of their many American crafts or Belgian stalwarts with some tasty pierogies and you'll get it, too.
properties.name: Pig & Fiddle

properties.blurb: Cream City has come a long way from its Schlitz, Pabst, and Miller High Life days. While we still appreciate a classic macro brew, Pilot Project is where the real excitement is. This place is like a beer playground for local brewers, offering 24 rotating taps that range from homebrew experiments to fresh takes from established breweries. With elevated local fare, brewery tours, workshops, and even rooftop yoga, it’s the perfect spot to kick back before hitting a Bucks game or just to explore the craft beer scene.
properties.name: Pilot Project Brewing

properties.blurb: What happens when a former homebrewer restores a historic body shop into a brewery and event space? Great things happen. Great things like Harold's St. Pete Style Lager, Milk Bone Sweet Stout, or any of their seasonal tastey treats. With over 35 taps, Pinellas Ale Works aka 'PAW' is sure you'll find a craft beer you'll love. Don't forget to bring the dog! because PAW is one of the most dog friendly breweries in St. Petersburg and 1st Runner Up for Best Small Brewery in Florida 2020!
properties.name: Pinellas Ale Works

properties.blurb: What happens when two former homebrewers combine their championship pedigrees and restore a historic body shop into a brewery? Great things happen. Great things like Harold’s Lager, Milk Bone Sweet Stout or the deceptively drinkable Grapefruit IPA. PAW even boasts a single barrel brewhouse dedicated to making beers you can only try at the taproom. The patio is one of our favorite spots on a gorgeous night.
properties.name: Pinellas Ale Works

properties.blurb: What happens when two former homebrewers combine their championship pedigrees and restore a historic body shop into a brewery? Great things happen. Great things like Harold’s Lager, Milk Bone Sweet Stout or the deceptively drinkable Grapefruit IPA. PAW even boasts a single barrel brewhouse dedicated to making beers you can only try at the taproom. The patio is one of our favorite spots on a gorgeous night.
properties.name: Pinellas Ale Works

properties.blurb: What’s better than a kickass pizzeria with an equally kickass brewery inside it? Nothing, duh. If you’ve made the trek down to Brewer’s Pizza / Pinglehead Brewing Company in Orange Park, congratulations — this is, in fact, the single raddest thing you could have chosen to do today. Don’t mess around now. Be like several thousands of fellow converts before you and order up a signature Florida Smacker (an eight-inch deep Detroit-style pie with chewy crust and golden brown, crunchy sides) and a pint of Imperial Red Ale (Pinglehead’s flagship for a damn good reason), and proceed to smash that happy meter into next week. We can say that because we’ve been in your position. A few times. This week.
properties.name: Pinglehead Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Webster’s says pinnacle means “the highest point of achievement.” Around here, it just means killer beer, good times, and a calendar so full of events you’ll wonder if they ever sleep. Pinnacle Brewing Co. has already snagged awards for their brews, opened their doors to pups of all shapes and sizes, and carved out a spot at the very top of the local beer scene. Call it coincidence if you want, but we’re pretty sure they’re living proof of their own name.
properties.name: Pinnacle Brewing Co

properties.blurb: If you’re looking for an ale house that serves more than greasy burgers and nachos, Pint Brothers is the spot for you. Nestled in the heart of Denver’s Tech Center in the Marriott, this brewery offers an expansive menu with breakfast options and unexpected bar food like crispy Brussel sprouts and bourbon pecan pie. Don’t worry, beer fiends: they also have an eclectic beer collection with rotating seasonal brews, tasting flights, and small-batch craft beers.
properties.name: Pint Brothers Ale House

properties.blurb: Pints is a good times bar where your inner party guy can peacefully coexist with the refined taste in craft beer you’ve been developing lately. A couple dozen craft lines greet your arrival, ready for you to grab something cold and complex to sip while you play shuffleboard with your other fancy beer friends who are busy reflecting on a time when they didn’t appreciate the finer points of hop varietals. You’re all so beerphisticated!
properties.name: Pints Bar Rooftop

properties.blurb: The Piper Inn prides itself on having the best wings in Denver. If you want to argue with a bar that’s been around for over 50 years, well, you’re probably just hangry. Quick—order the unbeatable Chinese Style wings, wash them down with a Tommyknocker Blood Orange IPA, and we’ll pretend your moment of doubt never happened.
properties.name: Piper Inn

properties.blurb: Pisco Sour Restaurant and Lounge is a true feast for the senses. A mouthwatering menu of Peruvian food and tons of patio seating means you can soak up the sunshine and pretend you’re on vacation. The ceviche and pollo a la brasa are menu standouts. And while we’d normally recommend a beer, we obviously gotta go with the pisco sour on this one. When in Peru, after all.
properties.name: Pisco Sour Restaurant

properties.blurb: You might be asking “What does Pivo mean?” Well, we are here to tell you. It means “beer” in 78 languages! So when we say this place knows beer, we MEAN this place knows beer. They specialize in Pilsner style beers and are always striving to be the best around. Are you more into IPAs? Not to worry, they have a strong line up of those as well. Did we mention the kick-ass brunch? Not yet? We’ll just say try the The Minnesota Hot Maple  Benedict. You’ll thank us later.
properties.name: Pivo Riverplace

properties.blurb: You’ll show up for a Tropical Snow Dance IPA, stick around for another round or two. Trust us, every brew’s a winner, you'll want to keep trying them. Then you’ll dive into the menu and order a Taco Tray, Double Baked Cayenne Wings, an Arugula Salad, and BBQ Carnitas Nachos just because you can. After all that, you might find yourself wanting to walk home to work it off, just so you can come back and do it all over again.
properties.name: Platt Park Brewing Co

properties.blurb: The Players Lockerroom calls itself the “come as you are” sports bar and we’re all for that. But showing up fresh out of the locker room after your rec softball game, still in a towel, might be taking things a little too far. ILuckily this lineup has everything else covered. You’ll find memorabilia-lined walls, TVs covering every angle, and a sandwich lineup worthy of an all-star team. It’s the perfect place to grab a drink, catch the game, and feel right at home
properties.name: Players Locker Room

properties.address: 16120 San Carlos Blvd, Fort Myers, FL 33908
properties.blurb: Point Ybel is going to be the best part of your Florida vacation. Yeah, the beach is cool, and sunshine is awesome, but have you tried Snook Bite, their flagship IPA, or Endless Summer Pale Ale? Look, we’re not gonna tell you how to spend your vacation, but the beach will still be there tomorrow. Grab your pals, shake the sand off your toes, and head to one of our fave spots in Fort Myers.
properties.name: Point Ybel Brewing

properties.address: 3200 NW 23rd Ave Suite 500, Pompano Beach, FL 33069, USA
properties.blurb: Every time you step into Pompano Beach Brewing, it's like walking into a surprise party for your taste buds. You never quite know what they've got on tap, but one thing's for sure – it's going to be amazing! Their lineup is a rollercoaster of flavors, featuring bold IPAs that'll make your hop-loving heart sing, decadent pastry stouts that are practically dessert in a glass, a rainbow of goses offering a tantalizing diversity of tastes, and everything in between. Seriously, it's a craft beer adventure waiting to happen.
properties.name: Pompano Beach Brewing Co

properties.blurb: The delightful scent of sizzling bacon perfumes the town of Cudahy and the good times never cease. At Porky's, you'll find mouthwatering $1 hot dogs that will have everyone oinking with joy. But it's not just about the delicious eats—this place is also a haven for beer enthusiasts. The taps are always flowing with a wide variety of brews, catering to every piggy palate. So, whether you're craving a pint or indulging in some porcine delights, Porky's on Squire is the place to be.
properties.name: Porky's on Squire

properties.blurb: Nestled in the heart of New Orleans, Port Orleans celebrates the city's rich culture with every pint. Whether you're a local or visiting for the first time, indulge in their handcrafted brews that capture the essence of the Big Easy while grubbing down on some tacos or wings. Join them for a beer and savor a taste of tradition and creativity.
properties.name: Port Orleans Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Potosi’s lineage dates back to the 19th century. As you feast your eyes on their stone built brewery, you might feel yourself magically growing a handlebar moustache. A grandfather of craft beer, Potosi proudly boasts core staples like the Snake Hollow IPA, Cave Ale Amber, or the confidently named Good Old Potosi Golden Ale. Be sure to also make some time for a brewery tour, a walk through their museum (that’s culture right there, baby!), and of course, any of their seasonals.
properties.name: Potosi Brewing

properties.address: 9500 University Ave Suite 1101, West Des Moines, IA 50266
properties.blurb: If you’re one of those people who really leaned into the plant mom aesthetic lately, then Pots & Shots is for you. If you’re one of those people who tried valiantly to be a plant mom but failed miserably, Pots & Shots is also the place for you. Just, you know, maybe keep to the shots side of place. Keep your black thumbs to yourself and sample one of the many inventive cocktails on the menu - the Perfect Plantista and Morning Jam Session are our faves.
properties.name: Pots & Shots

properties.address: 735 Girod St, New Orleans, LA 70130, USA
properties.name: Pour House @ District

properties.blurb: There’s just one thing in this world we love more than beer. And it goes by the name of pizza. In this case, we’re talking about wood-fired brick oven pizza. Swing by on Wednesdays for killer specials: $8 for 12” pies and $2 off all 14” pies. Grab a seat in the beer garden, order the Tony Montana—and 20 wings for $15 for good measure—and wash it all down with a local barley pop. Then, call an Uber. It’s time to get food drunk.
properties.name: Pour House Pizza and Beer Garden

properties.blurb: Ever been to a kick ass craft beer bar and wanted to try everything? Not possible, right? Wrong. Pour Taproom lets you pay by the ounce so you can try brews at your own pace. Start with a swig. Or go all in with a pint, then refill it, then refill your buddy’s glass then refill her buddy’s glass and… wait a minute? Are all these ounces going on your tab? You’re a giver, my friend.
properties.name: Pour Taproom St. Pete

properties.blurb: As you might imagine, a place called Pourhouse Bar and Grill has quite a large selection of beers on tap, and we love that they feature primarily local favorites. They also offer the “longest happy hour in Loveland,” running from 11 AM – 6 PM, Monday through Friday. Thank goodness it lasts forever, because the menu is huge and you’ll need the extra time to decide whether to order stuffed jalapenos, Jamaican jerk tacos, fish and chips, or all three.
properties.name: Pourhouse Bar and Grill

properties.blurb: Flip the switch on ordinary brewery experiences and step into a place where craft beer meets creativity and a serious love of science. Power Project Brewing brings engineering precision to every pint, from shockingly smooth stouts to high-voltage IPAs. The tech-inspired space feels part lab, part taproom, and all-around fun. Charge up with fresh brews, geek out over the brewing process, and enjoy a spark of something different in Hutto’s growing beer scene.
properties.name: Power Brewing Project

properties.blurb: Primitive Beer isn’t, like, Stone Ages primitive. But it is inspired by centuries-old Belgian brewing techniques, which means its beers are turbid mashed, koelschip inoculated, and barrel-fermented. And if you don’t know what any of those words mean, don’t worry: all you need to know is the blends are funky, balanced, and use Colorado-grown ingredients. And trust us: they go down nice and smooth.
properties.name: Primitive Beer

properties.blurb: Prison Pals was born from the idea of breaking free because sometimes you’ve gotta escape the ordinary to brew something extraordinary. These folks left feeling trapped behind and built a brewery that’s fresh, different, and proudly irreverent. The result? World-class beer made by people who know exactly what it’s like to crave freedom and pour it straight into every pint.
properties.name: Prison Pals Taproom

properties.blurb: Two best friends and bandmates decide to open a neighborhood bar together — and shenanigans ensue. No, this isn’t the premise of an early-2000s sitcom. It’s the actual plot of Joey Turbo and Casey Hughes’ lives. Introducing Promises, a new bar in the heart of Walker’s Point, with two stages, a covered patio, pinball, a pool table, pull tabs, a DJ booth, and more. And expect those stages to be put to good use.
properties.name: Promises

properties.blurb: We here at PubPass like to encourage you, our dear  drinkers, to diversify. Proper 21 is a spot that you  don’t need any urging to go to. It’s got an insane  food menu. It’s got a posh sports bar vibe (you might  be thinking “posh” and “sports bar” don’t go together  but you’d be wrong) and once you’re immersed in it,  my friend, you are in it. Now we know most of you  like burgers as much as you like beer, and the  Proper Burger does not disappoint.
properties.name: Proper 21

properties.blurb: Lucky for you, we loved Proper 21 so much we’re not-so-subtly suggesting you hit both DC locations. Have we steered you wrong yet? No, no we have not. Locals absolutely love this spot for its elevated sports bar vibe, unique menu, and cocktail list. You can definitely judge a place by the people that happy hour there, and only the cool kids are hanging here. So grab your buds, pull up a stool, and happy hour your heart out.
properties.name: Proper 21K

properties.blurb: Once an under-the-radar spot we couldn’t keep to ourselves, Prosperity has been found… and for good reason! We love this spot for quick sessions (Kettle Sour or Intra-KOLSCH-tal, please) or deep, dark, long sits (Mexican Hot Chocolate Imperial Stout) where we ponder all that is good in life. Like this place. And the fact that our buddy just brought another round of Tangering Suns.
properties.name: Prosperity Brewers

properties.blurb: We all could use a little more prosperity in our lives. Our advice: Do yourself a solid and find your luck at Prosperity Craft Beer & Wine Bar. This gem, located in Town Center at Boca Raton, features exclusive brews from Prosperity Brewers, along with cider, kombucha, and a full wine list. If good fortune’s on your side, you’ll make a day of it. After shopping. Or before. Or… in between?
properties.name: Prosperity Craft Beer & Wine Bar

properties.address: 115 E Main St, Pflugerville, TX 78660, USA
properties.blurb: History, community, and a whole lot of German-inspired goodness come together at Prost Alehouse. With 24 taps pouring house-brewed beers alongside wine, cider, and mead, there’s no shortage of options to raise a glass to. The taproom blends old-world character with modern touches, making it just as welcoming for a quick pint as it is for a full afternoon of good company and even better drinks. From crisp lagers to bold bocks, every pour is a Prost-worthy celebration.
properties.name: Prost Alehouse Texas

properties.blurb: We don’t have to be told twice to “drink like a German.” You might be wondering how exactly Germans drink. Our completely unscientific poll reveals that drinking like a German means with gusto, with friends, with ingredients sourced directly from Germany. It also means partaking in some delicious bar snacks. It means washing those snacks down with a nutty Dunkel or a summer seasonal Radler. Go forth, friends, and drink like Germans!
properties.name: Prost Brewing Co & Biergarten

properties.blurb: The fine folks at Prost Biergarten lean heavily into their German heritage and we gotta say, no one does it better in the beer business than the Germans. In fact, they offer an award-winning lineup that includes a refreshing pilsner and a rich dunkelweizen. Pair either of those with one of the mouth-watering menu items (bratwurst or schnitzel, anyone?) and you’re in for a good time, with or without your lederhosen.
properties.name: Prost Brewing Co. & Biergarten (Highlands Ranch)

properties.address: 351 W 104th Ave Unit A, Northglenn, CO 80234, USA
properties.name: Prost Brewing Company (Northglenn)

properties.address: 901 Bayfront Ct suite 102, San Diego, CA 92101, USA
properties.name: Protector Brewery

properties.address: 711 E Missouri Ave Ste 115, Phoenix, AZ 85014, USA
properties.name: PROVISION

properties.address: 1401 West River Rd N, Minneapolis, MN 55411
properties.blurb: We're certain you've tried Miraculum — the glorious IPA that helped this place grow into one of the hottest tap rooms in town. If not? Start with that. Then try something very few in the country have tried... feather bowling! Pryes is one of only three locations in the states where you can play said sport. Something tells us you'll be staying for a few! PubPass Not Redeemable For Barrel Aged Beers
properties.name: Pryes Brewing

properties.address: 8005 E Roosevelt St, Scottsdale, AZ 85257, USA
properties.name: Pub Rock

properties.blurb: Step into the world of Public Craft Brewing Co, where the vibe is all about laid-back enjoyment, quality brews, tasty bites, toe-tapping tunes, and a big ol' embrace of the downtown Kenosha community. At Public Craft Brewing Co, they're all about the craft-brewed ales that put a smile on your face and a little hop in your step. But it's not just about the beer; oh no, it's a full-blown experience. Delicious food that'll make your belly do a happy dance? Check. Live music that sets the perfect backdrop for your good times? Check. And let's not forget the warm, fuzzy feeling that comes from supporting your downtown Kenosha community – these folks take that responsibility seriously, and it shows.
properties.name: Public Craft Brewing Co

properties.address: 8725 N Deerwood Dr, Brown Deer, WI 53209
properties.blurb: Public House 55 opens its doors again this spring, just in time for you to enjoy its dog-friendly beer garden, family-friendly central green space, and, well, event-friendly event space. It also has a tap room with $1 off beers on weekdays from 3-6. Plus, it doesn’t hurt that those beers have names like Froggin’ Awesome (a sour ale) and Threat Level: Midnight (an imperial s’mores stout).
properties.name: Public House 55

properties.blurb: For as much as we love beer you’d assume we’ve given some thought about brewing our own. While we don’t have the skills needed for that, we have plenty of ideas. That’s where Public Offering Brewing Co comes in. They don’t just want to brew the beer THEY like, they want to brew the beers YOU want. They invite you to ask questions and tell them what kind of beer you think would be rad. Nothing is off limits when you put your creativity in the hands of these folks. So dream big, and stop in with those new ideas.
properties.name: Public Offering Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Nestled right in the mountains within spitting distance of Dillon Bay, Pug Ryan’s Brewery is about to be the highlight of your day. Their classic pub fare pairs perfectly with draft beer options ranging from the Pack Iron Pale Ale to the Outlaw Vanilla Stout. Daily specials and awesome outdoor seating will keep you coming back.
properties.name: Pug Ryan's Brewery

properties.blurb: With a name like Pump Haus, you know they're serious about their beer game. From hoppy delights to smooth sips, their taps are a symphony of flavors just waiting for you to explore. And speaking of bites, their kitchen is like a culinary playground where they whip up mouthwatering creations that'll have you coming back for more. Got a competitive streak? Challenge your friends to a game of pool, or settle in front of one of their TV's to catch the latest game action. And let's talk about the specials – every night of the week is a new opportunity to indulge in some seriously delicious deals.
properties.name: Pump Haus Pub & Grill

properties.blurb: With an impressive array of 24 taps, half of which showcase the brewery's in-house creations, Quarter Barrel is on a mission to delight taste buds with a diverse selection of beers. From time-honored classics to intriguing experimental blends, the beer menu promises an exciting journey for every guest. But the excitement doesn't stop there. A culinary haven as well, the pizzeria crafts delectable pizzas from scratch, embracing locally-sourced ingredients to ensure a top-notch dining experience that's a cut above the rest. You can enjoy the arcade filled with over 40 games, transporting you back to the heyday of video gaming while savoring their brews and bites. It's a family-friendly haven where both young and young-at-heart can make memories together.
properties.name: Quarter Barrel Brewery and Arcade

properties.blurb: There’s no better place to happy hour in Chandler (we checked our notes and can confirm that the Quarthaus patio beats all other patios). We’d never steer you away from sampling a local craft beer, but perhaps we can nudge you in the direction of a cocktail made with their small batch vodka or whisky. A gentle little nudge. Go on now, try that spicy bloody mary this weekend. You know you want to.
properties.name: QuartHaus

properties.blurb: It doesn't get much more inviting than QuartHaus. They're what they call themselves a Local Drinkery. And they live up to their name rather splendidly. Off the beaten path, away from the hustle and bustle of the city, QuartHaus has quickly become our home away from home. 25 craft beers and eight wines on tap. Light bites to fend off the hungries. We're a bit obsessed with their Bail Bond Brut, a Golden Ale so crisp and easy to put back that we typically have a couple just for good measure.
properties.name: QuartHaus

properties.address: 506 E Grand Ave, Des Moines, IA 50309
properties.blurb: We love Buffalo chicken... in any form or setting. We also love dips. So when we discovered the homemade Buffalo Chicken Dip at Quinton’s we just about lost it. Thank god we held it together! Had we not kept our wits about us, we might have missed out on other homemade noshables like poppers, fries and even more dips. And, kids? Those are just appetizers. When you’re ready to go main event? Get a sandwich like the T.A.C. or the Nelson Philly or... wait... is that?  Buffalo Chicken! We’re spent.
properties.name: Quinton's

properties.address: 5741 C St SW Suite D, Cedar Rapids, IA 52404
properties.blurb: At Quinton's, the culinary journey is a delectable exploration of flavors and favorites.From mouthwatering appetizers to sumptuous burgers, fresh salads, and a medley of specialties and classics, the menu caters to all tastes and cravings. But let's talk about the real showstopper – the spuds. These Idaho potatoes, hearty and wholesome, take center stage as a meal in their own right. Each serving features not just one, but two Idaho potatoes, generously adorned with an array of toppings limited only by your imagination. And of course, the libations are equally impressive. Quinton's proudly presents a beer list that showcases the finest brews Iowa has to offer, ensuring that every sip is a celebration of local craftsmanship.
properties.name: Quinton's (C Street Cedar Rapids)

properties.blurb: At Quinton's, the culinary journey is a delectable exploration of flavors and favorites.From mouthwatering appetizers to sumptuous burgers, fresh salads, and a medley of specialties and classics, the menu caters to all tastes and cravings. But let's talk about the real showstopper – the spuds. These Idaho potatoes, hearty and wholesome, take center stage as a meal in their own right. Each serving features not just one, but two Idaho potatoes, generously adorned with an array of toppings limited only by your imagination. And of course, the libations are equally impressive. Quinton's proudly presents a beer list that showcases the finest brews Iowa has to offer, ensuring that every sip is a celebration of local craftsmanship.
properties.name: Quinton's (Coralville)

properties.address: 450 1st St SW Unit 101, Cedar Rapids, IA 52404
properties.blurb: At Quinton's, the culinary journey is a delectable exploration of flavors and favorites.From mouthwatering appetizers to sumptuous burgers, fresh salads, and a medley of specialties and classics, the menu caters to all tastes and cravings. But let's talk about the real showstopper – the spuds. These Idaho potatoes, hearty and wholesome, take center stage as a meal in their own right. Each serving features not just one, but two Idaho potatoes, generously adorned with an array of toppings limited only by your imagination. And of course, the libations are equally impressive. Quinton's proudly presents a beer list that showcases the finest brews Iowa has to offer, ensuring that every sip is a celebration of local craftsmanship.
properties.name: Quinton's (Downtown Cedar Rapids)

properties.address: 215 E Washington St, Iowa City, IA 52240
properties.blurb: At Quinton's, the culinary journey is a delectable exploration of flavors and favorites.From mouthwatering appetizers to sumptuous burgers, fresh salads, and a medley of specialties and classics, the menu caters to all tastes and cravings. But let's talk about the real showstopper – the spuds. These Idaho potatoes, hearty and wholesome, take center stage as a meal in their own right. Each serving features not just one, but two Idaho potatoes, generously adorned with an array of toppings limited only by your imagination. And of course, the libations are equally impressive. Quinton's proudly presents a beer list that showcases the finest brews Iowa has to offer, ensuring that every sip is a celebration of local craftsmanship.
properties.name: Quinton's Bar and Deli (Iowa City)

properties.address: 4354 N Federal Hwy, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33308, USA
properties.blurb: Offer is only good on Full-priced menu items. Does not include Happy Hour offers.
properties.name: Quvo Tacos & Craft Beer

properties.blurb: R Bar hits all the right notes to make it feel like, well, our bar. A full menu of your favorite bar food, from burgers to pizza to apps like deep fried pickles and tots. A strong selection of beers—or a Bloody Mary if it’s that kind of morning. Daily specials and events like Tuesday Bingo and a Friday Fish Fry. Yeah, we’ll be coming back.
properties.name: R BAR

properties.blurb: At R'Noggin Brewing Co, the heart of Southeast Wisconsin, passion for high-quality beer comes to life. With a dedication to tradition and a deep-seated respect for the craft, R'Noggin proudly produces exceptional brews that pay homage to the region's time-honored beer legacy. Step into a world where beer is not just a beverage but a cherished tradition. With an ever-evolving selection of 90+ different beers on tap throughout the year, each visit is a new adventure. Explore a diverse array of flavors, styles, and aromas that only the art of brewing can provide. And now, there's even more to savor. R'Noggin Brewing Co has joined forces with Antioch Pizza Shop, bringing you a mouthwatering fusion of flavors. From classic pizzas and hearty pastas to delectable Italian beef and hot dogs, the menu offers a tantalizing journey through Italian-American culinary delights.
properties.name: R'Noggin Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Community. Culture. Cerveza. That's what's on tap at Raices Brewing and we're here for it. We started with a couple Manguitos and a Celebración — a Champagne-style beer perfect for mowing the lawn (their words, not ours!). From there? It was time for some coffee... in beer form! Cafecito is a Coffee Blonde Ale made with beans from Indigenous Women-owned roasters. We'll drink to that!
properties.name: Raices Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Is there anything more Wisconsin than a good ol’ fashioned Friday night fish fry? Okay, it ranks at least in the top five. Rail House, a family-owned brewpub, is frequented by locals and out-of-towners just passing through, but one thing they can all agree on is that the service is friendly, the onion rings are piping hot, and the beer is cold.
properties.name: Rail House Restaurant & Brewpub

properties.address: 4055 Central Ave NE, Columbia Heights, MN 55421, USA
properties.blurb: At Rail Werks Brewing Depot, you’re in for a trifecta: cold beer, smokin’ BBQ, and a family-friendly atmosphere that feels like a backyard cookout. Whether you're grabbing a pint from their rotating taps or diving into some fall-off-the-bone BBQ, it's always a good time. Bring the whole crew—yes, even the picky eaters. The beer flows, the meat smokes, and the vibe is just right.
properties.name: Rail Werks Brewing Depot

properties.address: 11625 Reed Ct Unit B, Broomfield, CO 80020
properties.blurb: If you rely on a smart phone for directions, chances are you’ll feel like you’re on a wild goose chase trying to navigate your way to Rails End. But this tucked-away brewery is worth all the wrong turns. Best of all, you’ll be rewarded with events aplenty (corn hole tourneys, yoga, trivia nights, etc.), succulent suds like their Blood Light IPA, and an expansive outdoor area. We’ll save you a seat.
properties.name: Rails End Beer Co

properties.address: 11625 Reed Ct Unit B, Broomfield, CO 80020
properties.blurb: In case you hadn't heard, Rails End Beer is the place with the Blood Orange IPA that has everyone in the greater Bloomfield area drooling. It’s also the place with the amazing patio with the mist that feels refreshing in the summer. It also happens to be the place that’s super dog-friendly, if that’s your thing. At least one of these things has to be your thing. Make Rails End Beer your new thing, you won’t regret it.
properties.name: Rails End Beer Co

properties.blurb: Rain Dogs, the "Living Room of Riverside," is nestled right in the beating heart of 5 Points! Prepare to unwind in a laid-back atmosphere where good vibes flow as freely as the 18 beers on tap and the countless bottles of beer and wine. Immerse yourself in the local arts scene with monthly exhibits, and don't forget to peruse the community book and music shelf for a hidden gem. Weekends come alive with captivating shows, featuring both homegrown talents and renowned national acts. Join in on this community-based, independently owned gem and experience the spirit of 5 Points.
properties.name: Rain Dogs

properties.blurb: You’ve seen the maneuver. Maybe you’ve even participated. The cap goes inside out. The beer starts flowing. The comeback’s in full effect. The bar’s in jubilation. There are ducks on the pond. It’s a shot to the gap. The runner rounds third. He’s heading home. The throw is late! Excuse us, bartender—another round for the victors. We loved the Ambush, a slightly malty amber ale with caramel notes on the backbone, perfect for raising a glass to a winning night at the yard. Play ball!
properties.name: Rally Cap Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Range Restaurant and Cocktails invites you to embark on an unparalleled experience, where three distinct concepts seamlessly intertwine to create a captivating Central Iowa destination like no other. The American cuisine restaurant at Range is a culinary haven, boasting signature steaks, artisanal pastas, unique burgers, delectable appetizers, and more. Their commitment to utilizing the freshest, locally-sourced ingredients, primarily from the Midwest, shines through in every dish, all complemented by reasonably priced, high-quality proteins.Embracing innovation, their carefully curated cocktail menu features house-made ingredients, handpicked bourbon barrels, and premium liquors. With an impressive selection of over 20 draft beers, including seasonal picks, and one of Iowa's most extensive arrays of special reserve whiskeys, Range's libation offerings are second to none. For a truly unique experience, explore their custom indoor golf center – a space of unparalleled distinction. With six PGA-endorsed indoor golf simulators, Range caters to both avid golfers and those seeking an entertaining night out.
properties.name: Range Restaurant and Cocktails

properties.address: 10930 Endeavour Way Suite C, Seminole, FL 33777, USA
properties.name: Rapp Brewing Company

properties.address: 9535 Park Meadows Dr unit f, Lone Tree, CO 80124
properties.blurb: Stepping through the door at Rare by Grist is a little bit like climbing through the wardrobe and landing in Narnia. This eclectic space is far from your average brewpub and boasts a southern-inspired menu (deviled eggs, anyone?). While the food options are completely drool-worthy, we’re here to recommend some offbeat but delicious beer and unique cocktail offerings. There’s nothing on this menu we don’t want to try, and that’s really saying something.
properties.name: RARE By Grist

properties.address: 295 Pine Lake Dr Ste C103, Nocatee, FL 32081, USA
properties.blurb: The selection of craft beers is seriously top-notch. You won't believe the variety—they've got everything from local gems to sought-after brews from all over the country. The best part is, you don't have to be a beer expert to appreciate the goodness. They've got a welcoming atmosphere, and the staff is always ready to help you find the perfect brew for your taste. And let me tell you, the name doesn't lie—seriously, it's really good beer!
properties.name: Really Good Beer Stop

properties.blurb: As the name might suggest, Reclaimed Rails Brewing has a sense of where they’ve come from. Their historic two-story taproom/brewery is decked out with reclaimed wood and it is a beaut. Located right on the Chichaqua Valley trail, the brewery is known for attracting cyclers. However you get there, make sure you try the Blood Orange Wheat, or pick up a four-pack if you’re on the go.
properties.name: Reclaimed Rails Brewing Co

properties.blurb: When you find a place with a little bit of history, it always makes the beer taste just a little bit better. At Reclaimed Rails Brewing, you’ll find a unique taproom that makes folks want to gather around. If you’re lucky, you’ll gather while live music is playing. Or on one of those perfect summer nights that is best spent on the gorgeous patio. The Lager Ride is a must drink and if you’re pulling off the bike trail, we recommend a refreshing Blood Orange Wheat to help you rehydrate.
properties.name: Reclaimed Rails Brewing Co

properties.blurb: The rad folks behind Red Bear Brewing are doing it all right. There’s an impressive lineup of craft beers and you’ll fall in love at first sip. They are creating an inviting community space with stacks on stacks on stacks of board games so gather your pals (or make new ones at the communal tables) and settle in with a brewski and a rousing game of Life.
properties.name: Red Bear Brewing

properties.address: 11000 Metro Pkwy Unit 23, Fort Myers, FL 33966, USA
properties.name: Red Horse Distillery

properties.blurb: If you wandered into Red Rock Saloon, you’d be forgiven for thinking you weren’t in Madison anymore. Billing themselves as the city’s only country and rock bar, they mean business, hosting live music every Friday and offering some of the tastiest wings and BBQ around. Come thirsty, though we recommend riding the mechanical bull before throwing back the whiskey.
properties.name: Red Rock Saloon

properties.blurb: Red Rocks Grill is the perfect place to pop in for a pre-show burger and beer on your way to spend an epic night at the legendary Red Rocks Amphitheater. This rustic spot has charmed locals and concert-goers for years. The Dinosaur Eggs are spot on, and we enthusiastically endorse the Man Handler burger (meat on meat on meat), with a side of onion rings and a cold brewski to wash it down.
properties.name: Red Rocks Grill

properties.blurb: If you swing by the Red Rooster on a Thursday you are in for some grooving and jamming, as the Blues Jam sets the stage on fire! On Fridays, it's all about the mouthwatering Fish Dinner - beer-battered cod that'll make your taste buds do a happy dance! With slaw and house-made tartar sauce, it's a seafood delight. But don't worry if you visit any other day, because there's always something to crow about!
properties.name: Red Rooster

properties.blurb: Lean into your patriotic side at Red Sash Brewing — because this veteran-owned micro-brewery serves exclusively American-made beers. On the menu right now, enjoy 7 classically brewed IPAs, Pale Ales, Stouts, and California Commons. Bottoms up, Kansas City. And once you've had a few, pose in front of the wings painted on the patio. Your 'gram will thank you. So will your Gram.
properties.name: Red Sash Brewing

properties.blurb: Creative cocktails, a thoughtful, sustainable menu, a fantastic beer lineup and a hip-yet-chill vibe that just screams “date night”. The Stag remains one of our favorite Northeast haunts. For our money, it’s hard to beat a basket of their truffled kettle corn and pint of something cold, hoppy and local. They’ve got you covered. Oh, and as far as that date night goes? They’ve got one of the best specials in town. Ask about it!
properties.name: Red Stag Supperclub

properties.blurb: We came for a beer and stayed all evening. That’s bound to happen at a place with a staff and clientele as fun as Red Star’s. A celebration of creativity — from the art on its walls to the bands that rock outside — there’s always something going on at this Seminole Heights hotspot. Whether you start or end your night here, you’ll be glad you did.
properties.name: Red Star Rock Bar

properties.address: 521 S Tejon St, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
properties.name: Red Swing Brewhouse

properties.blurb: If you want to catch the game let us suggest Red’s. If you want to catch some good vibes may we also suggest Red’s. Now, if you want to catch BOTH, boy are you in for a treat. With TVs galore a hopping beer list and some of the best wings around you can’t go wrong. You can choose from any of their 9 sauces or four dry rubs. Trust us, you’ll be feeling saucy.
properties.name: Red's American Grill

properties.address: 2245 E St Francis Ave, St Francis, WI 53235
properties.blurb: "Not mistaken for anything else in the neighborhood, Redbar is well, a red..bar. Even if you’re colorblind, the smoky aroma of succulent barbeque courtesy of onsite Saucy Swine will probably guide you through the front doors. Inside are all the hallmarks of attentive and creative ownership, like cool exposed brick walls and fun additions like jukebox bingo. Take in the game with a pint of Sprecher under the murderous gaze of an impressive collection of bobbleheads, or saunter upstairs for some tiki lounge cocktailing.  "
properties.name: redbar

properties.address: 405 N Dubuque St, North Liberty, IA 52317
properties.name: Reds Alehouse

properties.blurb: Renegade kindly refers to their beer as offensively delicious. They brew for the risk-takers. The rebel spirits. And, you guessed it, the renegades. It definitely shows in their variety of beers. If you consider yourself a die-hard hop head, you’ll be pleased by their Endpoint Triple IPA that, at 11%, is terrifyingly easy to drink. Just don't forget to call an Uber. Plz and thx.
properties.name: Renegade

properties.blurb: Need an excuse to treat yourself to a beer today? Resolute Brewing Company has just the thing. Food trucks seven days a week. Twenty tap lines serving traditional, barrel-aged, and unique styles. A dedicated gluten-free tap. A large outdoor patio and accompanying lawn. Need we say more? Fine. Juice Index might be the tastiest New England-style IPA this side of Vermont.
properties.name: Resolute Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Resolute Brewing turned out to be a welcome stop between Denver and Boulder, and boy, we sure need to make that trip more often if it’s gonna mean fantastic food trucks, twenty or so tap lines, and a postcard worthy view of the mountains from their patio. Events like pop music bingo and movie nights offer a little something extra than other breweries, but the All the Fuss Lemondrop Sour and Resolute Brewing’s Transparently Trendy New England Style IPA were ones that we would squeeze into that postcard.
properties.name: Resolute Brewing Tap & Cellar

properties.blurb: Belly up for a night of revelry at Revel Bar. Our recommendation? Try their take on an Old Fashioned — complete with a fire-kissed orange peel and rosemary (crazy good). After that? Sip on a Harvest Margarita before you hit the hookah (yeah, it’s a hookah bar, too) and get all contemplative about whether or not Bay View is one of the best neighborhoods in the universe. What is a universe, anyway? [exhale]
properties.name: Revel Bar

properties.blurb: Belly up for a night of revelry at Revel Bar. Our recommendation? Try their take on an Old Fashoned — complete with a fire-kissed orange peel and rosemary (crazy good). After that? Sip on a Harvest Margarita before you hit the hookah (yeah, it’s a hookah bar, too) and get all contemplative about whether or not Bay View is one of the best neighborhoods in the universe. What is a universe, anyway? [exhale]
properties.name: Revel Bar

properties.blurb: The folks at Reverence Brewing Company are bringing a touch of Minnesota Nice to Denver and we gotta say, we’re here for it. This new spot in the neighborhood brings the cozy vibes and we know it’s going to be your favorite place to grab a pint or a flight. The Hazy IPA is a favorite amongst regulars and many are begging for the Raspberry Wheat to become a year-round staple. Reverence is straight-up good humans serving good beer.
properties.name: Reverence Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Rewined Beer & Wine Bar is the brainchild of two individuals with a shared love for warm retro nostalgia and gaming. This eclectic establishment is a reflection of their vision, much like their ever-evolving selection of 130 beers, ciders, and wines, which includes a rotating lineup of eight beer and wine taps. At Rewined, you're invited to step into a world where you can enjoy 8-bit Nintendo games, Halo matches, or even challenge a friend to a game of Risk while savoring a refreshing brew and indulging in a classic grilled cheese sandwich. But the passion at Rewined extends beyond beverages and games. This is a veteran- and gay-owned business that takes pride in its place within the community. So, pull up a barstool, select your favorite brew, and join in the fun. Whether you're sinking Battleships or simply savoring a good time, Rewined Beer & Wine Bar looks forward to welcoming you with open arms.
properties.name: Rewined Beer And Wine Bar

properties.address: 1360 W Sweden Rd, Brockport, NY 14420, USA
properties.blurb: ** Deal for $3 beer OR 1/2 Priced Flight! **
properties.name: RG Brewery

properties.address: 239 E Main St, Sun Prairie, WI 53590
properties.blurb: Just northeast of Madison in lovely Sun Prairie, Right Bauer Brewing sling fantastic beer, thoughtful cocktails and southern-style BBQ ready to make you a regular. The building’s original 19th century hardwood floors and brick have been restored recently and the renovations made the place feel new and refreshing. They have a cozy backyard patio as a fresh air option, but the bartender kept us indoors with witty banter and a few tasting flights. Will a second order of their absurdly tasty cheese curds spoil your appetite? They make To-Go boxes for a reason, pal. Go crazy.
properties.name: Right Bauer Brewing

properties.address: 920 Girard St NE, Washington, DC 20017, USA
properties.blurb: Nestled in Northeast DC, Right Proper Brewing Co.'s Tasting Room is your passport to a world of flavors. Open from Wednesday through Sunday, this cozy haven serves up their core beers that are bound to make your taste buds dance. How about diving into the Senate Beer or sipping on the Li'l Wit? But the story doesn't end there. Right Proper's ever-evolving seasonal offerings are sure to keep you intrigued. Enjoy their eclectic creations through flights and glasses, or if you're on the move, grab cans, bottles, or growlers to go. Step inside and soak in the ambiance of reclaimed cherry wood and a creatively quirky chalk art mural. Peek into the brewery and witness the magic happening. When the weather's lovely, head out to the patio for some good times under the open sky. Right Proper Brewing Co. is all about crafting memories, one sip at a time!
properties.name: Right Proper Brewing Co

properties.address: 624 T St NW, Washington, DC 20001, USA
properties.blurb: Just when you thought Right Proper couldn’t top their game, you step into the Shaw Brewpub & Kitchen and realize they absolutely can. Their brews pair perfectly with some of the tastiest food in town. Whether you’re diving into a juicy burger, crispy chicken sandwich, hot wings, or beer-battered cheese curds (yes, they’re as good as they sound), every bite is a winner. Trust us, you can’t go wrong. Especially when you’ve got a cold Right Proper beer to wash it all down.
properties.name: Right Proper Brewing Company (Shaw Brewpub & Kitchen)

properties.address: 28120 Hunters Ridge Blvd, Bonita Springs, FL 34135
properties.blurb: Whether you’re on vacation or just wish you were, Riptide Brewing is the spot for laid-back vibes and refreshing beers. Their newest location is spacious and airy, with lots of outdoor seating and a rotating roster of food trucks, plus the same brews you’ve come to love. Stop by for trivia night or to soak up some sun with a Seafoam Haze IPA in hand.
properties.name: Riptide Brewing Co (Bonita Springs)

properties.blurb: Riptide Brewing feels like a casual beach bar without all that pesky sand. Get ready for laid-back vibes and refreshing beers like their Olde Naples Blonde or Seafoam Haze IPA. If you hoped to soak up some sun, you’re in luck. Grab a table out back by the food truck, or challenge your friends to some yard games. Loser buys the next round!
properties.name: Riptide Brewing Co (Naples)

properties.blurb: Hate to break it to you, PubPassers, but we now deal exclusively in queso. We had a good run hustling beer for the past few years, but when you roll up to Rita’s Cantina and order that queso flight, you’ll understand why we pivoted our business model. We haven’t even mentioned the margarita flight, which is a definite must. When you’re tucked away at a table at Rita’s, with the garage doors open and a cool breeze blowing in, it’s hard to believe you’re in Iowa, and not a taco stand south of the border.
properties.name: Rita's Cantina

properties.blurb: Some people’s ritual is meditation, or running, or cleaning the kitchen every Thursday. Personally we like the ritual of eating incredible food in a place that feels like a cozy cabin retreat. Ritual Social House is exactly that, with a menu that packs serious flavor, from the pizzas to the parmesan fries. Did we mention their cocktail lineup and selection of local microbrews? Say hello to your new weekly tradition.
properties.name: Ritual Social House

properties.address: 12 Avenida Menendez, St. Augustine, FL 32084, USA
properties.blurb: River & Fort serves southern continental flavors with a side of skyline envy. Local seafood, steaks, craft cocktails, and rotating local beers pair perfectly with rooftop views of St. Augustine’s historic streets, the Bayfront, and even the lighthouse on a clear day. It’s elevated dining, literally.
properties.name: River & Fort

properties.address: 201 Main St St 101, Kansas City, MO 64105
properties.blurb: History permeates the very air of this space, carrying the essence of the pre-prohibition breweries that once thrived here. River Bluff Brewing has fully embraced these historical vibes, and we believe they've successfully struck paydirt by doing so. With an ever-changing tap list, you’re sure to always find something that’ll suit your fancy. The brewers here meld together tricks-of-the-trade from the past and present to create some truly golden elixirs. This is definitely our favorite kind of alchemy.
properties.name: River Bluff Brewing

properties.blurb: As a brewery that’s big on community, River Hops is ready to welcome you. With a full calendar of events, including tons of live music, it’s the perfect spot for you and your group of friends to spend the afternoon. In the warmer months, there’s ample outdoor seating. Try the Oxbow Orange and see for yourself why it’s their top-selling beer.
properties.name: River Hops Brewing

properties.blurb: Pull up a seat and get ready to deep-dive into the delectable world of Belgian style and barrel-aged beers. Our friends at River North established their reputation for these tasty yeast-forward beers as soon as their doors first opened, and believe us, it is well deserved. Pop into the Washington St. location, and you’ll get a chance to sample their heavy-hitting barrel-aged brews – yes we love the God Complex, no we will not be discussing the reasons why at this time. Should you find yourself exploring the spaces and views of RiNo, bop into their Blake St. location! Personally, we’d say grab a Mountain Haze and head outside to their patio with your pup, but what do we know?
properties.name: River North Brewery

properties.blurb: When you’re a brewery in Colorado, you have to prepare yourself for all four seasons. River North has the perfect patio and accompanying tap lineup to maximize those long summer nights. When snowy season hits, you can hunker down inside with a delightful array of winter seasonal offerings. Regardless of the season, you know you’re going to get a great beer brewed by great folks.
properties.name: River North Brewery

properties.blurb: One of the best ‘beer stores’ in the land, Riverside Market is a Fort Lauderdale staple, with 350 bottle options and countless taps! Couch potatoes are welcomed with cozy sofas and armchairs. The coolest part about this place is the pay-as-you-go system. Down as many bottles as you like, bring those empties to the register, and pay before you leave. Oh and the food? Dude the food. Come hungry and thank us later.
properties.name: Riverside Market Plantation

properties.blurb: Laudy, aren’t you lucky bastards for having all the Riverside Markets? We’re not going to play favorites, but South serves breakfast… and we could use some grub after last night at Plantation. The hand-dipped Buffalo Shrimp hits so hard. Been a minute since you’ve been to NYC? Order the Pastrami Sammich and thank us later. Or just get up from your barstool and yell, “I’m walkin’ here!”
properties.name: Riverside Market South

properties.blurb: Beer and coffee. Together. In one place. No, we swear you’re not dreaming. There truly is a place you can delight your senses with a perfectly crafted latte, or a rich cup of coffee, and THEN delight them in an entirely different way with a hazy IPA, a tart sour, or a crushable lager. Y’all we gotta say this is about as close to perfection as you’re going to get.
properties.name: Rochester Brewing

properties.blurb: Beer and coffee. Together. In one place. No, we swear you’re not dreaming. There truly is a place you can delight your senses with a perfectly crafted latte, or a rich cup of coffee, and THEN delight them in an entirely different way with a hazy IPA, a tart sour, or a crushable lager. Y’all we gotta say this is about as close to perfection as you’re going to get.
properties.name: Rochester Brewing (Parkville)

properties.blurb: Coffee shop by day, brewery by night. Sounds like our type of place, right captain? Come in the morning for a cinnamon cold press and a roasting class; stay later for the Black Currant Sour and homemade mead. The space doubles as an event venue, so even if you’re stopping in for breakfast, you’ll be aesthetically set for the rest of your day.
properties.name: Rochester KC

properties.blurb: Rock out to the classics while you enjoy Sgt. Pepper’s Jalapeno Poppers, Ring of Fire wings, or the “Any Way You Want It” pizza. This rock ’n’ roll-themed spot offers an entertaining night out and is great for families or large groups. With over 50 taps, there are plenty of beers to choose from, many from local craft breweries.
properties.name: Rock & Brews

properties.address: 26000 State Rd 56 W, Lutz, FL 33559, USA
properties.name: Rock & Brews

properties.blurb: Kansas Rock City, anyone? Founded in 2012, Rock & Brews is the brainchild of a small group headlined by none other than Gene Simmons and Paul Stanley of KISS fame. This is the place to be if you wanna rock ‘n’ roll all night after indulging in some retail therapy or getting some much-needed spa treatment or visiting the museum or movie theater at the impressive Prairiefire shopping and entertainment complex. Classic rock fans will delight in the ambiance and menu items named in honor of songs and artists from the glory days. All manner of classic American fare (appetizers, burgers, BBQ, pizzas) are on offer here, as are over 50 rotating taps with plenty of craft faves, including local stalwarts like Boulevard and Free State Brewing Co.
properties.name: Rock and Brews (Prairie Fire)

properties.blurb: We love a pub with a little bit of history behind it. Nestled inside the historic train depot, Rock Bottom Express is just such a place. They’ve retained much of the original building while creating a classic bar feel. Also classic is the food: burgers, wings, cheese curds. As luck would have it, all of those pair perfectly with their line-up of—you guessed it—classic beer favorites.
properties.name: Rock Bottom Express

properties.blurb: Music and beer just make sense together. That’s the ethos behind Rock Brothers Brewing, a brewery that works with bands from around the corner (and around the country) to create recipes, artwork, and products that will go down nice and easy. Support amazing artists with every can you crack open, and show up for live music in person to experience the partnership in full.
properties.name: Rock Brothers Brewing

properties.blurb: You’ve got the East Coast and you’ve got the West Coast, but in Loveland, it’s all about the Rock Coast. Brewed in-house with beers named after Colorado’s trails, passes, and iconic figures, it’s a lineup that feels right at home in the Rockies. Pair it with some seriously crave-worthy bar food, and you’ve got the perfect mix of local flavor and laid-back vibes. Beer in your glass, bites on your plate. Let us welcome to the best coast of all.
properties.name: Rock Coast Brewery

properties.blurb: Step into the world of Rock'n Dough Pizza & Brewery, where the magic of delicious brews and irresistible food dances together in perfect harmony. This lively brewpub boasts an impressive lineup of ales, IPA's, ciders, and even some tongue-tingling sours on tap. It's a paradise for beer enthusiasts and flavor explorers alike. But hold on, it's not just about the brews here. Prepare to have your taste buds dazzled by our culinary creations beyond the realm of pizza perfection. Sink your teeth into juicy burgers, wrap your hands around savory wraps, or embrace the fresh goodness of our vibrant salads. At Rock'n Dough, we believe that good times are best enjoyed with a frothy mug in one hand and a scrumptious bite in the other. So come on over and experience the lively atmosphere, where brews flow, flavors explode, and laughter fills the air. It's a taste adventure you won't want to miss!
properties.name: Rock'n Dough Pizza & Brewery

properties.blurb: Rockhound describes itself as ‘a place for good beer, good food and good company.’ They aren’t wrong. But even if the company was lousy (it isn’t) or the beer was awful (it’s anything but) we’d settle for one out of three when the menu offers notable consumables like duck nachos and a hummus so good it made us thinking about becoming health nuts for a moment before we ordered a reuben and a toasted French dip with curds and laughed about that one time we almost became health nuts at that kick ass brewery. That was hilarious.
properties.name: Rockhound Brewing  Company

properties.blurb: You can tell the people at RockPit don’t take themselves too seriously, even if the beer is seriously good. This lowkey spot in the SoDo district has 20 taps and debuts a new beer every week. We love the friendly bartenders and feeling like we’re hanging out at a neighbor’s backyard grill-out. Speaking of grilling, food is available to order from a BBQ place right next door. Rock on.
properties.name: Rockpit Brewing

properties.address: 1320 S Vandeventer Ave, St. Louis, MO 63110
properties.blurb: Rockwell Beer Co. shares a mission with all of us who appreciate a finely crafted brew – they're here for good beer, and we're definitely here for good beer too! Nestled proudly in the heart of St. Louis, Missouri, this 15-barrel brewery has deep roots in the community and a refreshingly straightforward philosophy: Beer should be all about fun, not fussiness. And we couldn't agree more! At Rockwell, they're always pushing the boundaries, experimenting with new styles, and approaching beer-making with an open and creative mind. Every sip is a testament to their dedication to crafting exceptional brews that'll put a smile on your face.
properties.name: Rockwell Beer Co

properties.blurb: Rockwell Beer Garden is your go-to destination for an outdoor escape. With its inviting open-air patio and delectable tavern-style pizza, it's the perfect spot to indulge your senses. Sip on a refreshing craft beer while basking in the sun and enjoying the vibrant ambiance. Fuel up with a cup of expertly brewed coffee from Coma Coffee Roasters, ensuring a delightful start to your day. With 16 HDTVs scattered throughout, you can catch all the action of your favorite sports games while immersing yourself in the lively atmosphere. Owned by two former rugby players, Rockwell Beer Garden exudes a spirit of camaraderie and good times. Come and experience the joy of great food, great drinks, and great company at Rockwell Beer Garden, where every visit is a memorable escape.
properties.name: Rockwell Beer Garden

properties.blurb: If Watermelon Cucumber, Blackberry Chocolate, and Pineapple Passionfruit don’t sound like your typical beer flavors, you must not have been to Rocky Mountain Brewery lately. Specializing in cider-flavored beers, these guys always have something unique on tap. Standard lager more your thing? Yeah, they’ve got that too—but trust us when we tell you the Pineapple has a cult following for a reason.
properties.name: Rocky Mountain Brewery

properties.blurb: The combo of pizza, beer, and sports is pretty much always a slam dunk. Rocky Mountain Pizzeria knocks it out of the park with their collection of Colorado sports memorabilia and their commitment to showing every game imaginable on the bar’s numerous TVs. Ready for the hat trick? In the summer, they have live music every Friday on their patio. Chant it with us now: MVP! MVP!
properties.name: Rocky Mountain Pizzeria

properties.blurb: Wagyu burgers. Seasonal produce. Tap-tending. No, we’re not playing a delicious game of Mad-Libs. We’re describing Rocky Mountain Tap & Garden, an upscale beer emporium that boasts the best of Colorado craft beer. Skip the bar line with one of their 30 self-serve beer taps, or check out the market menu for dishes featuring seasonal produce from a local Boulder farm. Rocky Mountain also offers a premium selection of wines and carefully crafted mixed drinks, because of course they do.
properties.name: Rocky Mountain Tap & Garden

properties.blurb: Founded in 1999 by a quartet of Colorado-raised siblings, Rockyard Brewing Co proudly stands as the enduring brewpub gem of Douglas County. With a rich history and a dedication to quality, Rockyard has emerged as a true pioneer in the craft beer scene. Their brews have earned esteemed accolades from renowned beer competitions like the Great American Beer Festival, World Beer Cup, and the Colorado State Fair. These medals of distinction are a testament to Rockyard's unwavering commitment to crafting exceptional beers that capture the essence of Colorado's vibrant brewing culture. Beyond their liquid triumphs, Rockyard offers a full-fledged culinary experience. Their menu is a symphony of flavors, featuring a tempting array of appetizers, nourishing soups and salads, mouthwatering burgers, satisfying sandwiches, and enticing entrees.
properties.name: Rockyard Brewing Co

properties.address: 509 W McDowell Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85003, USA
properties.blurb: Roses by the Stairs isn’t just about brewing great beer (though, trust us, they’ve got that covered). It’s about doing it the right way. Sourcing local grains, seasonal fruits, and high-quality ingredients that give back to the land and the community. Every pour here carries a purpose, with 1% of revenue donated to environmental causes and plenty of love shown to local artists, businesses, and neighbors along the way. The taproom itself feels like an extension of that mission: inclusive, welcoming, and buzzing with creativity. Come for a pint that tastes as good as it feels, stay for the sense of connection that lingers long after the glass is empty.
properties.name: Roses by the Stairs Brewing

properties.address: 2727 S Broad Ave Suite #103, New Orleans, LA 70125, USA
properties.name: Roulaison Distilling Co

properties.address: 101 W Austin St, Round Top, TX 78954, USA
properties.blurb: Tucked into Texas’ favorite tiny town, Round Top Brewing brings small-batch craft beer to the heart of the countryside. With house-made brews on tap alongside a rotating lineup of guest beers, there’s always something new to discover. Live music sets the scene, and a solid food menu keeps you fueled while you soak it all in. Whether you’re a local or just passing through, this cozy brewery offers a laid-back spot to kick back, grab a pint, and enjoy the good vibes.
properties.name: Round Top Brewing & Kitchen

properties.blurb: This brewery has a warning on its website: “micro-brewed beer has a lot of flavor and may cause you to actually taste your beer!” And Rowland’s is as full of history as it is cheek: these walls once housed a fire station, a city hall, a dress shop, and a tavern. The place is lined with family photos and eclectic, one-of-a-kind items, including a mirror that dates back to 1926. Pop in for a cold brew and warm vibes, or take a growler home with you.
properties.name: Rowland's Calumet Brewing Co

properties.address: 7366 Townsend Pl Building B, New Orleans, LA 70126
properties.blurb: The best things in life are conceived in Creole cottages. Exhibit A: Our cousin Cosmo. Exhibit B: Royal Brewery. Unpretentious, unassuming, and unconventional, the brainchild of Raymond and Mandy Pumilia has become like a second home to yours truly. Hunker down on the outdoor patio or belly up at the tasting room and cheers a glass of Culicidae American Pale Ale in honor of Louisiana’s unofficial state bird.
properties.name: Royal Brewery New Orleans

properties.blurb: This neighborhood pub has everything we love–a rotating tap list that includes local brews, a menu with some seriously mouth-watering offerings, and a laid-back vibe that’ll keep you coming back for more. Our perfect order starts with an IPA and some garlic cheese curds, followed by an ale and a platter of fish and chips (the pal we’ve dragged along promises to share a bite of their Shea Burger). The staff are truly committed to creating a welcoming atmosphere, so go ahead and order one more.
properties.name: Royal Hilltop

properties.blurb: When you talk local watering hole, you’re talking RPJ’SS. Next door to a tailor, it’s cash only and on weekdays they wrap up by 10pm. Needless to say, pretension does not live here. Friendly staff, whiskey and Jag tap machines, a pool table...what more does one need? We say put life’s troubles on hold with a Pool Party Lemon Ale from 3 Sheep Brewing.
properties.name: RPJ'SS

properties.blurb: Ruby Beach Brewing is all about locally sourced, adventurous beers that keep the Jacksonville craft scene interesting. Grab a Scallywag Golden Sour or take a gamble on the ever-changing Sour Du Jour. Think flavors that rotate from mango to lychee and beyond. With a modern taproom and outdoor Jaguars viewing parties, it’s a great spot to sip, cheer, and enjoy some local flavor.
properties.name: Ruby Beach Brewing

properties.blurb: You know Rudolph's for legendary BBQ. You may not know that Rudolph's also has a commitment to craft beers you can pair with those lick-the-plate-clean ribs. You're welcome.
properties.name: Rudolphs Bar-B-Que

properties.blurb: Embark on a flavor-filled journey at Rule105, where craft beers take center stage. From stouts and porters to IPAs, pale ales, and lagers, each brew is meticulously crafted and gluten-reduced for your enjoyment. With live music, delicious wood-fired pizzas, wings, and pretzel balls, along with trivia nights and local club gatherings, Rule105 offers a vibrant and engaging experience. Let their friendly staff guide you to the perfect beer while you soak in the lively atmosphere. Join them for a memorable time filled with tasty bites, live tunes, and amazing brews!
properties.name: Rule 105 Brewing

properties.address: 4800 W Hillsboro Blvd # A12, Coconut Creek, FL 33073, USA
properties.blurb: Rule G Brewing really does have it all. Whether you're craving cold beer, tasty snacks, or indulging in some Pie & Pints, they've got you covered. With live music, events, and seltzers or wine for the non-beer lovers, there's something for everyone. Plus, with Sunday happy hour specials during football season, you’ll find yourself making this spot a regular hangout. They’ve combined all the best things into one place, so come for the drinks and stay for the good vibes—you might never want to leave!
properties.name: Rule G Brewing Co

properties.address: N7454 Summit Rd, Plymouth, WI 53073, USA
properties.name: Rumors Roadhouse BBQ

properties.address: 4721 S Orange Ave, Orlando, FL 32806, USA
properties.blurb: Beer. Oysters. Pizza. Burgers. Charcuterie boards. Do we really need to say more? Runabout Brewing is basically a greatest-hits playlist of everything you actually want to eat and drink. Cold brews, a raw bar, and wood-fired bites mean you’re set for the night. Whether you came hungry, thirsty, or both. Honestly, the only thing left to ask is why you’re still reading this instead of already grabbing a pint and a dozen oysters. Go on, we’ll save you a seat.
properties.name: Runabout Brewing

properties.blurb: Saddle Mountain has plenty of great beers, but their Clan-Destine Scottish Ale is the true standout. Once just a seasonal, it’s now their most-brewed beer thanks to five GABF medals in six years (and fans demanding it year-round). Pair a pint with one of their killer craft burgers—or go full Homer Simpson and grab hot buttermilk donuts for the table.
properties.name: Saddle Mountain Brewing Co

properties.address: 827 Shaver Rd NE, Cedar Rapids, IA 52402
properties.blurb: Sipping some beer by a body of water is what we consider “living the dream”. Now add pizza to the mix, on a patio? I think we are in heaven. Stop by after a bike ride (or just come in your athleisure attire and no one will know the difference. That’s what we’ll be doing) for some trivia or to catch any Iowa game you can think of. You can find us here, watching the sunset over the lake with a tall boy in hand.
properties.name: Sag Wagon

properties.blurb: If you climb a mountain you get to start a brewery, them's the rules. At least around here. Sahale Peak is perhaps not the place to start if you're an amateur climber. A more reasonable ascent? The Summit — a tastng flight of all ten of Sahale's taps. You might need to warm up tho. Use your PubPass for a Golden Ale or Wheatsconsin Weiss and prepare for glory.
properties.name: Sahale Ale Works

properties.address: 228 W King St Ste A, St. Augustine, FL 32084, USA
properties.blurb: Set sail with Sailbird Distilling, where every bottle is a true labor of love. Each spirit is crafted in small batches of just 20-25 bottles, ensuring the highest quality and attention to detail. From smooth vodkas to bold whiskeys, aromatic gins, rich rums, and unique liqueurs, their selection offers something special for every palate.
