At which point we’ll order another Hazy Rabbit IPA and a side of Crispy Brussels Sprouts. Oh and don't sleep on their brunch. It's worth a weekend stop!
properties.name: Stubby's Gastrogrub & Beer Bar

properties.address: 782 W Montrose St, Clermont, FL 34711, USA
properties.blurb: Sud’s Bottleshop and Saloon is proof that size doesn’t matter, at least when it comes to bars. This cozy little spot brings big beer energy with 120 rotating taps that keep things fresh every time you walk in. One-off brews? Check. Hard-to-find pours you’ll be bragging about later? Absolutely. With a lineup this stacked, you’ll never need to drink the same thing twice (unless you really, really want to).
properties.name: Sud's Bottleshop & Saloon

properties.address: 793 W Montrose St, Clermont, FL 34711, USA
properties.blurb:
After exploring the Montrose Street Market food hall, you’ll want to swing by Suds Saloon to wash it all down. With more beers than we could sample in one visit, you’ll definitely find yourself coming back for more. Feeling re-energized after your pint? Maybe now's the time to try a second round—or even a third! After all, beer makes everything better, even that second slice of pizza you weren't sure about.
*** Deal good for One BOGO Draft ***
properties.name: Sud's Saloon

properties.blurb: Sudhouse is a low-key mom and pop bar that embraces the spirit of our nation’s capitol - freedom, equality, and ice cold beer (forgive us, it’s been a minute since U.S. History class). The freedom to choose your appetizers and draft beer is the kind of thing our forefathers fought for. Okay, that might be a bit of an exaggeration, but when there’s a giant mural of Abe Lincoln looming over you, it’s impossible not to engage in life, liberty, and the pursuit of happy hour.
properties.name: Sudhouse

properties.blurb: Cider with fresh, local ingredients including apples, honey, blueberries, and more.Nine barrel aged rum with a spicy sweetness on the front end, and a long oaky finish. A jammy, balanced and endlessly quaffable Syrah. Need we say more? Probably not, but the Soltau Brothers have in our expert opinion carved out one of the obligatory distillery visits every time we cross into Louisiana, and our palette continually asks us to return.
properties.name: Sugarfield Spirits

properties.address: 1543 NE Douglas St, Lee's Summit, MO 64086
properties.blurb: Summit Pizza is for those of us who grew up going to that chain pizza joint with the salad bar and mini pizzas. If you’re nostalgic for creating a unique salad combination, go ahead and embrace your inner child and indulge in the lunch buffet - your more sophisticated palate will thank you for it. And as long as you don’t have to go back to work, or maybe even if you do, we’re not here to judge, you may as well indulge in a bevvie. Just a little something to wash that Summit Supreme pizza down.
properties.name: Summit Pizza Co

properties.blurb: SunUp Brewing Company is one of the oldest, independently owned breweries in Arizona. Needless to say, this little place is a Phoenix staple. They have a healthy lineup of hand-crafted Cask Ales, ranging from traditional to, well, whatever the hell Brewer Uwe feels like brewing. Don't get us wrong—we like our Cask Ales. But it's tough to top SunUp's White Russian Imperial Coffee Stout. It's like a sugar rush, caffeine fix, and beer buzz in one.
properties.name: Sun Up Bar

properties.address: 8665 Argent St Suite B, Santee, CA 92071, USA
properties.name: Sunny Grove Brewing Co

properties.blurb: From humble roots and hard work comes this newcomer to the Colorado beer scene and we're here for it. The folks at Sunroom care about the beers you're about to drink — right down to the names of the brews themselves. So try a Casa Blanca or a Colorado Dreams and ask them what's coming soon. Something tells us they'll be as fantastic as the ones we just tried.
properties.name: Sunroom Brewing

properties.address: 121 S Main St, Lake Mills, WI 53551
properties.blurb: Roll into Lake Mills and the first stop should be Sunshine Brew Company, a stone’s throw from Rock Lake. We had some of the freshest-tasting jackfruit tacos you can get east of the Mississippi. Brewed on site, their beers give everyone something to love. Friendly bar staff introduced us to a slamming amber with the name Wisco Sun as well as the crisp n’ cold Sunrise Reach pale. Very bright and fun decor almost has a tropical vibe. Perhaps as tropical as one gets in southeastern Wisconsin.
properties.name: Sunshine Brewing Co

properties.blurb: We are so thrilled that a DC spot threw their hat in the ring when it comes to crafting cider. At Supreme Core Cider, there’s a lineup of flagship ciders but if you’re lucky enough to drop in when a seasonal is on tap, just lean in and go for it; the intriguing flavor combos are mouth-watering. We’re for sure adding Pounda Gold and Ginger Lime to our flight, due to their superior crushability.
properties.name: Supreme Core Cider

properties.blurb: Surly’s larger-than-life Beer Hall is back and better than ever. Sit down, scan a QR code and behold a myriad of choices from pints to plates. The Hog Frites, Pretzel, and Pierogis are still our go-to snacks. In the mood for pizza? They’ve got you covered upstairs with some spectacular New Haven-style pies. We’re partial to the Walter White and ‘I’m Your Daddy’ but you do you, boss.
properties.name: Surly Brewing Co

properties.blurb: You know those adorable small town farms you see depicted in movies? Sutliff Farm and Cider House is that place. It’s the very best of Iowa, highlighting its beautiful landscape, its abundance of fresh produce, and its friendly people. The cider is refreshing and the seasonal flavors continue to impress us. Settle in with your family and friends to enjoy live music, sip on a cider, and be the main character in your very own charming movie.
properties.name: Sutliff Farm and Cider House

properties.address: 1943 Fowler Street, Enter off, Hough St, Fort Myers, FL 33901, USA
properties.name: Swamp Cat Brewing Co

properties.blurb: If you are looking for a place with not only the best Iowa breweries on tap, but some of the best food we’ve tasted AND a place to play pool, look no further than Sweet Caroline’s. Might we suggest a Boone Valley Roxie Irish Red with their delicious homemade chips and ranch dip while you school your friend in a game of shuffleboard. Meanwhile, you can find us at the bar watching the Iowa/Iowa State games.
properties.name: Sweet Caroline's

properties.address: 1020 E Lincoln Ave, Fort Collins, CO 80524
properties.blurb: With space for a crowd and plenty of drink options, this is about to become your go-to spot. SweetWater’s beer menu is extensive, including some like the Point Fly available only in their taproom. But wait, there’s more! Hard tea, hard seltzer, and a vegan almond milk stout means everyone can raise a glass. A corner of the brewery is dedicated to merch and beer to-go, so you’ll never leave empty-handed.
properties.name: SweetWater Brewing (Fort Collins)

properties.address: 122 S Main St, River Falls, WI 54022
properties.blurb: It’s a scientific fact that when the word “pub” precedes a food item, it’s going to be the most delicious thing you’ve ever put in your food hole. For example, pub pretzel. Or how about this? Pub nachos. Paired with an IPA or one of the other various yet equally tasty brews on tap and it’s simply *chef’s kiss.* Swinging Bridge Brewery knows exactly what they’re doing, offering up creative craft beers that will keep even the savviest beer drinker on their toes.
properties.name: Swinging Bridge Brewery

properties.blurb: You know it’s a Wisconsin bar when they have branded flannels and a beer named Pontoon Pounder. SwitchGear Brewing set up shop in the historic Feed Mill building in 2017 and they’ve been serving up delicious craft beers in flights and pints ever since. For serious beer lovers, they’ve got membership options that get you a free birthday crowler, discounts on merch, and other sweet perks.
properties.name: SwitchGear Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Get ready to dive into the spirited realm of Syntax Distillery and Cocktail Bar, where real Colorado magic happens. They take the grain-to-glass craft spirits game to a whole new level. They've got their sources locked down, with grains handpicked from trusted local farmers within a 100-mile radius and sugar cane molasses snatched as close as possible - straight from the sunny shores of Florida. Now, let's raise a glass to their mind-blowing cocktails. Crafted with their very own spirits (of course!), these liquid marvels will have your taste buds dancing with joy. With a dazzling array of flavors to explore, you'll keep coming back for more, because life's too short to miss out on all the spirited fun at Syntax Distillery and Cocktail Bar!
properties.name: Syntax Distillery and Cocktail Bar

properties.address: 2190 S Platte River Dr, Denver, CO 80223, USA
properties.name: Table Public House

properties.address: 1676 Union Blvd, Bay Shore, NY 11706, USA
properties.name: Take Two Brewery

properties.blurb: You can easily spend the whole day at Takoma Beverage Co. and honestly, why wouldn’t you? Start with expertly crafted coffee and breakfast in the morning, then ease into the afternoon with fresh, flavorful lunch and dinner options. When the workday winds down (or even a little before, we won’t judge), you’ve got a solid list of beer, wine, and cocktails to explore. It’s the kind of spot that feels right from sunrise to last call.
properties.name: Takoma Beverage Co

properties.blurb: Prepare for a hoppy adventure at Tall Trellis Brew Co., where the air is filled with the tantalizing aroma of hops and the views of their majestic trellises will leave you in awe. Step inside and get ready to embark on a flavor-packed journey. Their Cruisin' The Fe Pale Ale is a modern masterpiece, brewed with oats and wheat for that perfect hazy goodness. But it's not just about the beer here, folks. Their full menu boasts an incredible selection of sharables, mouthwatering flatbreads, and smoked meats that'll have you saying "abs or squeal?" So, grab a pint, savor the sights, and indulge in the delicious offerings of Tall Trellis Brew Co. Cheers to good times and great brews!
properties.name: Tall Trellis Brew Co

properties.blurb: You want a casual place to grab a beer and a bite and bring your dog? OK, you’re asking a lot but luckily, we have just the spot! Tamm Avenue Bar has a superb beer selection to go with some of the best burgers and sandwiches around (courtesy of their neighbor, Nomad). Also the neighborhood’s largest dog-friendly patio. Also an arcade room. And live music. …Need we go on?
properties.name: Tamm Avenue Bar

properties.blurb: Discover the lively world of Tampa Bay Brewing Company, where a trio of core beers, along with a lively rotation of five seasonal brews, and an array of annually released and specialty beers await your palate. But this place isn't just about fantastic beers. Brace yourself for a culinary journey that transcends ordinary pub fare, featuring a menu that's nothing short of extraordinary. Prepare to have your taste buds tantalized, as many of their dishes are infused with the very beers that make this brewery famous. Among their lineup, consider trying the Reef Donkey. It's more than just a beer; it's a companion to the vibrant Florida lifestyle. Whether you're out on an adventure or just soaking up the sun, this beer is your ultimate outdoor partner. With its refreshing lemon-lime aroma, bursts of tropical flavors, and a touch of light maltiness, the Reef Donkey APA embodies the spirit of Florida in every sip.
properties.name: Tampa Bay Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Tampa Bay Brewing doesn’t play favorites when it comes to craft beers. They brew hoppy IPAs, expressive sours, and fruity Florida-Weisse at their Westchase and Ybor locations. The former has 24 constantly rotating taps, a sprawling outdoor patio with fire pits, and a covered bar. Oh, and a full-service eatery specializing in beer-infused fare. After all, why should you limit your beer to liquids?
properties.name: Tampa Bay Brewing Co (Westchase)

properties.address: 4910 S Newport St, Denver, CO 80237, USA
properties.name: Tap & Burger Belleview Station

properties.address: 1565 N Raleigh St #100, Denver, CO 80204, USA
properties.name: Tap & Burger Sloan's Lake

properties.address: 515 Mouton St Suite 103, Baton Rouge, LA 70806
properties.blurb: Tap 65 takes its name seriously, with 65 whiskies and 65 beers, seltzers, and wines on their self-pour tap wall. They also serve upscale Indian fusion and have a full bar dedicated to hand-crafted cocktails featuring innovative recipes with an Indian flair. But they don’t have 65 of them. It’s okay, that would probably be overkill. Probably.
properties.name: TAP 65
Tap Dragon Craft Beer & Wine Bar

properties.address: 835 S Gilbert Rd Ste. 101, Gilbert, AZ 85296
properties.blurb: As mythical creatures go? It’s hard to top a dragon. As dragons go? It’s impossible to top one that refers to itself as a ‘Thirst Responder’, delivering the best in craft beer right to your glass. This brand new spot promises to keep pints and hearts full. Want to explore some non-beer lands? The dragon will oblige with wine, cider and even mead. So stop down to Tap Dragon and breathe a little excitement into your evening!
properties.name: Tap Dragon Craft Beer & Wine Bar

properties.blurb: With a recent changing of the guard in ownership, Tap on the Rocks has won over the locals, and of course when you have nice pastoral views, stellar beer, and wood-fired pizzas, you’ll win over Pub Pass too. Rolling through Morrison around breakfast time makes Tap on the Rocks a savvy stop as well, with scrambles and burritos packed with tasty goodnes
properties.name: Tap On The Rocks

properties.address: 13000 W Indian School Rd suite a-6, Litchfield Park, AZ 85340
properties.blurb: It isn’t just a clever name, these folks really are Tap Savvy. With an ever changing tap list you will always be able to find something to please the palate here. With most taps being from the best local breweries we couldn’t help but settle in for the evening. Don’t worry, after a few we ordered some delicious snacks to keep us going.
properties.name: Tap Savvy

properties.address: 6047 U.S. Hwy 17-92 N, Davenport, FL 33896, USA
properties.name: Tap That Beer House

properties.address: 363 Delaware St suite 300, Tonawanda, NY 14150, USA
properties.name: Tap That Tap Room
Tap This! Bar and Brewing Co.

properties.blurb: So you’ve checked out Tap Savvy before and you thought to yourself, I want this, but in Avondale, AND cushy seating… Well my friends, we have some great news. Tap2 is here for you. Owned by the same incredible team that is laser-focused on bringing you the best of the best tap beers, Tap2, after a few silly delays, is now open and ready to serve you! We thought the tap list was long at Tap Savvy, but buckle up my friends. You’re in for a treat!
properties.name: Tap2 Premium Beer & Wine Lounge

properties.address: 2000 El Cajon Blvd, San Diego, CA 92104, USA
properties.name: TapRoom Beer Company

properties.blurb: Get your hands dirty and pour your own beer at Taps on Main. With 40 taps available on their pour-your-own-beer wall, you can sample as much (or as little) as your taste buds desire. And if beer isn’t your thing we’re very confused and have a lot of follow-up questions, but you can also order from a full menu of craft cocktails — and try the best Buffalo style wings in the city.
properties.name: Taps on Main

properties.blurb: Tarpon River Brewing’s spacious taproom is the ultimate spot to kick back and indulge. Start with a crisp Native Lager and dive into their menu of flavorful brews. Whether you're pairing the Good as Gold Golden Ale with some Phish Tacos or matching the Inzane American Light Lager with their Loaded Nachos, every bite and sip is a win. With an ever-expanding beer list and a menu to match, this is one Fort Lauderdale hangout where you really can't go wrong.
properties.name: Tarpon River Brewing

properties.blurb: Tarpon River's spacious taproom is one of our favorite places to spread out and dig in. Start with a Native Lager and get ready for a trip to Flavor Country as you work your way around their beer list and menu. Pair a Pink Slip (Tarpon's delightfully tart Berliner) with some Buffalo Cauliflower or Chorizo Queso. Then? Pick an IPA to chase the IPA Mac Burger. What's that? Didn't plan on napping this afternoon? Sorry, boss. With a lineup like this? A lot of us are changing our plans.
properties.name: Tarpon River Brewing

properties.address: 715 Fourth Ave, San Diego, CA 92101, USA
properties.name: Taste & Thirst

properties.address: 3200 NE 12th Ave, Oakland Park, FL 33334, USA
properties.blurb: Hold onto your sombrero because Tatts & Tacos Beer Garden is where the fiesta never stops! Just picture this: esquite, queso, fried avocados, tacos, burritos, chimichangas – we could go on and on about their mouthwatering Mexican delights. And the best part? The party keeps going with nightly specials that'll keep you coming back for more. But what's a fiesta without drinks? They've got a lineup of beer and margaritas that'll make every night a celebration.
properties.name: Tatts & Tacos Beer Garden

properties.blurb: Architecturally modern, the Tavern Lowry is not your Uncle Frank’s sports bar. That place was condemned and we’re not allowed to talk about it, especially to Frank. But Tavern Lowry is sharp as a 3 thousand dollar suit. Highlights from the menu were  the  Volcano Burger with pepper jack, honey peppered bacon, fried jalapeño coins, chipotle BBQ sauce and the Beer-Battered Fish n Chips with coleslaw. Grab a brew out on the beautiful patio with a circular gazebo bar. Sorry Frank, but this is how it should be.
properties.name: Tavern Lowery

properties.blurb: Build your own appetite, then come here and build your own burger while you build your table's own collection of empty pint glasses.
properties.name: Tavern On France

properties.blurb: Come for the pizza. Stay for the pizza. Tavolio’s reputation among Milwaukee pizza aficionados has been as good as goWith a smartly curated wine list, six drafts to choose from like the crisp yet malty Raised Guitar City lager from Raise Grain, Tavolino is a sneakily-professional choice for a classy date night. Pasta dishes like the Cacio e Pepe and the pappardelle with wine-braised boar are good alternatives if you’re not in a pizza mood, but we say give both a shot.
properties.name: Tavolino

properties.address: 103 W Honeysuckle St, Litchfield Park, AZ 85340
properties.blurb: This Litchfield favorite is doing creative things with funky ingredients and the results are outstanding. Everything coming out of the taps is fresh and crisp and you can’t drink just one. Which is why flights were invented. Duh. The taps might lean IPA heavy, but snag a Mexican lager or sour when it appears, you won’t be disappointed. And neither will your pup, because it’s invited to one of our favorite dog-friendly tap rooms. But please remember, dogs drink water, not IPAs.
properties.name: TCBC Beer

properties.address: 209 N Cleveland Ave, Loveland, CO 80537, USA
properties.name: TeeShots

properties.blurb: Calling all history buffs to come check out Ten Twelve Main. With good food and amazing drinks in this beautiful historic building, could you really want anything more? Once you step inside you’ll feel transported to a simpler time and with the cold beer flowing you’ll forget all those troubles. Not really into history? That’s okay, too. With an expansive and ever growing food menu there is something for everyone. And the drinks? Also always changing with rotating taps from the best of the local beer scene. Trust us, this is a must stop when you’re downtown historic Lexington.
properties.name: Ten Twelve Main

properties.blurb: At Ten10 Brewing Co., the taps are always flowing with fresh, rotating brews that keep things interesting and keep you coming back. It’s all about small-batch flavor and laid-back neighborhood vibes, the kind of place where one pint quickly turns into two. Pro tip: Say the words "Jumbo Smoked Wings" to your server. Trust us, your taste buds will thank you.
properties.name: Ten10 Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Tennessee Brew Works “works hard to create high quality craft beer that makes Tennessee proud. Our culture places importance on family, friends, and community, and we hope you’ll be a part of it.” and let us just say, we absolutely want to be a part of it. They understand the importance of brewing with integrity, only using natural ingredients and are sourcing them from local farmers whenever possible. On top of the quality Tennessee Brew Works also gives back through their State Park Blonde Ale which benefits the TSP Conservancy. We could go on and on about their expansive beer and their incredible snacks and entrees, but you’ll just have to go experience it for yourself as we are running out of room…
properties.name: Tennessee Brew Works

properties.blurb: Prepare to be transported by Aztec-inspired brews without needing to sacrifice anything—except maybe your previous beer loyalties. This San Marco favorite, named after the Aztec god of jaguars, offers a rotating selection of easy-drinking cervezas, ensuring there’s always something on tap for everyone. Pair your pint with their mouthwatering tacos, nachos, or esquite, but make sure to leave room for their epic chicken and waffle burrito with hot sauce-infused maple syrup. And don’t even think about skipping the caramel-stuffed churros con chocolate—they’re worth every bite.
properties.name: Tepeyolot Cerveceria

properties.blurb: test description
properties.name: test bar 1234 test bar

properties.blurb: At Textile Brewing, they're brewing up a storm right in the heart of their building's basement. Picture this: you're sinking your teeth into a massive Bavarian pretzel or munching on some pretzel bites, all while sipping on their exclusive brews. And guess what? These brews are like the hidden treasure of the beer world, only found in their taprooms. Whether you're dining in, grabbing takeout, or swinging by for curbside service, they've got you covered. Oh, and did we mention they've got a dog-friendly Biergarten? Yep, it's decked out with umbrellas and firepits, making it the paw-fect spot for you and your furry friends to chill.
properties.name: Textile Brewing Co

properties.blurb: If we were Phoenix dwellers full-time, we’d definitely chill at The 1227 Taproom. A lot. It’s hot here, and it’s important to stay hydrated during all the seasons - summer and almost-summer. What’s not to love about the 1227 Taproom? There’s a solid rotation of local food trucks to pair with local and regional beers on tap. Beers that promise to keep you hydrated. If hydration is not enough motivation, savvy locals love it for the live music and comedy shows. You’re guaranteed a good time. Also maybe drink some water.
properties.name: The 1227 Taproom

properties.blurb: "30 is the number of beers on tap at The Abbey. It’s also the number of burgers you’ll want to order as soon as you take that first juicy bite. De Pere’s “Original Neighborhood Bar & Grill” also offers pizzas, wraps, sandwiches, and salads as well as bar classics like the Blooming Onion and Loaded Baked Potato. Now we’ve gone and made ourselves hungry. "
properties.name: The Abbey

properties.blurb: Does your soul long to be comforted by the classic hospitality of an Irish pub? But do you also find yourself with a hankering for a tasty quesadilla or chicken wings? Well here you’ll find the best of both worlds. Whether it’s fish & chips, shepherd's pie, or pork green chili fries…they’re serving it up hot and tasty for you. And who says you can’t have a perfectly poured Guinness while you’re eating a baja fish wrap? We certainly didn’t.
properties.name: The Abbey Tavern

properties.blurb: Intimately sized, the Alley packs a lot of punch into their square footage. Live music and even open mic nights dot the weekly calendar, all of which is proudly free of a cover charge. And while they have a half dozen taps of Colorado cold ones, they kill at the margarita game. That should go hand in hand with the Mountain style Mexican food packed with smokey, cheesy goodness. This is the best alley we’ve ever blindly walked into.
properties.name: The Alley

properties.address: 316 Centre St, Fernandina Beach, FL 32034, USA
properties.blurb: They don't just hold the line with their commitment to doing it right, they raise the bar! This place is a masterclass in doing everything to a high standard, and they don't cut any corners – from the brewing process to the restaurant and the outstanding food. It's a true labor of love, and they pour their heart and soul into every glass they serve you.Small batch brewing is their jam, and let us tell you, these brewers have got some serious talent! Each sip is a testament to their passion and dedication. The tap list is a delightful dance of flavors, so make sure to visit often, as it's always changing – just like your cravings for their fantastic brews!
properties.name: The Alley by AIBC

properties.address: 15350 E Smoky Hill Rd, Aurora, CO 80015
properties.name: The Angry Clover

properties.blurb: The Backyard is the perfect spot for apartment dwellers who don’t have room for amazing patio furniture. Or for homeowners whose backyards are boring and don’t have immediate access to an awesome craft beer lineup or live music or jalapeno pretzel bites. Like many backyards, you can expect to find kiddos and pups running around, which just adds to the lively atmosphere. This is most definitely not the place for you if you hate fun, which is practically guaranteed because of the puppies.
properties.name: The Backyard

properties.blurb: Ever found a bar that just makes sense? Like, there’s nothing special about it, but it draws you in and keeps you there? This is one of those places for us. We came for a beer and before we knew it we were shooting Jameson, playing dice with the bartender and a guy we just met offered us some of the burgers he brought to grill (yep. you can cook your own meat here. sharing optional.). If you don’t have a backyard? You do now.
properties.name: The Backyard

properties.blurb: Vegan we may not be - please see our normal order of the Hunter’s Pie - but we would be lying if we didn’t admit that the Curry Cranberry Chickpea Wraps are out of this world. And don’t even get us started on the bowl of Green Chili. But we will gladly wax on poetically about the Sea Salt Caramel Cheesecake for hours if given the chance. Pair that with their French Silk Stout and you’ll be feeling like you live in a world of decadence.
properties.name: The Bakers' Brewery

properties.address: 606 Lime Kiln Rd, Green Bay, WI 54302
properties.blurb: Imagine being so confident in your bar that you can just name it The Bar. It’s not cocky if it’s true, right? The Bar offers an extensive lineup of beers, with locals from the likes of New Glarus on draft and plenty more to choose from. The menu options are plentiful and the folks at The Bar know what Green Bay likes to eat - wings, burgers, and buffalo mac and cheese. Okay, maybe the last one is specific to our PubPass taste buds, but whatever. The Bar is The Place to watch the Packers take down their rivals.
properties.name: The Bar

properties.blurb: A hand-picked tap list that runs both deep and thoughtful awaits your arrival at The Barley Oak! You might just walk away with a new favorite beer or two. So much to explore from across Mandeville or across the globe. Better get some food to fuel your investigation. We started with the Cajun Sausage Platter (that creole mustard sauce… damn!) and a local Hazy as we tried to decide between the Brisket Sandwich or a Reuben. Trick question we split both. The sustenance helped us delve even deeper into our delicious beerducation.
properties.name: The Barley Oak

properties.blurb: The Bearded Brewer describes itself as “focused on creative and aggressive styles.” And it is aggressive. Aggressive with its stellar brewing technique. Aggressive with finding the perfect names for its craft beers. Aggressive with grilling up delicious specialty burgers. And aggressively worth a return trip for more of all the things.
properties.name: The Bearded Brewer

properties.blurb: A rock solid tap list is just the beginning at one of our favorite watering and grazing holes. This is bar food done right, so arrive with an appetite. We recommend the Buffalo Chicken Mac, the Gyro or the Bacon Cheeseburger — and those are just pizzas! Wait ’til you see the rest of the menu. From apps to salads to burgers and sandos, you’re more than covered at The Beerhouse. Did we order too many things? We’re gonna need more beer to soak up. Or extra friends…
properties.name: The Beerhouse

properties.blurb: Having a drink at The Berry & Rye has all the glamour of the Roaring ’20s without, you know, the whole Prohibition thing. Omaha’s oldest craft cocktail lounge is classy as they come, with exposed brick interior, plush seats, and crystal chandeliers. The bartenders use seasonal ingredients to build the drink menu around classic cocktails with a twist. Go on, old sport, order another round.
properties.name: The Berry & Rye

properties.address: 3325 Center Point Rd NE, Cedar Rapids, IA 52402
properties.blurb: “Don’t Stop Believing” that you can find the best spot for karaoke on a Friday night at The Blind Pig. While you’re there belting your favorite jams you can chow down on some grub from their huge menu and sip some of the best local brews. Are you planning on going on a Sunday instead? Catch every NFL game and on a nice day watch from their oversized patio. Honestly we couldn’t ask for more.
properties.name: The Blind Pig

properties.blurb: TBH, they had us at “pork belly cheese fries,” but that’s just one of many reasons to visit The Blue Duck. Maybe for you it’ll be the duck fried rice. Or the Slamburger, an always over-the-top combo that changes weekly. Or the—I’m sorry, is that a Duck Glazed Manhattan cocktail we spy? We're not drooling, you’re drooling!
properties.name: The Blue Duck

properties.blurb: Looking for a hockey haven in Kansas City? The Blue Line's got you covered. It's one of the rare spots where die-hard hockey fans can find themselves in good company, cheering on the game while sipping some of the best local KC brews. With beers flowing like a perfect assist and TVs showing the latest face-offs, it’s a hat trick of good times. You’ll skate through their tap list, crack open a cold one, and maybe even shout, "He shoots, he scores!" before the final horn sounds.
properties.name: The Blue Line Hockey Bar

properties.blurb: You came for a beer. You stayed for a bourbon from their unrivaled list. Then you ordered a pizza, garlic knots, another bourbon, and two more beers. Oh, wait. That was us.
properties.name: The Bluegrass Lounge

properties.blurb: No night out on KK is complete without a session at The Bottle. The building itself is worth the trip. It’s had many lives — from an Odd Fellows hall to a Schlitz-owned bowling alley — but one thing remains the same: the urinals. You have to check them out (sorry ladies). But before you do? Work up some pressure with a few cold ones and ask the barkeep to spin a yarn or two about the saloon you’re sitting in. Chances are you’ll learn some useless trivia you can share in the crazy cool game room later!
properties.name: The Bottle

properties.blurb: No night out on KK is complete without a session at The Bottle. The building itself is worth the trip. It’s had many lives — from an Odd Fellows hall to a Schlitz-owned bowling alley — but one thing remains the same: the urinals. You have to check them out (sorry ladies). But before you do? Work up some pressure with a few cold ones and ask the barkeep to spin a yarn or two about the saloon you’re sitting in. Chances are you’ll learn some useless trivia you can share in the crazy cool game room later!
properties.name: The Bottle

properties.blurb: Brass Monkey is easily one of our favorite Old Towne drinkeries, bar none. Awesome atmosphere, 36 craft beers on tap, 70 more in bottles and cans, plus Sunday Fundays that include live music and a crawfish boil. But Sundays aren’t the only days worthy of a visit. Mondays just got a whole lot better with $3 pints. Wednesdays call for BINGO. And Thursdays always have something fun in store: trivia, movies, tie-dye nights, and more.
properties.name: The Brass Monkey

properties.address: 13271 Bass Pro Dr Ste 110, Colorado Springs, CO 80921
properties.blurb: We could spend all day here, trying to choose from the gigantic selection of local craft beers. In fact, we have! Which is how we know The Brass Tap lives up to its mission of being the Ultimate Beer Bar. But, as they say, seeing is believing, so come peep the tap list for yourself, and order some tasty bar bites while you’re at it. You can thank us later.
properties.name: The Brass Tap

properties.blurb: Backpocket Kitty Lime Pie. Barn Town Swish and Flick. Confluence The Pineapple Banana Wizard. No, we’re not in the middle of a hungry person’s Mad Libs game – we’re naming some of The Brass Tap’s many creative brews! If you’re a classic beer person, don’t worry: they also have Bud Light. In addition to their many beers, The Brass Tap also has Tuesday Trivia, Music Bingo, and more potential after-work activities.
properties.name: The Brass Tap

properties.address: 1205 W Mt Royal Ave, Baltimore, MD 21217
properties.blurb: Baltimore’s beer scene just got a little better, thanks to The Brass Tap. With over 60 taps and a rotating selection of local and national brews, it’s the kind of place where decisions are tough, but there are no wrong answers. Pair your pint with their drool-worthy pub fare—think juicy burgers, crispy wings, and shareable apps. And hey, if you happen to get into a debate about which local brewery is best, just grab another round. It’s Baltimore; it’s all good.
properties.name: The Brass Tap (Baltimore)

properties.address: 28010 Race Track Rd, Bonita Springs, FL 34135
properties.blurb: A world of beer awaits at The Brass Tap Bonita Springs, where 150+ taps pour craft brews from around the globe. The vibe is equal parts laid-back and upscale, with knowledgeable staff ready to help you find your next favorite pour. Live music, daily specials, and monthly events keep things lively, and a full menu of delicious bites makes it easy to stay awhile. Need a pairing recommendation? Just ask—these beer experts have you covered.
properties.name: The Brass Tap (Bonita Springs)

properties.address: 825 Dulaney Valley Rd Space 1285, Towson, MD 21204, USA
properties.blurb: Over in the Towson, MD location, The Brass Tap is bringing the same brew expertise with a side of Towson charm. With more taps than you can count and a lineup that changes as fast as your plans, it's your go-to for the best craft beers from near and far. Of course, the food game is strong here. With burgers, wings, and all the deliciousness that pairs perfectly with a cold one. So, belly up at your new favorite spot for sporting events, good conversation, and incredible vibes.
properties.name: The Brass Tap (Towson)

properties.blurb: Get ready for great times and well-crafted beer at Brass Tap Verde! This place is a beer lover's dream, with an impressive mile-long list of brews sourced from local breweries. It's like a one-stop-shop for all your beer cravings, making it the perfect spot to start and end your night without missing a beat. But it's not just about the drinks here; Brass Tap Verde knows how to satisfy your hunger too. When you're in the mood for some delicious grub, you'll find yourself spoiled for choice. The Boom Boom Shrimp will tickle your taste buds, and the Old-World Pizzas are a definite crowd-pleaser. And hey, why not explore further? Tacos, burgers, baskets, wraps—they've got it all, and then some!
properties.name: The Brass Tap Verde

properties.blurb: Beer is all about building community. These fine Fernandina Beach folks not only have 16 taps with elite craft options on rotation at all times, and over 100 canned beer and wine options (available for on-site consumption or take away) — they also operate as a homebrew supply shop that services the robust DIY brewing scene in northeastern Florida and southeastern Georgia. Swing in and sample some exciting new local suds, talk shop with fellow brewheads, check out an informative demo, or maybe even sign up for a homebrewing class and learn the ins and outs of a whole new style.  BONUS: Pick up $50 or more in homebrew gear and score a free pint at the bar!
properties.name: The Brew Shed

properties.address: 1106 N Charles St, Baltimore, MD 21201, USA
properties.blurb: At the Brewer's Art, you might find yourself daydreaming about a home makeover inspired by their charming early 20th-century townhouse vibe. Sure, we can't actually move in (though we’d totally try to snag a spot in the cavernous basement or a window seat overlooking the bustling street). And don’t even get us started on the dining room. The food is so good, you might start thinking about how to remodel your own place to match the feel of this dreamhouse.
properties.name: The Brewer's Art

properties.address: 720 Park Ave, Lake Park, FL 33403, USA
properties.blurb: Brewhouse Gallery is the ultimate fusion of art, live music, and craft beer. With a rotating selection of limited-edition brews, you can count on finding a flavor profile that hits the spot. Their philosophy? Brew what they love, which just happens to be what we love too—lucky us! While they offer beers year-round, they leave room for creativity to brew something new at any moment. Pair that with different food options every night, and you've got yourself a craft beer paradise where art and beer meet.
properties.name: The Brewhouse Gallery

properties.address: 1443 Swift St, North Kansas City, MO 64116
properties.blurb: If you’re not a big drinker or you want a refreshing change of pace, hit up the Brewkery for a flight, pour, float, growler, or cocktail of kombucha. The Brewkery is a sustainable and environmentally conscious company that serves house-made paninis and soup — along with everything kombucha. New to 'buch? Not to worry, they'll ferment your interest in no time!
properties.name: The Brewkery

properties.address: 20 Hudson Yards Unit 207, New York, NY 10001, USA
properties.name: The Bronx Brewery & Hudson Yards Kitchen

properties.blurb: "This place is exactly what you want in a neighborhood haunt. This mother-son operation is frequented by regulars who know what they’ll find here — fantastic down-to-earth service, real conversation and a story on every stool. Look elsewhere for the latest, greatest Double Cascade Northeast Hazy Whatchamacallit. Just grab a cold beer, order a pizza and say hello to a new (old) favorite.   "
properties.name: The Bubbler

properties.blurb: No matter your liquid refreshment pleasure, this do-it-all café has you covered. Seeing as we’re in Wisconsin, let’s start with beer—20 rotating taps include Sconnie stalwarts New Glarus and Lakefront Brewery, as well as bold regional offerings like the Naked Threesome Juicy IPA (Raised Grain Brewing Company) and Kohl's Cash Millionaire Tropical Sour Ale with blackberry and pineapple (Young Blood Beer Company). You can also pick from over 200 craft beer bottles and cans, or from over 200 wines by the bottle—holy cow! Elite coffee options abound as well, including several spiked, boozy bean concoctions. Then there’s the food—mouth-watering, deli-style wraps, paninis, soups, salads, bagels and more will have you buzzing back around again and again.
properties.name: The Buzz

properties.blurb: The Casual Pint in Phoenix keeps it, well… casual. With over thirty taps and a relaxed vibe, it’s the kind of spot where exploring new beers feels as easy as catching up with an old friend. Local brews, rotating flavors, and a no-pressure atmosphere make this taproom perfect for anyone who loves good beer without the fuss.
properties.name: The Casual Pint

properties.blurb: Want a pint? Perhaps a flight? How about a crowler? Wait, no, make that a growler. Or, you know what, why not create your own six pack while you're at? If you haven't noticed, The Casual Pint likes beer just as much as we do. But since they're not ones to rest on their laurels, they also have a carefully curated wine list, as well as appetizers and entrées. We found their buffalo chicken flatbread to be especially good. And with a bountiful tap list of craft beer from breweries across the U.S., you're bound to find something that'll make your beer belly smile.
properties.name: The Casual Pint

properties.blurb: The Casual Pint is BYOF — Bring Your Own Family. You won’t find a more casual, family-friendly brewery in the Omaha area. It boasts an upscale ambience more akin to a coffee shop than a raucous bar scene. And it makes sense: owned and operated by a father and son, it’s been a family business from the beginning. Cheers to that.
properties.name: The Casual Pint

properties.address: 1095 W Queen Creek Rd # 8, Chandler, AZ 85248, USA
properties.blurb: Couldn’t settle on just one? Lucky for you, The Casual Pint in Chandler has thirty rotating taps ready to keep the choices flowing. From local gems to national standouts, this spot makes it easy to turn “maybe I’ll try one more” into your new favorite find. With a laid-back vibe and plenty of variety, you’ll always have a reason to come back for another round.
properties.name: The Casual Pint (Ocotillo)

properties.address: 4221 SE Orilla Rd suite a, West Des Moines, IA 50061
properties.blurb: Come for the chicken, including specialties like Broasted Chicken, Nashville Hot Chicken, and the Hot Chicken Sundae (yes, you read that right). Stay for the long list of domestic and craft brews. And if you’re the outdoorsy type who likes to work for your supper, the Chicken is located right off the Great Western bike trail, serving as the perfect mid-ride fuel stop.
properties.name: The Chicken

properties.address: 5906 Ashworth Rd, West Des Moines, IA 50266
properties.blurb: The Chicken Coop in Des Moines is a sports lover’s paradise, loaded with TVs so you never miss a game. The menu is stacked with wings, steaks, burgers, and ribs—perfect fuel for a night of cheering on your team. A long draft list keeps the drinks flowing, making it easy to settle in for the action. While bringing your own couch might be a stretch, you’ll still feel right at home with great food, cold beer, and the ultimate game-day atmosphere.
properties.name: The Chicken Coop

properties.blurb: Family owned and operated, Cider Farm is an unabashed leader of Wisconsin's cider and brandy industry. Unlike other names in the business however, Cider Farm organically grows English and French cider apples that, unlike eating apples, have tannins. These apples are only grown for alcoholic beverages. It's that level of detail that puts them amongst our favorites., their ciders and brandies will keep us coming back harvest after harvest.
properties.name: The Cider Farm

properties.blurb: You know what they say: location, location, location. And The Collins, in the heart of historic Old Market, has theirs on lock. The patio offers great people-watching while inside has plenty of TVs to catch your favorite game. Also on lock: the flatbread and burger options, bar games like darts and pool, and the craft beer menu of course!
properties.name: The Collins

properties.blurb: Live jazz—two words that make any night better. Every other Saturday, the smooth sounds of brass and bass set the mood while you sip on local brews or your go-to standby. A full menu rounds out the experience, offering everything from comfort food classics to bold new flavors. Whether you're here for the music, the drinks, or the perfect combination of both, it's a spot that turns an ordinary evening into something special.
properties.name: The Continental

properties.blurb: Here the copper tanks are always brewing up something new and exciting. With a constant rotation of fresh beers on tap, you'll never run out of options to tantalize your taste buds. And what goes better with a delicious brew than some mouthwatering pizza from The Village Pub and Bavarian pretzels from MKE Pretzel Co? It's the perfect combination to satisfy your cravings. If you're looking to soak up the sunshine, their patio is the place to be. And for those who prefer cocktails, fear not! The Copper Turtle has you covered with a delightful selection of crafted drinks.
properties.name: The Copper Turtle Brewery & Tavern

properties.address: 172 N College Ave Suite C, Fort Collins, CO 80524
properties.blurb: No need to hop a plane to NYC—this slice shop brings the big city’s best right to Fort Collins. Thin, foldable, and loaded with flavor, the pizza here is as authentic as it gets, with a touch of Colorado freshness in every bite. Grab a slice after a day on the slopes or order a whole pie to share. Pair it with a crisp local brew, add a salad or an app, and settle in for a meal that hits just right.
properties.name: The Corner Slice (Fort Collins)

properties.address: 635 Lincoln Ave N, Steamboat Springs, CO 80487
properties.blurb: No need to hop a plane to NYC—this slice shop brings the big city’s best right to Steamboat Springs. Thin, foldable, and loaded with flavor, the pizza here is as authentic as it gets, with a touch of Colorado freshness in every bite. Grab a slice after a day on the slopes or order a whole pie to share. Pair it with a crisp local brew, add a salad or an app, and settle in for a meal that hits just right.
properties.name: The Corner Slice (Steamboat Springs)

properties.blurb: Corner Taproom is run by the folks behind Textile Brewing. It brings the same vintage charm and beers as the original location, plus cocktails, hard ciders, and guest taps from other Iowa breweries too. Once you’ve settled on a drink, order pizza or wings from the restaurant next door. With an indoor fireplace for winter, and outdoor fire pits once the snow melts, Corner Taproom is a spot we’ll be visiting year-round.
properties.name: The Corner Taproom

properties.blurb: Courtyard Brewery has been perfecting the art of day drinking since 2014. What a coincidence. We’ve been practicing our day drinking for just as long. A match made in heaven. They’ve got IPAs. Sours. Saisons. Porters. Honestly, if you’ve heard of it, Courtyard Brewery is brewing it. This creative and inventive menu represents everything you love about New Orleans. It’s quirky. It’s unexpected. It’s what we’ve been training years for. Let’s go!
properties.name: The Courtyard Brewery

properties.blurb: Welcome to The Courtyard! This family-owned Lower Garden hotspot will keep you sated with suds from near (their own nano-brewery) and far (a great guest selection always). Let’s focus on the stuff they make, shall we? The hop game is on point here. We warmed up with a Baby IPA then graduated to the beautifully complex Illusory Body. Courtyard’s Lab Circle West Coast was also a highlight. Want to wander the map a bit? Ask them what guest lines they’re excited about. There’s always something new and the list is fire.
properties.name: The Courtyard Brewery

properties.blurb: Nestled into downtown Waukesha you’ll find The Craft Lounge with picnic table seating to accompany their long marble-topped bar. And while you can keep things simple with their impressive draft options (such as the Hazy Barbarian IPA from Three Floyds), house cocktails like the Kentucky Honey (bourbon, pamplemousse liqueur, honey and lemon) and build-your-own pizzas, The Craft Lounge leans haaaaard into all things “craft.” Painting, live jazz, dancing, trivia nights, karaoke. Damn, that’s a lot to offer from one bar. We half expect next time to walk into a sip-n-craft tutorial on crochet or glass blowing, and maybe leave with a lovely gift for mom.
properties.name: The Craft Lounge

properties.blurb: We couldn’t wait to check out this new spot taking over familiar Walker’s Point digs. You can expect an authentic and welcoming staff, thoughtful tap list and probably lots of laughter and warm conversation echoing throughout the place. Welcome Dark Horse to the neighborhood as they welcome you to one of your new favorite haunts.
properties.name: The Dark Horse
The Dean West Restaurant and Brewstillery

properties.blurb: Next door to First Ave and the Entry, The Depot is a great spot to grab a burger (try the Inside Out) and a couple pints before the show or game.
properties.name: The Depot

properties.address: 3701 NE Stinson Blvd, St Anthony, MN 55421, USA
properties.blurb: Hey, you've got to check out The Dive! It's the kind of place where everyone's welcome, and you'll love the relaxed vibe and the strong sense of community and respect they've got going on. But here's the best part – they've got something fun happening all the time, from karaoke nights and trivia challenges to meat raffles and bingo. And if you're ever in the mood for some games, they've got a game room to keep you entertained. So, if you're looking for a spot to unwind, hang out with friends, and enjoy good food and drinks without emptying your wallet, The Dive is where you want to be.
properties.name: The Dive Sports Bar and Grill

properties.address: 2736 Banks St, New Orleans, LA 70119, USA
properties.name: The Dog House

properties.address: 307 Chartres St, New Orleans, LA 70130, USA
properties.name: The Double Club

properties.blurb: Draft Horse is located in 'The Food Building' and that's entirely fitting when you get to know their menu. If you're in a rush, the grab & go deli features a varity of vendors from the building, but we suggest pulling up a chair and staying a while. Pair that free pint with fried bologna, a charcuterie plate or one of the tastiest takes on cheese curds we've run across.
properties.name: The Draft Horse

properties.blurb: Picture this….48 craft beer lines, all for $3. But wait! There’s more! Not only is the Drunk Uncle the premier craft beer bar in lovely West Allis, it’s also the only REAL professional wrestling bar in the world. If inexpensive craft beer and the last real sport in America isn’t enough, The Drunk Uncle is also home to the MOST UNIQUE vending machines in the GALAXY. All that combined with a fun and welcoming atmosphere is why The Drunk Uncle should be your first (or last) stop of day.
properties.name: The Drunk Uncle

properties.blurb: Swing by Dubliner Pub for some St. Paddy’s Day charm right here in Omaha. It’s nestled in the Old Market with an easy-to-miss basement entrance. If you didn’t know better, you might stroll right past it, never knowing the jaunty live music and cold Irish ales tucked inside. Good thing we’re telling you. Another pro tip? Dump a shot of Jameson in your Guinness. Really.
properties.name: The Dubliner Pub

properties.blurb: If you’ve got the hungries, The Duke is a great place to make a pig of yourself. Their Fried Mac & Cheese is to die for. Their Dragon’s Breath burger is guaranteed to light you on fire (hot tip: you’ll need a cold beer or two to cool down your chops). If either of those don’t tickle your fancy, give the Chili Cheese Dawg a go. Oh, and we’d be remiss if we didn’t recommend ordering the Fried Oreo for dessert. Salivating yet?
properties.name: The Duke

properties.address: 2850 N Brookfield Rd, Brookfield, WI 53045, USA
properties.blurb: The Elbow Room isn't just a name, it's a promise! This local bar and grill serves up amazing food in a modern, fun environment with plenty of space to stretch out on their huge patio. You'll have so much elbow room, you might even consider doing a cartwheel (we don’t recommend it after a few beers, though). The game will definitely be on, the beer will be ice-cold, and the menu is packed with all your favorite bar foods. It's the perfect spot to relax, chow down, and enjoy some good times.
properties.name: The Elbow Room

properties.blurb: Cruise out to Greendale and make a night of it at one of our favorite places to eat, drink and explore. You can’t go wrong with a Golden Ale and some wings. Or pair their dangerously tasty Imperial IPA with a Black Eye Burger. Ah, then there’s the Barleywine — a high gravity warmer worth drawing straws for who captains the land yacht home. Here for Sunday brunch? French Fried Toast or the Hangover Calzone.
properties.name: The Explorium (Greendale)

properties.address: 11200 W Burleigh St, Wauwatosa, WI 53222, USA
properties.name: The Explorium Brewpub (Wauwatosa)

properties.blurb: We don’t get too many pool halls during the week, and for our billiard fix, the Famous Door 2 had us covered. Weekly events like poker, trivia, karaoke spring up as well, but by and large, a game of 8 ball is the draw. No frills and stiff drinks, the Famous Door 2 doesn’t have a shred of pretension.
properties.name: The Famous Door 2

properties.blurb: Rainbows & Lollipops, Scorpio Rising, Underwater Panther. It’s not Willy Wonka’s factory but it might be the next best thing. Presenting Fermentorium Barrel House, where the only thing more creative than the names are the flavor combos of the beers they produce. With 24 taps, including exclusive barrel-brewed beers and ever-changing options, it’s like winning the golden ticket every time.
properties.name: The Fermentorium Barrel House

properties.blurb: Go big or go home could easily be the Fermentorium Brewery’s motto. It’s not just their tap room, with tons of seating plus an event space and pool tables. Their beers also go big on flavor and innovation. Juice Packets IPA is as mouthwatering as it sounds, and we haven’t even told you about the Coffee Sweater Weather yet. With 24 taps and new beers constantly in rotation, we guarantee you’ll keep coming back.
properties.name: The Fermentorium Brewery and Tasting Room

properties.blurb: We came for the breakfast and stayed for the beer. First up was Eggs Benny paired with a delicious Bloody. From there, we hopped to the beer menu and kicked things off with the No. 49 City Girl Cold Press Stout. Then, stuffed to the gills, we went for something lighter. The Freehouse 99 Low Cal Ale sounds dainty, but this little sipper drinks like the genuine article. Plus, now we won’t have to spend the afternoon working off our beer belly with donkey kicks and bicycle crunches.
properties.name: The Freehouse

properties.address: 1176 Edgewood Sve S Unit 4 Jacksonville, FL 32206
properties.blurb: Tucked into Jacksonville’s Murray Hill neighborhood, The FRIDGE is part craft beer hangout, part vinyl lover’s paradise, and all neighborhood vibe. With a rotating lineup of taps, a curated bottle shop, and crates of records waiting to be flipped through, it’s the kind of spot where good music and good beer naturally collide. Grab a pint, dig through some vinyl, and settle into a laid-back space that feels equal parts bar, record store, and community living room.
properties.name: The Fridge

properties.blurb: The Front Row considers itself the #1 Hawkeye Bar in Iowa (in all caps!), so it’s safe to say they’re not messing around. Pop in to watch the game, take advantage of the free popcorn, and — if it’s a weeknight – order a prime cut steak with a baked potato, garlic bread, and grilled mushrooms and onions or bleu cheese. They call it The Original Steak Night, and our mouths are officially watering.
properties.name: The Front Row

properties.blurb: If you're the kind of freak who doesn't like having fun, don’t even think about heading to the Game Lounge. Food. Beer. Hundreds of tabletop and traditional boards games. All sorts of awesome vinyl spinning on the ones and twos. Sadly, it'd be so much fun you'd be miserable. But in the event you're the kind of freak who does like having fun, then my friend, this is the place for you.
properties.name: The Game Lounge

properties.blurb: If you're the kind of freak who doesn't like having fun, don’t even think about heading to the Game Lounge. Food. Beer. Hundreds of tabletop and traditional boards games. All sorts of awesome vinyl spinning on the ones and twos. Sadly, it'd be so much fun you'd be miserable. But should you be the kind of freak who does like having fun, then my freaky friend, this is the freakin' place for you.
properties.name: The Game Lounge

properties.blurb: We could tell you to come watch the big tilt here but that's obvious. We could tell you they'll have cold craft beer and cocktails but that, too, seems like it sort of goes without saying. So what do you NEED to know about The Game? Two words: Overtime Burger. Trust us. Thanks us later. Etc. It's a jammer. More of a health nut? Fair enough. Two more words for you: Fried Oreos. We'll show ourselves out (and head to The Game).
properties.name: The Game Sports Pub

properties.address: 401 S Nevada Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80903
properties.name: The Garden

properties.address: 817 NE Rice Rd, Lee's Summit, MO 64086
properties.blurb: Prepare to witness the G.O.A.T. (Greatest Of All Time) in brewing at The Goat Brewing. This esteemed brewery pays homage to a local Mob organization that kept the alcohol flowing during Prohibition. Luckily, times have changed, and now they serve their exceptional brews legally. With a staggering lineup of 22 taps pouring their fresh-brewed beers on site, you'll be in hoppy heaven. But it's not just about the brews! This place knows how to satisfy your hunger too, offering a full menu of grilled-to-order paninis, hot dogs, quesadillas, and more. It's the perfect fuel to accompany your legendary drinking experience.You won't be "kidding" around when it comes to enjoying great beer and delicious bites in this amazing establishment.
properties.name: The Goat Brewing Co

properties.blurb: The Gold Pan Saloon has a rich history (pun intended). Being around since the Gold Rush days has left them plenty of time to perfect their menu. Today you’ll find a selection of well-crafted burgers, sandwiches, and specialty cocktails. They recently debuted Carboy Winery and now offer wine on tap too. No matter what you order, it’ll feel like you’ve struck gold.
properties.name: The Gold Pan Saloon

properties.blurb: The Green Room Public House feels like your local hangout even if you’ve never set foot in it before. Equal parts cocktail lounge and neighborhood bar, it nails that “pull up a stool and stay awhile” energy. Trivia nights bring the heat, the acoustics are begging for live music. And that rotating craft beer lineup? It has a sneaky way of turning a quick pint into “guess we’re closing this place down.”
properties.name: The Green Room Public House

properties.address: 116 W Ashland Ave, Indianola, IA 50125, USA
properties.blurb: Want to experience a bit of British charm without leaving Iowa? Head to The Groggy Dog, a cozy pub serving up a rotating selection of 12 craft beers and ciders, plus a menu full of hearty pub fare. You’ll find comforting classics like bangers and mash alongside American favorites, all in a warm, welcoming space that feels like your neighborhood hangout if your neighborhood happened to be across the pond.
properties.name: The Groggy Dog

properties.address: 10539 W Forest Home Ave, Hales Corners, WI 53130, USA
properties.blurb: The Hale House is where good vibes meet great pours, and yes, it’s absolutely “approved by awesome people” (that would be us). Their scratch kitchen serves up expertly grilled favorites, while the tap list is packed with craft beers, seasonal brews, and plenty of Wisconsin pride. Stop by on the weekend for breakfast that rivals your grandma’s fluffy pancakes, golden French toast, and a Bloody Mary that could start a cult following.
properties.name: The Hale House

properties.address: 1607 Sugar Grove Ave, Dallas Center, IA 50063
properties.blurb: Come to the Handlebar for the 32 craft beers on tap. Stay for the mouth-watering Handleburger, covered with smoked gouda and smothered with bacon onion jam (and nominated in the Iowa Beef City Council 2022’s Top 10 Best Burger Contest). But the Handlebar doesn’t just have an expansive beer menu and the best food on the Raccoon River bike trail: it also has concoctions like the Churro Leche, a decadent mix of Rumchata, Kahlua, cinnamon, susgar, milk, and chocolate.
properties.name: The HandleBar

properties.address: 1418 Dean St, Fort Myers, FL 33901, USA
properties.name: The HIDEAWAY Bar

properties.blurb: Tucked in alongside Hwy 25, the Hideaway has a feel of walking into a chalet with the help of a password.  A snazzy looking billiard area and TVs inside, but catch some rays out on their dog-friendly patio if weather permits. Burgers, burritos, and fajitas are just a few of the menu options and the Breckenridge Avalanche and Bootstrap IPA were recent champs from their draft list. Pick the right night and karaoke might be in the cards too at this Castle Rock gem.
properties.name: The Hideaway Bar & Grill

properties.blurb: Local draft varieties on tap and classic Americana bar food on their menu, the Hideaway Lounge is nestled into a mini strip mall just off the road. It’s the place where you meet an old friend, slide up to the bar and catch up from the last five years. Or where you settle that five year feud over a game of pool. Friendly staff quick with a smile and TVs with the game on if you’re just needing to chill
properties.name: The Hideaway Lounge

properties.blurb: Locally owned and operated since 2018 The Hills Bar and Grill have been bringing New American, Spanish, Mexican, AND Italian meals to the Pleasant Hills and surrounding areas. They are here to give us all new, fresh and homemade cuisine. No matter your taste you will be able to find something on this expansive menu. From delicious small plates to burgers, Italian specials to their Mexican specials you honestly can’t choose wrong. To top it off they have brunch! Might we suggest the Enfrijoladas. Three traditional Mexican Brunch enchiladas with scrambled eggs, country potatoes and chorizo. Topped with bean sauce, sour cream, queso fresco and avocado. Just trust us on this one.
properties.name: The Hills Bar and Grill

properties.address: W2463 County Rd ES, East Troy, WI 53120
properties.blurb: "If you’ve ever wondered what the exact mid-point between a wine bar, a microbrewery and a European pub was, the answer is The Hive Taproom. This dog and family-friendly taproom has 14 session meads on tap, honey kombucha, and house-made soda elixir. The mead is gluten-free and retains the medicinal properties of raw honey through careful processing. Plus, the biggest bonus: many people report feeling no hangover the next day! "
properties.name: The Hive Taproom

properties.address: 3575 S Yosemite St, Denver, CO 80237, USA
properties.name: The Hound Sports Pub + Burger

properties.blurb: ** Deal good for BOGO draft wine **
properties.name: The Infinite Monkey Theorem

properties.blurb: Never thought we’d have a milkshake beer of our dreams, but we found one here at The Iowa Project. The CranCherry Cremeshake, a milkshake style IPA, hits all the right notes. This gem of a brewery mixes up classics with inventive new brews to sport a lineup that is unique and offers something for everyone. The flagship Cabo San Lager is an absolute perfect beer to enjoy on a sunny patio. Take a trip to this northwest Iowa spot and you too can have milkshake beer dreams.
properties.name: The Iowa Project Brewing Co

properties.address: 9246 4th St N, St. Petersburg, FL 33702, USA
properties.name: The Irish Pub on 4th

properties.address: 24 W Camelback Rd Suite D & E, Phoenix, AZ 85013, USA
properties.blurb: The Joy Lush Club, or JLC for those *in the know*, is your future favorite neighborhood bar in Uptown Phoenix. With 16 killer beers on tap, a top-tier wine selection, and to-go drinks for when you just can’t stay away, JLC’s got your back. Come for happy hour, stay for the game, the live music, or just to say “JLC” like you’ve been coming here forever. Shuffleboard? Food trucks? Local art? Yeah, they’ve got that too. Stick around long enough, and you'll be one of the insiders dropping the JLC nickname like a pro.
properties.name: The Joy Lush Club

properties.blurb: With a name like Keg King, it’s not too hard to guess what this South Florida business’ niche is. But they don’t just offer kegs: they also deliver beer cases, ice, soda, wine, energy drinks, and other party supplies. Not a beer person? Not sure how you got here, but you can also buy a kombucha keg, wine keg, or a craft cocktail keg tailored to your personal preferences.
properties.name: The Keg King

properties.address: 9905 Gulf Coast Main St, Fort Myers, FL 33913
properties.blurb: Forget the Florida Keys—this spot is all about piano keys. Welcome to The Keys Bar and Grille, where dueling pianos face off every Friday and Saturday night. It’s a perfect place to bring a group for an unforgettable evening of entertainment while you chow down on fish tacos or coconut shrimp. Browse the beer menu or go for a frozen piña colada for major beach vibes.
properties.name: The Keys Bar and Grille

properties.address: 767 N Eustis St Suite 115 St. Paul, MN 55114
properties.blurb: The Lab is doing some truly unique stuff that allows breweries to test recipes by piloting them on a small system. You see, great beers don’t just make themselves. There’s a good deal of trial and error involved. Well, consider us very willing Guinea Pigs. Because you can pour just as much as you’d like from their self-serve wall of taps, there’s a chance to cruise around the list until you find something you need to “research a bit further”. Get after it, Einstein. You might just unearth the next big thing.
properties.name: The Lab

properties.blurb: Whether you stop in before a game, after a concert or just to enjoy a few drinks on their phenomenal rooftop patio, The Liffey is aways a great choice.
properties.name: The Liffey

properties.address: W191S6409 S Hillendale Dr, Muskego, WI 53150, USA
properties.blurb: The Local is exactly what its name promises. A cozy, come-as-you-are spot that feels like home the second you walk in. Ice-cold craft beer flows alongside a menu stacked with all the Wisconsin hits, from a classic Friday fish fry to wings, pizzas, and hearty sandwiches. Settle in for a laid-back meal or grab a pint with friends. This is the kind of place where good food, cold beer, and easy vibes makes you feel like a local with every visit.
properties.name: The Local

properties.address: 1050 Cypress Village Blvd, Ruskin, FL 33573, USA
properties.name: The Local Brew Co

properties.blurb: ** Deal also good on glasses of wine and ciders! **
properties.name: The Local Drive

properties.blurb: Unique-but-approachable beers. That’s the mission at Lone Girl and they’re crushing it. Start with a SpeaKEasy Light or a Red Banks Amber before exploring something a bit more boisterous like Quadnado — a Belgian Quad that tips the scales north of 10% ABV but drinks like a dream. Did we mention there’s food, too? Great food. Curds and Crab Cakes and Meatballs and those are just appetizers! Wait, signature cocktails, too?! Just get here already…
properties.name: The Lone Girl Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Unique-but-approachable beers. That’s the mission at Lone Girl and they’re crushing it. Start with a SpeaKEasy Light or a Red Banks Amber before exploring something a bit more boisterous like Quadnado — a Belgian Quad that tips the scales north of 10% ABV but drinks like a dream. Did we mention there’s food, too? Great food. Curds and Crab Cakes and Meatballs and those are just appetizers! Wait, signature cocktails, too?! Just get here already…
properties.name: The Lone Girl Brewing Co

properties.blurb: Unique-but-approachable beers. That’s the mission at Lone Girl and they’re crushing it. Start with a SpeaKEasy Light or a Red Banks Amber before exploring something a bit more boisterous like Quadnado — a Belgian Quad that tips the scales north of 10% ABV but drinks like a dream. Did we mention there’s food, too? Great food. Curds and Crab Cakes and Meatballs and those are just appetizers! Wait, signature cocktails, too?! Just get here already…
properties.name: The Lone Girl Brewing Company

properties.blurb: Remember the first time you set foot in the original Boone & Crockett and thought, “Where has this place been all my life?” Get ready to think that again. Or say it out loud. Hell, shout it! Because Lost Whale is the truth. The cocktails are so well-presented you almost don’t want to disturb them, yet so delicious you want to drink them all right now. Relax. Take your time. This won’t be your last visit. Especially once you try the Harvey Wallbanger.
properties.name: The Lost Whale

properties.blurb: If you think drinking and axe throwing is dangerous, you’re not Minnesotan enough. After all, this is the home of Paul Bunyan for cryin’ out loud. We’re born to be plaid. We’re plaid to the bone. We prefer plaid jokes over dad jokes, which—apparently—appear to be one and the same. That said, if throwing a tool fashioned for felling trees at a target isn’t your cup of tea, a pint of Surly Axe Man surely is. Especially when you pair it with some spicy poke.
properties.name: The LumberJack

properties.blurb: “Madison’s Craft Beer Oasis” is every bit of its nickname. If you’ve lived here a while, you’ve undoubtedly bellied up to their Civil War-era bar and tipped a few at this O.G. beer destination. It’s routinely named among the best craft bars in the country but the story doesn’t end there. Their Whisk(e)y game is strong. The wines are on point. And if you love Absinthe? You’ll be hooked. With all this and live music on the regular? It’s no wonder The Malt House is as much a part of this town as Bucky himself.
properties.name: The Malt House

properties.blurb: This ain’t your average Iowa burger joint. With a menu inspired by international flavors, The Map Room is testing the taste buds of the locals in Cedar Rapids. But you and your adventurous palette will know the perfect IPA to pair with Montreal Fries and a Maui Burger. Or maybe it’s a craft cocktail with Cusco Fries and a Saigon Burger. The point is, adventure is the name of the game at The Map Room and the fun, cozy vibe makes it feel like you’re not in Iowa anymore.
properties.name: The Map Room

properties.blurb: Welcome to The Mucky Duck, where the beers flow and the good times roll! With an ever-changing lineup of beers on tap, this lively establishment is a beer lover's paradise. From local favorites to unique and strong brews, there's always something exciting to try. As the only British Pub in Ames, it's only fitting to indulge in their famous fish and chips, but don't be afraid to venture out and savor the flavors of bangers and mash for a delightful twist. So, gather your friends, raise a pint, and immerse yourself in the warm and inviting atmosphere of The Mucky Duck.
properties.name: The Mucky Duck Pub

properties.address: 9541 W Cleveland Ave, West Allis, WI 53227, USA
properties.blurb: Step into The Network Bar, where the possibilities for entertainment are boundless. This vibrant venue offers an array of enticing drink specials, a lively jukebox, and four large-screen TVs that broadcast all the latest action. With four top-quality pool tables, gaming machines, and a bustling crowd, there's never a shortage of things to do at The Network Bar. On Monday nights, pool enthusiasts can test their skills in the open 8-ball tournaments, vying for the coveted championship title. Whether you're seeking a thrilling game of pool, immersive gaming experiences, or simply a place to unwind with friends, The Network Bar has it all. Join the vibrant atmosphere, savor the excitement, and indulge in an unforgettable night of entertainment.
properties.name: The Network Bar

properties.blurb: One of the best breweries in Wisconsin now has an outpost on East Wash in Madison! Meet the O’so Madhouse. Finally, you can get your favorites from O’so right here in town. Not to mention new stuff and limited release beers. We’re stoked they’re here! Let’s celebrate with an Infectious Groove or three (hey, it’s a Berliner, don’t judge!). Once you’ve finished your session, you can also grab some suds to go. Looks like this O’so party is just getting started…
properties.name: The O'so Madhouse

properties.blurb: This is the neighborhood spot you and your pals keep going to, even though you swear you’re going to try somewhere new, but that sounds exhausting, and then you all agree that Olde Towne Tavern has amazing wings and chili cheese fries and really cold beer so you might as well go with what you know because it’s so great so why bother with anywhere else.
properties.name: The Olde Towne Tavern

properties.blurb: Make plans to make it an evening here. Why? Because the beer is only the beginning. Creative cocktails with unexpected ingredients (like breakfast cereal) will set the mood as you work your way around an always-changing menu with standouts like Nashville Hot Tofu and a breakfast burger so good it made us want to return for The Original’s much ballyhooed brunch.
properties.name: The Original

properties.address: 265 50th St, West Des Moines, IA 50265, USA
properties.name: The Pelican Post Bar & Grille

properties.blurb: The main event at Perch Brewery is not its impressive lineup of craft cocktails. It’s not the full bar or the live music on the rooftop or the stunning views of downtown Chandler. It’s not the refreshing beers brewed on site. It’s not the solid sammie and pizza lineup on the menu. No, folks. The main event at Perch is the collection of 50 brightly colored tropical rescue birds. We absolutely dig the vibe here and know you will, too.
properties.name: The Perch Brewery

properties.address: 720 W Ray Rd, Gilbert, AZ 85233, USA
properties.name: The Perch Brewery  (Gilbert)

properties.address: 923 72nd St N, St. Petersburg, FL 33710, USA
properties.blurb: Nestled in St. Petersburg's western corner, the Pesky Pelican Brew Pub is a hidden gem that captures the essence of "old school Florida." This family and bike-friendly haven invites you to indulge in unique St. Louis-style dishes paired perfectly with their house-brewed beers and local craft selections. From their small-batch brews to their exclusive "Taste of St. Louis" menu featuring delights like Provel Toasted Ravioli and butter cake, this spot is a culinary adventure. Step into a cozy, casual atmosphere that welcomes both neighbors and travelers, promising good times and unforgettable flavors. Come join the community at Pesky Pelican Brew Pub!
properties.name: The Pesky Pelican Brew Pub

properties.address: 200 N Tampa St g118, Tampa, FL 33602, USA
properties.blurb: The Pint and Brew is your go-to for local craft brews, serving up some of the best pints in Tampa Bay. With an ever-rotating tap list, you can sample top-notch beers from around the area, all under one cozy roof. And while you're here, don't miss their upscale pub fare—comfort food classics with a delicious twist that pair perfectly with your favorite brew. It’s the perfect spot to kick back, sip, and savor the best of what Tampa’s craft beer scene has to offer.
properties.name: The Pint & Brew

properties.blurb: Wait. You didn’t know CO-style fried chicken was a thing? Buddy. We suggest you get yourself to The Post and remedy that right quick. With beers brewed to perfectly complement those crispy, juicy birds, your options for ultimate enjoyment are pretty much endless. Try the Townie IPA or the Top Rope with your fried chicken, and don’t skimp on the sides either. Trust us.
properties.name: The Post Chicken & Beer  Brewery (Boulder)

properties.blurb: The Post has found the secret: fried chicken and microbrews. We salute this union and may it continue to for centuries. Not just run of the mill fried chicken either, but seriously crispy tender goodness the Colonel himself would be jealous of. At their Roseland location, there’s outdoor seating or grab a booth and the bartender will set you up with the Townie, a spritely and crisp IPA, as well as a rich and toasty Top Rope Mexican Lager.
properties.name: The Post Chicken & Beer Brewery

properties.address: 333 E Wonderview Ave, Estes Park, CO 80517
properties.blurb: Even though it’s located inside a building of the Stanley Hotel, which famously inspired Stephen King’s The Shining, we promise there’s nothing scary about The Post. Except how scary-good the fried chicken and beer is. Beers like the Townie IPA are brewed to perfectly complement their crispy birds, so your options for ultimate enjoyment are pretty much endless. Did we mention the fried chicken is somehow gluten free on top of being delicious? Maybe there’s some black magic in that recipe after all.
properties.name: The Post Chicken & Beer Brewery (Estes Park)

properties.address: 1002 S College Ave, Fort Collins, CO 80524
properties.blurb: Long ago this Fort Collins spot was an IHOP, but lucky for all of us, The Post has taken over, creating a restaurant with rustic vibes where you can enjoy the best fried chicken around. Did we mention it’s also gluten free? After working their magic on the fried chicken recipe, they went ahead and brewed a lineup of beers as a perfect match. As they like to say, hot chicken loves cold beer. As we like to say, PubPass loves The Post.
properties.name: The Post Chicken & Beer Brewery (Fort Collins)

properties.blurb: Welcome to the OG spot. The Post Chicken & Beer in Lafayette is where the dream of the crispiest, juiciest fried chicken around was born. This location has a great outdoor patio, so you can catch some rays while you chow down on a ten-piece plate. Wash it all down with a Backyard Saison or a Howdy Beer and then do it again tomorrow. Because life is short and best enjoyed with beer.
properties.name: The Post Chicken & Beer Brewery (Lafayette)

properties.blurb: Legally we can’t tell you that The Post’s fried chicken is finger-lickin’ good. But we’re pretty sure we can say it’s better than that. Don’t take our word for it though! Guy Fieri featured this location on Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives, and we haven’t even started raving about the beers yet. Brewed to perfectly complement their crispy, juicy birds, options like the Townie IPA and Top Rope hit the spot every time.
properties.name: The Post Chicken & Beer Brewery (Longmont)

properties.blurb: Step inside this small batch brewery in Belmond, Iowa for live music, lemon shandy, and unique craft beers. The rotating roster has everything from your classic stouts and IPAs to more adventurous beers with names like Jalapeno Happiness or Pineapple Pale Ale. Not sure what to get? Get a selection with a refreshing beer flight.
properties.name: The Pressroom Brewery

properties.address: 9101 International Dr Suite 1003, Orlando, FL 32819, USA
properties.blurb: Why hop across the pond when The Pub brings all the British charm right to your city? No need to pack or deal with passport drama when you can enjoy a true Brit experience. Complete with brilliant food, top-notch drinks, and warm hospitality that’ll make you feel like a local. Known for their award-winning Fish & Chips, along with an impressive selection of beer, spirits, and signature cocktails, The Pub gives you all the UK vibes without the hassle of a hours on a stuffy airplane.
properties.name: The Pub

properties.address: 3104 N Nevada Ave, Colorado Springs, CO 80907
properties.blurb: An excellent draft list goes a long way in our books. The Public House @ The Alexander has that on lock, but they’ve also impressed us with their other offerings.
